    end

    subgraph Bridge["전송 계층 (JSON-RPC)"]
        GC["GatewayClient.ts<br/>(child spawn / WS attach)"]
        WS["tui_gateway/ws.py<br/>(WSTransport)"]
        STDIO["tui_gateway/entry.py<br/>(StdioTransport)"]
    end

    subgraph Back["백엔드 (두뇌)"]
        SRV["tui_gateway/server.py<br/>(_methods 레지스트리, dispatch)"]
        SW["_SlashWorker<br/>(영속 HermesCLI 서브프로세스)"]
        Agent["AIAgent.run_conversation()"]
        DB["SessionDB (state.db)"]
    end

    Ink --> GC
    Remote --> WS
    GC -->|spawn -m tui_gateway.entry| STDIO
    GC -.->|HERMES_TUI_GATEWAY_URL| WS
    STDIO --> SRV
    WS --> SRV
    SRV --> SW
    SRV --> Agent
    Agent --> DB
    Agent -->|event 프레임| SRV
    SRV -->|event| GC & WS

    CLIrepl -->|in-process| Agent

Main analysis

Hermes Agent TUI/Input Surface

Analysis Snapshot: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/160_hermes-agent/ (TypeScript + gateway sources).

This document narrows the analysis of the operating principles of [[00_hermes_overview]] to the TUI/CLI rendering/input/streaming/slash UX dimension and analyzes it based on the source. The target of analysis is the actual repository /Users/jun/developer/codex/hermes-agent, and all structural claims are based on file paths (or path:line). Unlike the gold reference ([[01_cc_tui]]), Hermes is the original source, not an obfuscated bundle, so the identifier is read as is. The analysis version standard is 0.15.1 of acp_registry/agent.json.

If Claude Code's TUI is a "custom Ink fork in a single 622K line bundle," Hermes' core TUI is a two-process architecture with its own forked Ink renderer (@hermes/ink) in a separate Node/TypeScript workspace (ui-tui/), and the screen (TS) and brain (Python) separated by a newline-delimited JSON-RPC pipe. In other words, two independent input surfaces coexist in Hermes.

  1. Ink TUI (ui-tui/ + tui_gateway/) — hermes --tui / HERMES_TUI=1. React+Ink draws the terminal, and Python tui_gateway owns the session, tools, model, and most command logic (ui-tui/README.md:1-3).
  2. Classic interactive CLI (HermesCLI in cli.py) — The default hermes. prompt_toolkit fixed input area + rich markdown + background spinner thread.

Additionally, there is a gateway surface (tui_gateway/ws.py) that **re-exposes] the same tui_gateway handler as a WebSocket, allowing iOS/web clients to attach to the same RPC. This is the argument of this document: “Hermes has its own Ink TUI + gateway surface”.

graph TD
    subgraph Front["프런트엔드 (화면)"]
        Ink["ui-tui (React + @hermes/ink 포크)<br/>entry.tsx → App → AppLayout"]
        CLIrepl["cli.py HermesCLI<br/>(prompt_toolkit + rich)"]
        Remote["원격 클라이언트<br/>(iOS / 웹, WebSocket)"]
    end

    subgraph Bridge["전송 계층 (JSON-RPC)"]
        GC["GatewayClient.ts<br/>(child spawn / WS attach)"]
        WS["tui_gateway/ws.py<br/>(WSTransport)"]
        STDIO["tui_gateway/entry.py<br/>(StdioTransport)"]
    end

    subgraph Back["백엔드 (두뇌)"]
        SRV["tui_gateway/server.py<br/>(_methods 레지스트리, dispatch)"]
        SW["_SlashWorker<br/>(영속 HermesCLI 서브프로세스)"]
        Agent["AIAgent.run_conversation()"]
        DB["SessionDB (state.db)"]
    end

    Ink --> GC
    Remote --> WS
    GC -->|spawn -m tui_gateway.entry| STDIO
    GC -.->|HERMES_TUI_GATEWAY_URL| WS
    STDIO --> SRV
    WS --> SRV
    SRV --> SW
    SRV --> Agent
    Agent --> DB
    Agent -->|event 프레임| SRV
    SRV -->|event| GC & WS

    CLIrepl -->|in-process| Agent

1. Separation of the two input surfaces

1.1 Entry points and TTY gates

The client entry point for Ink TUI is ui-tui/src/entry.tsx. Shebang (#!/usr/bin/env -S node --max-old-space-size=8192 --expose-gc, entry.tsx:1) is a heavy Node app that requires 8GB heap + GC exposure. Sequence of action:

  1. Import forceTruecolor.js first — nudge truecolor before initializing chalk/supports-color (entry.tsx:3-4).
  2. Immediately process.exit(0) (entry.tsx:16-19) unless process.stdin.isTTY — does not spew out broken ANSI in a pipe/redirect environment.
  3. Clean the mouse/focus/paste DEC mode left by the previous crash with resetTerminalModes() (entry.tsx:23).
  4. If not Termux, start with \x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[3J (clear all + remove scrollback); In Termux, only newlines are used to preserve the previous output (entry.tsx:28-32).
  5. Create GatewayClient·start() → Then, mount the dynamically imported App with ink.render (entry.tsx:34-104).

ink.render is not a standard ink, but a @hermes/ink fork (entry.tsx:94, exitOnCtrlC: false + onHyperlinkClick hook — when mouse tracking is enabled, click events are intercepted before the terminal's own URL detection, so link clicks are opened directly in an external browser, entry.tsx:99-103).

1.2 Memory Monitoring — Longevity TUI’s self-defense

Ink TUI can live as long as a gateway daemon, so startMemoryMonitor monitors the heap, leaves a heap dump when critical, and then commits suicide with process.exit(137) (entry.tsx:60-69). setupGracefulExit ensures resetTerminalModes() + gateway child kill on exit (entry.tsx:41-58). This is a defense layer that is not present in [[01_cc_tui]] and is a by-product of the two-process structure.

2. @hermes/ink — Own Ink fork

Hermes forks Ink as an independent package in ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/ and uses it as a local dependency ("@hermes/ink": "file:./packages/hermes-ink", ui-tui/package.json). Assumes React 19.2 + react-compiler (the c as _c memo slot of react/compiler-runtime is visible in the build output, AlternateScreen.tsx:2,52). The standard ink ^6.8.0 is also in the dependencies, but the core render path is handled by the fork.

2.1 Surface exported by fork

ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/entry-exports.ts is the public API. There are a number of custom components/hooks that are not in the standard ink:

Category export Evidence
component AlternateScreen, Box, Text, Spacer, Link, Newline, ScrollBox, NoSelect, RawAnsi, Ansi entry-exports.ts:3-13
Enter render render, renderSync, createRoot, forceRedraw entry-exports.ts:25
input/focus hook useInput, useStdin, useTerminalFocus, useTerminalViewport, useTabStatus, useSelection/useHasSelection, useTerminalTitle entry-exports.ts:17-23
Cursor Hook useCursorAdvance, useDeclaredCursor entry-exports.ts:14-15
external process useExternalProcess, withInkSuspended (Ink pauses when launching $EDITOR) entry-exports.ts:16
Measuring/Wrapping measureElement, stringWidth, wrapAnsi entry-exports.ts:24,27-28
Cache Management evictInkCaches, scrollFastPathStats entry-exports.ts:4,26
External library re-exposure TextInput/UncontrolledTextInput (ink-text-input) entry-exports.ts:31

useExternalProcess/withInkSuspended and evictInkCaches (forcibly evicting caches when memory is stressed) are extensions for 2-process+long-lived TUI operation that do not exist in the custom fork of [[01_cc_tui]].

2.2 Layout Engine — Yoga (same lineage as Claude Code)

The fork has its own native-ts/yoga-layout/(enums.ts, index.ts), and ink/layout/yoga.ts maps React Box props to Yoga node properties. The structure in which EDGE_MAP (yoga.ts:32-) converts left/right/top/bottom/horizontal/vertical/all to Yoga Edge is isomorphic to Claude Code's applyYogaProps. In other words, the big picture of both harnesses is the same: flexbox (Yoga)-based declarative terminal layout.

2.3 Double Buffer + Blit Fastpass

The renderer (ink/renderer.ts) receives the frontFrame/backFrame double buffer and outputs a diff (renderer.ts:17-29). The key optimization is the prevFrameContaminated flag: if the selection overlay has post-transformed the screen buffer, or if the buffer has been reset by an alt-screen entry/resize/forceRedraw, disable blit (copy unchanged cells from previous screen) as it copies stale cells, otherwise ride the O(changes) blit fastpass (renderer.ts:110-124). Output is reused between frames to maintain a tokenize+grapheme clustering cache (charCache) (renderer.ts:33-37).

2.4 Alternate Screen + Mouse Tracking

The AlternateScreen component is the core of full screen (components/AlternateScreen.tsx). When mounting, use ENTER_ALT_SCREEN + ERASE_SCROLLBACK + ERASE_SCREEN + CURSOR_HOME + DISABLE_MOUSE_TRACKING + enableMouse at once to enter the correct state (AlternateScreen.tsx:74-83), and wrap the child with <Box height={rows} flexShrink={0}> to tie it to the viewport height. Turn off scrollback (AlternateScreen.tsx:48-49 docstring, :113-117). When unmounting, unconditionally use DISABLE_MOUSE_TRACKING + EXIT_ALT_SCREEN to prevent a crash from leaking DEC mode to the host shell (AlternateScreen.tsx:90-98).

Mouse tracking is a 4-preset machine: off | wheel | buttons | all. wheel (DEC 1000+1006) is a subset that preserves scrolling while avoiding hover causing "No image in clipboard" spam in tmux (AlternateScreen.tsx:17-24 Props docstring). Both ends agree on the Python side by using the same alias table (tui_gateway/server.py:853-896 _MOUSE_TRACKING_ALIASES).

2.5 Inline mode (Termux)

There is INLINE_MODE for environments where full screen cannot be used. In appLayout.tsx const Shell = INLINE_MODE ? Fragment: Split into AlternateScreen(appLayout.tsx:393), which preserves host terminal scrollback if inline. INLINE_MODE can be explicitly overridden with HERMES_TUI_INLINE, and the default follows Termux detection values ​​(ui-tui/src/config/env.ts:52-60).

3. Transport Layer — JSON-RPC Bridge

3.1 GatewayClient (TS) — child spawn or WS attach

GatewayClient (inheriting EventEmitter) in ui-tui/src/gatewayClient.ts is the only backend window in the front. Supports two transmission modes:

  • Spawn mode (default): Spawns python -m tui_gateway.entry as a child (gatewayClient.ts:337). The interpreter interpretation order is HERMES_PYTHON → PYTHON → $VIRTUAL_ENV/bin/python → ./.venv → ./venv → python3 (gatewayClient.ts:44-63). stdout is parsed line by line with readline, broken lines are isolated with gateway.protocol_error events (:341-350), and stderr is pumped with gateway.stderr events (:352-362) — Neither write directly to the terminal (to prevent JSON-RPC pipe pollution).
  • Attach mode: If HERMES_TUI_GATEWAY_URL exists, it attaches to the existing gateway via WebSocket (gatewayClient.ts:404-498). If HERMES_TUI_SIDECAR_URL is present, all event frames will be mirrored to the dashboard sidebar (:261-304).

Robustness code is heavy: ignore stale child/socket with identity gate (:364-401, :471-487), attach 20 lines of stderr tail to gateway.start_timeout payload on startup timeout to distinguish between “bad python / missing dependencies / config parsing failure” at a glance (:227-246), bearer token in URL log redact(:102-122). The default RPC timeout is 120s (:15).

3.2 tui_gateway/server.py (Python) — Handler registry

The server self-registers handlers in the _methods dict with the @method("name") decorator (server.py:438-443), and dispatch() routes them (server.py:477-516). Design details:

  • stdout protection: keep real stdout in _real_stdout and redirect sys.stdout = sys.stderr (server.py:175-176) — a stray print() in a library/tool becomes a harmless gateway.stderr without breaking the JSON protocol.
  • Separate the slow handler pool: slash.exec, cli.exec, shell.exec, session.resume/branch/compress, skills.manage, browser.manage block for a few seconds to several minutes, so send them to a small threadpool, and process the rest inline on the main thread — while approval.respond/session.interrupt starves on stdin (server.py:138-170, :497-513).
  • Transport polymorphism: If write_json is an event frame, it routes to the transport bound to its session_id, otherwise it is bound to contextvar, otherwise it routes to module stdio (server.py:365-383) — The basis for the same handler to transparently support stdio/WS.
  • Panic Logger: Writes unhandled exceptions/thread exceptions to ~/.hermes/logs/tui_gateway_crash.log and flows a one-line summary to stderr and floats it on TUI Activity (server.py:49-107).

3.3 _SlashWorker — persistent HermesCLI subprocess

Some slash commands are not processed directly by the gateway, but are delegated to a persistent tui_gateway.slash_worker subprocess (internally HermesCLI) launched per session (server.py:184-265). run(command) matches the request/response with the seq id and sets a timeout (HERMES_TUI_SLASH_TIMEOUT_S, default 45s). Restart the worker when switching models (server.py:1077-1090 _restart_slash_worker). As a result, the command logic already implemented by Python CLI is not redundantly implemented in TUI.

3.4 WebSocket Surface — Reuse the same handler

tui_gateway/ws.py reuses tui_gateway.server.dispatch verbatim (module docstring ws.py:1-21). Therefore, all RPC method/slash/approval/clarify/sudo flow/agent events ride the same handler, whether the client is Ink-over-stdio or iOS/Web-over-WebSocket. The wire format is also the same (newline-delimited JSON-RPC). WSTransport.write marshals to a loop with run_coroutine_threadsafe in a full worker thread, but detects a deadlock and fire-and-forgets when called from the loop thread itself (ws.py:51-). This is the core of the "gateway surface" level, a remoteization of the TUI protocol, as opposed to the multi-channel gateway (messaging) of [[00_hermes_overview]].

4. Screen composition — App tree

The top-level App is thin (ui-tui/src/app.tsx:9-25): useMainApp(gw) collects all the state, injects the context of the gateway with GatewayProvider and passes actions/composer/progress/status/transcript to AppLayout. Heavy logic is broken down into src/app/* hooks (createGatewayEventHandler.ts, createSlashHandler.ts, useComposerState.ts, useInputHandlers.ts, turnController.ts, etc., ui-tui/README.md:71-80).

4.1 Transcript — Virtual Scroll

AppLayout draws the transcript as a ScrollBox but renders it as a virtual history (virtual list) (appLayout.tsx:90-148): virtualHistory.topSpacer/bottomSpacer Let Box occupy only the height, and only virtualRows.slice(start, end) does the actual work. render(appLayout.tsx:103-137). Each row measures its height with measureRef to create an offset cache. This is its own virtual list implementation, different from [[01_cc_tui]]'s Static component (fixed output of rendered lines) strategy (it is also heavily tested: useVirtualHistory*, virtualHistoryClamp/OffsetCache, etc.).

On the right, there is a hand-implemented TranscriptScrollbar that draws a thumb that can be jumped by dragging the mouse (appChrome.tsx:451-522). StickyPromptTracker tracks user prompts across the top of the current viewport and displays them as sticky headers (appChrome.tsx:442-449).

4.2 Status Bar (StatusRule)

The bottom/top status line is StatusRule in appChrome.tsx:294-424. In the left area (truncate), the following are displayed sequentially separated by │:

segment Display contents Evidence
status/spinner FaceTicker (rotating face+verb+lapse) if busy, static status if idle appChrome.tsx:346-352
model shortModelLabel (remove the claude-/anthropic- prefix) + effort + fast :353-356,256-267
Context used/max token + [████░░] bar + % (50/80/95% color level) :316-368,126-155
session time SessionDuration(1s ticks) :369-374
Compression Count cmp N (5/10 critical color) :375-384
Subagent HUD SpawnHud: depth d/, concurrency ⚡/, paused ⏸, cap ⚠ :174-233,385
voice voiceLabel(●/◉ state) :386-396
number of live sessions / bg / cost N sessions (clickable), N bg, $cost :330-409

There is a cwd label (truncate-end) on the right, and statusRuleWidths distributes left/right/separator widths based on the number of columns (appChrome.tsx:157-172). Toggle off/top/bottom with /statusbar (/sb) (server.py:842-851 _STATUSBAR_MODES).

4.3 Indicator Styles — 4 Spinners

The busy spinner is ascii | emoji | kaomoji | unicode 4 styles (appChrome.tsx:47-76, Python verification table server.py:653-654, default kaomoji). kaomoji is FACES rotation (2.5s), emoji is ['⚕ ','🌀','🤔','✨','🍵','🔮'], ascii is |/-\ (100ms), and unicode is a braille spinner (verb hiding). Use the unicode-animations package (appChrome.tsx:4).

5. Input processing

5.1 Own line editor (textInput.tsx)

The main chat input is not ink-text-input but self-implemented line editor components/textInput.tsx (line 1340). ink-text-input is only used in simple cases such as clarify free-text/masking prompt (ui-tui/README.md:158). Key binding table (README.md:103-132 + useInputHandlers.ts):

key Action
Enter Submit current draft
empty Enter ×2 If there is a queue and it is busy, it is interrupted / if it is idle, the next queue item is sent
Shift+Enter / Alt+Enter / \+Enter Multiline newline (final fallback to terminal without modifier) ​​
Ctrl+C Interrupt → clear draft → (if neither) exit
Ctrl+D End
Cmd/Ctrl+G / Alt+G Edit draft with $EDITOR (Alt+G for VSCode/Cursor)
Ctrl+L new session (/clear)
Ctrl+V / Alt+V Paste text first → Attach image/path fallback
Tab Apply active completion
Up/Down Completion Traversal → Edit Cue → Input History
Ctrl+A/E, Home/End, Ctrl+W/U/K, Meta+B/F, word by word Backspace/Delete Emacs-like line editing
!cmd / {!cmd} Inline shell interpolation before executing/sending shell commands

Paste is inserted inline only (no newline smoothing, README.md:171), and the input history is persistent in ~/.hermes/.hermes_history (or HERMES_HOME sub) (README.md:173).

5.2 Queue/Steer/Interrupt — busy input policy

When the agent is busy, the plaintext is not transmitted immediately but is queued (README.md:163-169). Slash·!cmd skips the queue and executes immediately. The queue is automatically drained on each assistant response. On the Python side, _load_busy_input_mode() is set to queue | steer | Determine interrupt (default interrupt) (server.py:268-273). Explicit control with /queue(/q)·/steer (slash §6). This is a Hermes version of the mechanism that solves problems such as inputQueue+queryGuard concurrency control in [[01_cc_tui]].

5.3 Completion

Tab completion has a 60ms debounce (README.md:170). If the input starts with /, it hits the complete.slash RPC, and if the last token starts with ./ ../ ~/ / @, it hits the complete.path RPC. Path normalization is Python _normalize_completion_path (including WSL C:/ → /mnt/c/ conversion, server.py:633-644).

5.4 Blocking Prompt — approval/clarify/sudo/secret

The Python gateway can stop the main loop and request structured input (README.md:186-195). _block(event, sid, payload, timeout=300) creates threading.Event with request_id, emits the event, and waits for a response (server.py:728-740). Front handles this as a modal UI branch:

Event UI key
approval.request options (prompts.tsx) ↑↓+Enter, quick pick o/s/a/d(once/session/always/deny), Esc/Ctrl+C=deny
clarify.request Optional or free text ↑↓+Enter, number quick pick, “Other” → free input
sudo.request/secret.request masked input (maskedPrompt.tsx) Enter=Submit, Ctrl+C=Empty Cancel response
session.list SessionPicker (/resume) ↑↓+Enter, 1-9 quick pick, Esc=Close

When the blocking prompt opens, the main chat hotkey is paused (README.md:159). _clear_pending(sid) clears only a specific session prompt, preventing an interrupt in one session from incidentally canceling the clarifying of another session in the same process (server.py:743-755).

6. Slash/Key Command UX

6.1 Local-first → Python-fallback three-stage dispatch

createSlashHandler.ts is the core of slash handling (ui-tui/src/app/createSlashHandler.ts). Order:

  1. Local registry Handled directly by the client with findSlashCommand (things that require screen manipulation). For registration, app/slash/registry.ts combines the 5 groups of core/session/ops/setup/debug (registry.ts:8-14).
  2. If not present, catalog alias matching — exact/prefix match from catalog.canon and recurse to canonical name (createSlashHandler.ts:48-79). If multiple matches, ambiguous message.
  3. If it is still not there, slash.exec RPC → In case of failure, fall back to command.dispatch RPC (createSlashHandler.ts:81-128). This ensures that Python owns aliases, plugins, skills, and registry commands, and TUI does not implement them redundantly (README.md:225-230).

The command.dispatch response type is exec | plugin | alias | skill | With send, if it is skill, “⚡ loading skill” is displayed and a payload message is sent. If it is alias, it recurses to the target (createSlashHandler.ts:106-128). This flow of injecting skills as user messages meshes with the prompt-cache preservation design of [[00_hermes_overview]] §4.

6.2 Client local slash commands (by group)

Actual local command names extracted from ui-tui/src/app/slash/commands/:

group file command
core core.ts help quit(alias exit,q) update mouse(alias scroll) clear(alias new) redraw status resume title compact details(alias detail) fortune copy paste terminal-setup logs history save statusbar (alias sb) queue steer undo retry
session session.ts background model sessions image personality compress branch voice skin indicator yolo reasoning fast busy verbose usage
ops ops.ts stop reload-mcp reload browser rollback agents replay replay-diff reload-skills skills tools
setup setup.ts setup
debug debug.ts heapdump mem

/copy copies the selected assistant response to the clipboard as OSC 52 (README.md:219, lib/osc52.ts), /details [hidden|collapsed|expanded|cycle] controls thinking/tool ​​visibility, /indicator switches the spinner style, and /yolo toggles automatic approval on a per-session basis. /mouse//redraw//skin etc. are pure screen manipulation and remain local.

6.3 Event Surface (Streaming/Tools)

Gateway → Front event type (_emit/_status_update in README.md:236-258, server.py):

  • Streaming: message.start/message.delta({text, rendered?})/message.complete({text, usage, status}).
  • Reasoning: thinking.delta, reasoning.delta, reasoning.available — Control exposure with display.show_reasoning config (server.py:921-922).
  • Tools: tool.start({tool_id, name, context}), tool.progress, tool.complete({duration_s, summary, todos?}). Progress mode is off|new|all|verbose(server.py:925-935). If verbose, attach args/result text with redact+capping (server.py:1504-1569).
  • System: session.info, status.update, background.complete, error, gateway.stderr, gateway.protocol_error, gateway.start_timeout.

6.4 Streaming Render

StreamingAssistant(components/streamingAssistant.tsx) draws a live area: completed segments + active tool lane(trail) + streaming assistant row + pending tool. For assistant output, if the payload contains ANSI, messageLine.tsx is output as is, otherwise markdown.tsx renders the Markdown subset (heading/list/quote/table/fence code/diff color/inline code/link) as an Ink component (README.md:176-184). Markdown rendering is supported by Python tui_gateway/render.py (make_stream_renderer, render_diff, render_message, server.py:116).

7. Second Surface — Classic CLI (cli.py)

hermes (without --tui) goes into HermesCLI (~697KB) in cli.py. The input surface technology stack is completely different from Ink TUI:

  • prompt_toolkit fixed input area: Configure full TUI with Application/Layout/HSplit/TextArea/KeyBindings/CompletionsMenu (cli.py:56-71). Secret masking with PasswordProcessor, support for CursorShape (cli.py:71).
  • rich output: Render banner/markdown with Console/Panel/Markdown. CJK width is handled as wcwidth (cli.py:775-779, :1876-1901).
  • Spinner thread: spinner_loop runs as a daemon thread, repaints with _invalidate(min_interval=0.1) while executing commands, only sleeps 0.2s when idle, does not redraw idle prompt every second — Intentional to avoid issues with tmux/Ghostty fighting viewport restoration when focus changes and input area moving Design (cli.py:14450-14467).
  • Derives slash from the same COMMAND_REGISTRY (hermes_cli/commands.py) — Ink TUI and CLI share command definitions ([[00_hermes_overview]] §2.7).

In other words, Hermes' input surface is a dual implementation of "Ink (JSON-RPC) / prompt_toolkit (in-process)", but command definition (registry), session core (AIAgent), and state (SessionDB) are shared.

8. Reuse seam / tight coupling risk

Reuse seam

  • The @hermes/ink fork itself is a separate npm package (depending on file:), so it can be separated into other Ink apps — AlternateScreen, ScrollBox, mouse 4-presets, and evictInkCaches are highly versatile.
  • tui_gateway JSON-RPC handler is independent of stdio/WS transport (write_json polymorphism) → new clients (iOS/web) can be attached without reimplementing the handler (ws.py is evidence).
  • Slash 3-level dispatch (local → catalog → RPC) is a neat separation of the patterns of “screen commands go to the client, logic commands go to the server”.
  • react-compiler(react/compiler-runtime) The premise is embedded in the build output, which limits porting to standard React (same risk as Claude Code).

Tightly coupled risk

risk Description
2-Process Life Combined The front is responsible for spawn/kill/timeout/stderr pumping of Python children — GatewayClient is a 731-line lifecycle code chunk (gatewayClient.ts)
Session key re-anchoring If compression rotates session_id, the gateway must rebind session_key into a new continuation session (server.py:1252-1326) — Vulnerability in distributed state
slash worker separate process Launch another persistent HermesCLI subprocess (_SlashWorker) for each session → Triple process (Ink, gateway, slash worker)
dual input surface Ink and prompt_toolkit keep the two line editors separate — force keybinding parity to test (slashParity.test.ts, terminalParity.test.ts)
DEC mode leak alt-screen/mouse tracking contaminates host shell on crash → resetTerminalModes + unconditional teardown defense, but inherently dangerous

List of core files/areas

  • ui-tui/src/entry.tsx — TTY gate + terminal reset + memory watch + render
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/entry-exports.ts — Fork public API
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/renderer.ts — Double buffer + blit fastpass
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/components/AlternateScreen.tsx — alt-screen + mouse 4-preset
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/layout/yoga.ts — Yoga property mapping
  • ui-tui/src/gatewayClient.ts — child spawn / WS attach bridge (line 731)
  • ui-tui/src/components/appLayout.tsx — Virtual scroll transcript + INLINE_MODE
  • ui-tui/src/components/appChrome.tsx — StatusRule + Scrollbar + SpawnHud
  • ui-tui/src/components/textInput.tsx — Own line editor (1340 lines)
  • ui-tui/src/app/createSlashHandler.ts — Triple slash dispatch
  • ui-tui/src/app/slash/commands/*.ts — Local slash command definitions.
  • tui_gateway/server.py — RPC handler registry (line 6997) + _SlashWorker + _block
  • tui_gateway/ws.py — WebSocket surface (reuse the same dispatch)
  • tui_gateway/entry.py, render.py, slash_worker.py — stdio entry/render bridge/slash worker
  • cli.py — classic prompt_toolkit + rich REPL surface

9. Comparison with Claude Code TUI

perspective Claude Code TUI ([[01_cc_tui]]) Hermes Ink TUI
Language/Structure JS (React), 622K single bundle TS (React 19), ui-tui/ separate workspace
renderer Custom Ink Fork (in bundle) @hermes/ink separate package (depends on file:)
Layout Yoga Yoga (itself native-ts/yoga-layout)
process model single process (in-process) 2(+1) processes: Ink ↔ JSON-RPC ↔ Python gateway (+ slash worker)
brain location JS Ahn Python(AIAgent/SessionDB), TS is screen only
Transcript Static output Own virtual history
remote surface None (local TUI) Re-exposure the same RPC with WebSocket (iOS/Web)
Number of input surfaces 1 2(Ink + prompt_toolkit CLI)
slash local/local-jsx Local→Catalog→slash.exec/command.dispatch 3 step fallback

cli-jaw comparison implications

  • (a) Input surface topology — Hermes is “screen/brain separation + remoteization”, cli-jaw is “host TUI delegation”. Hermes draws the screen directly with its own Ink fork, decouples the brain (Python) from JSON-RPC, and opens the protocol as is to remote clients via WebSocket (tui_gateway/ws.py). cli-jaw is a meta-harness that dispatches on the TUI of the host CLI (Claude Code, etc.) without having its own full-screen renderer — the TUI rendering dimension is intentionally delegated to the host, and the differentiation of cli-jaw lies in multi-channel dispatch/file memory/PABCD. In other words, the asymmetry between “making the TUI directly (Hermes) vs. orchestrating the host TUI (cli-jaw).”

  • (b) Multi-channel and multi-surface are different axes. Multi-channel in cli-jaw is messaging channel multiplexing (text input/output), such as Telegram, while Hermes' "gateway surface" is divided into two types — messaging gateway (channel multiplexing, same axis as cli-jaw) in [[00_hermes_overview]] §2, and TUI protocol remoteization (via WebSocket) in this document. Remote exposure of fully interactive TUI, axes not present in cli-jaw). If you want to provide cli-jaw-rich interactive surfaces remotely, Hermes' dispatch-transport-polymorphism (stdio/WS-same-handler) pattern is a direct reference point.

  • (c) Isomorphism of the busy input policy. Hermes' queue|steer|interrupt(server.py:268-273) + empty Enter cli-jaw's MEMORY.md directive ("Never exit while waiting; use /loop or ScheduleWakeup to poll") is a conceptual cousin of Hermes' /steer (message injection while running)·/queue — except that Hermes implements it first-class with client keybindings + gateway events.

  • (d) Separation of screen commands and logic commands. Hermes' three-stage slash dispatch (local screen commands are delegated to the client and the rest are delegated to Python with command.dispatch) is a boundary model that cli-jaw can refer to when distributing commands to multiple host CLIs. Since cli-jaw routes slashes/skills in the harness layer, Hermes' pattern of "display-only instructions owned by the surface, side-effects instructions owned by the core" can be applied to the channel-dispatch policy.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot (160_hermes-agent/) Current
Ink TUI + gateway Cited TS paths Hermes version bumps — check package.json
JSON-RPC surface Documented Transport fields may evolve

Change history

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1) Created first TUI/input surface dimension. 2(+1)-Process architecture (Ink ↔ JSON-RPC ↔ tui_gateway + slash worker), @hermes/ink own fork (entry-exports/renderer double buffer/AlternateScreen mouse 4-preset/Yoga), GatewayClient spawn·WS attach bridge, server.py handler registry·pool separation·transport polymorphism·_SlashWorker·_block Prompt, WebSocket surface (ws.py same dispatch), App tree (virtual scroll transcript/StatusRule/SpawnHud/quad spinner), own line editor keybinding·cue/steer/interrupt·completion·blocking prompt, three-level slash dispatch + local command table per group + event surface, classic prompt_toolkit+rich CLI second surface, Claude Code TUI comparison table, cli-jaw 4-item comparison included.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 001_tui-design/01_hermes_tui.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Hermes Agent TUI / 입력 표면

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/160_hermes-agent/ (TypeScript + gateway sources).

이 문서는 [[00_hermes_overview]]의 동작 원리 분석을 TUI/CLI 렌더링·입력·스트리밍·슬래시 UX 차원으로 좁혀 소스 기반으로 분석한다. 분석 대상은 실제 저장소 /Users/jun/developer/codex/hermes-agent이며, 모든 구조적 주장은 파일 경로(또는 path:line)로 근거를 단다. 골드 레퍼런스([[01_cc_tui]])와 달리 Hermes는 난독화 번들이 아닌 원본 소스라 식별자가 그대로 읽힌다. 분석 버전 기준은 acp_registry/agent.json의 0.15.1.

Claude Code의 TUI가 "622K 줄 단일 번들 안의 커스텀 Ink 포크"라면, Hermes의 핵심 TUI는 별도의 Node/TypeScript 워크스페이스(ui-tui/)에 자체 포크한 Ink 렌더러(@hermes/ink)를 두고, 화면(TS)과 두뇌(Python)를 newline-delimited JSON-RPC 파이프로 분리한 2-프로세스 아키텍처다. 즉 Hermes에는 두 개의 독립된 입력 표면이 공존한다.

  1. Ink TUI (ui-tui/ + tui_gateway/) — hermes --tui / HERMES_TUI=1. React+Ink가 터미널을 그리고, Python tui_gateway가 세션·도구·모델·대부분의 명령 로직을 소유 (ui-tui/README.md:1-3).
  2. 고전 대화형 CLI (cli.py의 HermesCLI) — 기본 hermes. prompt_toolkit 고정 입력 영역 + rich 마크다운 + 백그라운드 스피너 스레드.

여기에 더해, 같은 tui_gateway 핸들러를 WebSocket으로 그대로 재노출하는 게이트웨이 표면(tui_gateway/ws.py)이 있어 iOS/웹 클라이언트가 동일 RPC로 붙을 수 있다. 이것이 "Hermes는 자체 Ink TUI + 게이트웨이 표면"이라는 본 문서의 논지다.

graph TD
    subgraph Front["프런트엔드 (화면)"]
        Ink["ui-tui (React + @hermes/ink 포크)<br/>entry.tsx → App → AppLayout"]
        CLIrepl["cli.py HermesCLI<br/>(prompt_toolkit + rich)"]
        Remote["원격 클라이언트<br/>(iOS / 웹, WebSocket)"]
    end

    subgraph Bridge["전송 계층 (JSON-RPC)"]
        GC["GatewayClient.ts<br/>(child spawn / WS attach)"]
        WS["tui_gateway/ws.py<br/>(WSTransport)"]
        STDIO["tui_gateway/entry.py<br/>(StdioTransport)"]
    end

    subgraph Back["백엔드 (두뇌)"]
        SRV["tui_gateway/server.py<br/>(_methods 레지스트리, dispatch)"]
        SW["_SlashWorker<br/>(영속 HermesCLI 서브프로세스)"]
        Agent["AIAgent.run_conversation()"]
        DB["SessionDB (state.db)"]
    end

    Ink --> GC
    Remote --> WS
    GC -->|spawn -m tui_gateway.entry| STDIO
    GC -.->|HERMES_TUI_GATEWAY_URL| WS
    STDIO --> SRV
    WS --> SRV
    SRV --> SW
    SRV --> Agent
    Agent --> DB
    Agent -->|event 프레임| SRV
    SRV -->|event| GC & WS

    CLIrepl -->|in-process| Agent

1. 두 입력 표면의 분리

1.1 진입점과 TTY 게이트

Ink TUI의 클라이언트 진입점은 ui-tui/src/entry.tsx다. 셔뱅(#!/usr/bin/env -S node --max-old-space-size=8192 --expose-gc, entry.tsx:1)부터 8GB 힙 + GC 노출을 요구하는 무거운 Node 앱이다. 동작 순서:

  1. forceTruecolor.js를 가장 먼저 import — chalk/supports-color 초기화 전에 truecolor를 넛지 (entry.tsx:3-4).
  2. process.stdin.isTTY가 아니면 즉시 process.exit(0) (entry.tsx:16-19) — 파이프/리다이렉트 환경에서 깨진 ANSI를 토해내지 않는다.
  3. resetTerminalModes()로 직전 크래시가 남긴 mouse/focus/paste DEC 모드를 청소 (entry.tsx:23).
  4. Termux가 아니면 \x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[3J(전체 클리어+스크롤백 삭제)로 시작; Termux면 직전 출력을 보존하려 개행만 (entry.tsx:28-32).
  5. GatewayClient 생성·start() → 그 다음에야 동적 import한 App을 ink.render로 마운트 (entry.tsx:34-104).

ink.render는 표준 ink가 아니라 @hermes/ink 포크다(entry.tsx:94, exitOnCtrlC: false + onHyperlinkClick 훅 — 마우스 트래킹이 켜지면 터미널 자체 URL 감지보다 클릭 이벤트를 먼저 가로채므로, 링크 클릭을 직접 외부 브라우저로 연다, entry.tsx:99-103).

1.2 메모리 감시 — 장수 TUI의 자기방어

Ink TUI는 게이트웨이 데몬급으로 오래 살 수 있어, startMemoryMonitor가 힙을 감시하고 critical 시 heap dump를 남긴 뒤 process.exit(137)로 자살한다(entry.tsx:60-69). setupGracefulExit은 종료 시 resetTerminalModes() + 게이트웨이 자식 kill을 보장(entry.tsx:41-58)한다. 이는 [[01_cc_tui]]에 없는 방어 계층으로, 2-프로세스 구조의 부산물이다.

2. @hermes/ink — 자체 Ink 포크

Hermes는 ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/에 독립 패키지로 Ink을 포크해 로컬 의존("@hermes/ink": "file:./packages/hermes-ink", ui-tui/package.json)으로 쓴다. React 19.2 + react-compiler(빌드 산출물에 react/compiler-runtime의 c as _c 메모 슬롯이 그대로 보인다, AlternateScreen.tsx:2,52)를 전제한다. 표준 ink ^6.8.0도 의존성에 있지만 핵심 렌더 경로는 포크가 담당한다.

2.1 포크가 export 하는 표면

ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/entry-exports.ts가 공개 API다. 표준 ink에 없는 커스텀 컴포넌트/훅이 다수다:

분류 export 근거
컴포넌트 AlternateScreen, Box, Text, Spacer, Link, Newline, ScrollBox, NoSelect, RawAnsi, Ansi entry-exports.ts:3-13
렌더 진입 render, renderSync, createRoot, forceRedraw entry-exports.ts:25
입력/포커스 훅 useInput, useStdin, useTerminalFocus, useTerminalViewport, useTabStatus, useSelection/useHasSelection, useTerminalTitle entry-exports.ts:17-23
커서 훅 useCursorAdvance, useDeclaredCursor entry-exports.ts:14-15
외부 프로세스 useExternalProcess, withInkSuspended ($EDITOR 띄울 때 Ink 일시정지) entry-exports.ts:16
측정/래핑 measureElement, stringWidth, wrapAnsi entry-exports.ts:24,27-28
캐시 관리 evictInkCaches, scrollFastPathStats entry-exports.ts:4,26
외부 라이브러리 재노출 TextInput/UncontrolledTextInput (ink-text-input) entry-exports.ts:31

useExternalProcess/withInkSuspended와 evictInkCaches(메모리 압박 시 캐시 강제 축출)는 [[01_cc_tui]]의 커스텀 포크에도 없는, 2-프로세스+장수 TUI 운영을 위한 확장이다.

2.2 레이아웃 엔진 — Yoga (Claude Code와 동일 계보)

포크는 자체 native-ts/yoga-layout/(enums.ts, index.ts)를 두고, ink/layout/yoga.ts가 React Box props를 Yoga 노드 속성으로 매핑한다. EDGE_MAP(yoga.ts:32-)이 left/right/top/bottom/horizontal/vertical/all을 Yoga Edge로 변환하는 구조는 Claude Code의 applyYogaProps와 동형이다. 즉 두 하니스 모두 flexbox(Yoga) 기반 선언형 터미널 레이아웃이라는 큰 그림은 같다.

2.3 더블 버퍼 + blit 패스트패스

렌더러(ink/renderer.ts)는 frontFrame/backFrame 더블 버퍼를 받아 diff를 낸다(renderer.ts:17-29). 핵심 최적화는 prevFrameContaminated 플래그다: 선택 오버레이가 화면 버퍼를 사후 변형했거나, alt-screen 진입/리사이즈/forceRedraw로 버퍼가 리셋된 경우 blit(이전 화면에서 변경 안 된 셀 복사)이 stale 셀을 복사하므로 비활성화하고, 그렇지 않으면 O(변경분) blit 패스트패스를 탄다(renderer.ts:110-124). Output은 프레임 간 재사용되어 토크나이즈+grapheme 클러스터링 캐시(charCache)가 유지된다(renderer.ts:33-37).

2.4 Alternate Screen + 마우스 트래킹

AlternateScreen 컴포넌트가 풀스크린의 핵심이다(components/AlternateScreen.tsx). 마운트 시 ENTER_ALT_SCREEN + ERASE_SCROLLBACK + ERASE_SCREEN + CURSOR_HOME + DISABLE_MOUSE_TRACKING + enableMouse를 한 번에 써서 정확한 상태로 진입하고(AlternateScreen.tsx:74-83), 자식을 <Box height={rows} flexShrink={0}>로 감싸 viewport 높이에 묶어 네이티브 스크롤백을 끈다(AlternateScreen.tsx:48-49 docstring, :113-117). 언마운트 시 무조건 DISABLE_MOUSE_TRACKING + EXIT_ALT_SCREEN을 써 크래시가 DEC 모드를 호스트 셸로 누출하지 못하게 한다(AlternateScreen.tsx:90-98).

마우스 트래킹은 4-프리셋 머신이다: off | wheel | buttons | all. wheel(DEC 1000+1006)은 tmux에서 hover가 "No image in clipboard" 스팸을 유발하는 것을 피하면서 스크롤만 살리는 서브셋이다(AlternateScreen.tsx:17-24 Props docstring). Python 측도 같은 별칭표를 둬 양끝이 합의한다(tui_gateway/server.py:853-896 _MOUSE_TRACKING_ALIASES).

2.5 인라인 모드 (Termux)

풀스크린을 못 쓰는 환경을 위해 INLINE_MODE가 있다. appLayout.tsx에서 const Shell = INLINE_MODE ? Fragment : AlternateScreen(appLayout.tsx:393)으로 갈라져, 인라인이면 호스트 터미널 스크롤백을 보존한다. INLINE_MODE는 HERMES_TUI_INLINE로 명시 오버라이드 가능하며 기본은 Termux 감지값을 따른다(ui-tui/src/config/env.ts:52-60).

3. 전송 계층 — JSON-RPC 브리지

3.1 GatewayClient (TS) — 자식 spawn 또는 WS attach

ui-tui/src/gatewayClient.ts의 GatewayClient(EventEmitter 상속)가 프런트의 유일한 백엔드 창구다. 두 전송 모드를 지원한다:

  • Spawn 모드(기본): python -m tui_gateway.entry를 자식으로 띄운다(gatewayClient.ts:337). 인터프리터 해석 순서는 HERMES_PYTHON → PYTHON → $VIRTUAL_ENV/bin/python → ./.venv → ./venv → python3(gatewayClient.ts:44-63). stdout은 readline으로 줄단위 파싱, 깨진 줄은 gateway.protocol_error 이벤트로 격리하고(:341-350), stderr는 gateway.stderr 이벤트로 펌프(:352-362) — 둘 다 터미널에 직접 쓰지 않는다(JSON-RPC 파이프 오염 방지).
  • Attach 모드: HERMES_TUI_GATEWAY_URL이 있으면 WebSocket으로 기존 게이트웨이에 붙는다(gatewayClient.ts:404-498). HERMES_TUI_SIDECAR_URL이 있으면 모든 event 프레임을 대시보드 사이드바로 미러링(:261-304).

견고성 코드가 무겁다: stale child/socket을 identity 게이트로 무시(:364-401, :471-487), 시작 타임아웃 시 stderr tail 20줄을 gateway.start_timeout payload에 붙여 "잘못된 python / 의존성 누락 / config 파싱 실패"를 한눈에 구분(:227-246), URL 로그에서 bearer 토큰 redact(:102-122). RPC 타임아웃은 기본 120s(:15).

3.2 tui_gateway/server.py (Python) — 핸들러 레지스트리

서버는 @method("name") 데코레이터로 _methods dict에 핸들러를 셀프-등록(server.py:438-443)하고, dispatch()가 라우팅한다(server.py:477-516). 설계 디테일:

  • stdout 보호: 진짜 stdout을 _real_stdout에 보관하고 sys.stdout = sys.stderr로 재지정(server.py:175-176) — 라이브러리/도구의 stray print()가 JSON 프로토콜을 깨지 않고 무해한 gateway.stderr가 된다.
  • 느린 핸들러 풀 분리: slash.exec, cli.exec, shell.exec, session.resume/branch/compress, skills.manage, browser.manage는 수 초~수 분 블록하므로 작은 스레드풀로 보내고, 나머지는 메인 스레드 인라인 처리 — 그 사이 approval.respond/session.interrupt가 stdin에서 굶지 않게 한다(server.py:138-170, :497-513).
  • 트랜스포트 다형성: write_json이 event 프레임이면 그 session_id에 묶인 트랜스포트로, 아니면 contextvar 바인딩, 그것도 없으면 모듈 stdio로 라우팅(server.py:365-383) — 같은 핸들러가 stdio/WS를 투명하게 지원하는 근거.
  • 패닉 로거: 미처리 예외/스레드 예외를 ~/.hermes/logs/tui_gateway_crash.log에 적고 한 줄 요약을 stderr로 흘려 TUI Activity에 띄운다(server.py:49-107).

3.3 _SlashWorker — 영속 HermesCLI 서브프로세스

슬래시 명령 일부는 게이트웨이가 직접 처리하지 않고, 세션마다 띄운 영속 tui_gateway.slash_worker 서브프로세스(내부적으로 HermesCLI)에 위임한다(server.py:184-265). run(command)는 seq id로 요청/응답을 매칭하고 타임아웃(HERMES_TUI_SLASH_TIMEOUT_S, 기본 45s)을 건다. 모델 전환 시 워커를 재시작(server.py:1077-1090 _restart_slash_worker). 이로써 Python CLI가 이미 구현한 명령 로직을 TUI에서 중복 구현하지 않는다.

3.4 WebSocket 표면 — 같은 핸들러 재사용

tui_gateway/ws.py는 tui_gateway.server.dispatch를 그대로(verbatim) 재사용한다(모듈 docstring ws.py:1-21). 따라서 모든 RPC 메서드·슬래시·approval/clarify/sudo 플로우·에이전트 이벤트가 클라이언트가 Ink-over-stdio든 iOS/웹-over-WebSocket이든 동일 핸들러를 탄다. 와이어 포맷도 동일(newline-delimited JSON-RPC). WSTransport.write는 풀 워커 스레드에서 run_coroutine_threadsafe로 루프에 마샬링하되, 루프 스레드 자체에서 호출 시 데드락을 감지해 fire-and-forget 한다(ws.py:51-). 이것이 "게이트웨이 표면" 차원의 핵심으로, [[00_hermes_overview]]의 다채널 게이트웨이(메시징)와는 다른, TUI 프로토콜의 원격화다.

4. 화면 구성 — App 트리

최상위 App은 얇다(ui-tui/src/app.tsx:9-25): useMainApp(gw)이 모든 상태를 모으고, GatewayProvider로 게이트웨이를 context 주입한 뒤 AppLayout에 actions/composer/progress/status/transcript를 넘긴다. 무거운 로직은 src/app/* 훅으로 분해된다(createGatewayEventHandler.ts, createSlashHandler.ts, useComposerState.ts, useInputHandlers.ts, turnController.ts 등, ui-tui/README.md:71-80).

4.1 트랜스크립트 — 가상화 스크롤

AppLayout은 트랜스크립트를 ScrollBox로 그리되 가상 히스토리(virtual list) 로 렌더한다(appLayout.tsx:90-148): virtualHistory.topSpacer/bottomSpacer Box로 화면 밖 영역을 높이만 차지하게 두고, virtualRows.slice(start, end)만 실제 렌더(appLayout.tsx:103-137). 각 행은 measureRef로 높이를 측정해 오프셋 캐시를 만든다. 이는 [[01_cc_tui]]의 Static 컴포넌트(렌더된 줄을 고정 출력) 전략과 다른, 자체 가상 리스트 구현이다(테스트도 두텁다: useVirtualHistory*, virtualHistoryClamp/OffsetCache 등).

오른쪽엔 직접 구현한 TranscriptScrollbar가 붙어 마우스 드래그로 점프 가능한 thumb를 그린다(appChrome.tsx:451-522). StickyPromptTracker는 현재 viewport 상단에 걸친 user 프롬프트를 추적해 sticky 헤더로 표시(appChrome.tsx:442-449).

4.2 상태 표시줄 (StatusRule)

하단/상단 상태 줄은 appChrome.tsx:294-424의 StatusRule이다. 왼쪽 영역(truncate)에 다음을 │ 구분으로 순차 표시한다:

세그먼트 표시 내용 근거
상태/스피너 busy면 FaceTicker(회전 얼굴+동사+경과), idle면 정적 status appChrome.tsx:346-352
모델 shortModelLabel(claude-/anthropic- 접두 제거) + effort + fast :353-356,256-267
컨텍스트 used/max 토큰 + [████░░] 바 + % (50/80/95% 색 단계) :316-368,126-155
세션 시간 SessionDuration(1s 틱) :369-374
압축 횟수 cmp N (5/10 임계 색상) :375-384
서브에이전트 HUD SpawnHud: depth d/, 동시성 ⚡/, paused ⏸, cap 시 ⚠ :174-233,385
음성 voiceLabel(●/◉ 상태) :386-396
라이브 세션 수 / bg / 비용 N sessions(클릭 가능), N bg, $cost :330-409

오른쪽엔 cwd 라벨(truncate-end)이 붙고, statusRuleWidths가 좌/우/구분자 폭을 컬럼 수 기준으로 배분한다(appChrome.tsx:157-172). /statusbar(/sb)로 off/top/bottom 전환(server.py:842-851 _STATUSBAR_MODES).

4.3 인디케이터 스타일 — 4종 스피너

busy 스피너는 ascii | emoji | kaomoji | unicode 4스타일이다(appChrome.tsx:47-76, Python 검증표 server.py:653-654, 기본 kaomoji). kaomoji는 FACES 회전(2.5s), emoji는 ['⚕ ','🌀','🤔','✨','🍵','🔮'], ascii는 |/-\(100ms), unicode는 braille 스피너(동사 숨김). unicode-animations 패키지를 쓴다(appChrome.tsx:4).

5. 입력 처리

5.1 자체 라인 에디터 (textInput.tsx)

메인 챗 입력은 ink-text-input이 아니라 자체 구현 라인 에디터 components/textInput.tsx(1340줄)다. ink-text-input은 clarify free-text/마스킹 프롬프트 같은 단순 케이스에만 쓴다(ui-tui/README.md:158). 키 바인딩 표(README.md:103-132 + useInputHandlers.ts):

키 동작
Enter 현재 draft 제출
빈 Enter ×2 큐 있고 busy면 인터럽트 / idle면 다음 큐 항목 전송
Shift+Enter / Alt+Enter / \+Enter 멀티라인 개행 (마지막은 모디파이어 없는 터미널 폴백)
Ctrl+C 인터럽트 → draft 클리어 → (둘 다 없으면) 종료
Ctrl+D 종료
Cmd/Ctrl+G / Alt+G $EDITOR로 draft 편집 (VSCode/Cursor는 Alt+G)
Ctrl+L 새 세션 (/clear)
Ctrl+V / Alt+V 텍스트 우선 붙여넣기 → 이미지/경로 첨부 폴백
Tab 활성 완성 적용
Up/Down 완성 순회 → 큐 편집 → 입력 히스토리
Ctrl+A/E, Home/End, Ctrl+W/U/K, Meta+B/F, 워드 단위 Backspace/Delete Emacs류 줄 편집
!cmd / {!cmd} 셸 명령 실행 / 전송 전 인라인 셸 보간

붙여넣기는 inline으로만 삽입(개행 평탄화 없음, README.md:171), 입력 히스토리는 ~/.hermes/.hermes_history(또는 HERMES_HOME 하위)에 영속(README.md:173).

5.2 큐 / 스티어 / 인터럽트 — busy 입력 정책

에이전트가 busy일 때 평문은 즉시 전송이 아니라 큐잉된다(README.md:163-169). 슬래시·!cmd는 큐를 건너뛰고 즉시 실행. 큐는 어시스턴트 응답마다 자동 드레인된다. Python 측 _load_busy_input_mode()가 queue | steer | interrupt(기본 interrupt)를 결정(server.py:268-273). /queue(/q)·/steer로 명시 제어(슬래시 §6). 이는 [[01_cc_tui]]의 inputQueue+queryGuard 동시성 제어와 같은 문제를 푸는 Hermes판 메커니즘이다.

5.3 완성 (completion)

탭 완성은 60ms 디바운스(README.md:170). 입력이 /로 시작하면 complete.slash RPC, 마지막 토큰이 ./ ../ ~/ / @로 시작하면 complete.path RPC를 친다. 경로 정규화는 Python _normalize_completion_path(WSL C:/ → /mnt/c/ 변환 포함, server.py:633-644).

5.4 블로킹 프롬프트 — approval/clarify/sudo/secret

Python 게이트웨이가 메인 루프를 멈추고 구조화 입력을 요청할 수 있다(README.md:186-195). _block(event, sid, payload, timeout=300)이 request_id로 threading.Event를 만들고 이벤트를 emit한 뒤 답을 기다린다(server.py:728-740). 프런트는 이를 모달 UI 분기로 처리:

이벤트 UI 키
approval.request 선택지 (prompts.tsx) ↑↓+Enter, 퀵픽 o/s/a/d(once/session/always/deny), Esc/Ctrl+C=deny
clarify.request 선택지 or 자유텍스트 ↑↓+Enter, 숫자 퀵픽, "Other"→자유입력
sudo.request/secret.request 마스킹 입력 (maskedPrompt.tsx) Enter=제출, Ctrl+C=빈 응답 취소
session.list SessionPicker (/resume) ↑↓+Enter, 1-9 퀵픽, Esc=닫기

블로킹 프롬프트가 열리면 메인 챗 핫키는 정지(README.md:159). _clear_pending(sid)는 특정 세션 프롬프트만 풀어, 한 세션의 interrupt가 같은 프로세스의 다른 세션 clarify를 부수적으로 취소하지 않게 한다(server.py:743-755).

6. 슬래시 / 키 명령 UX

6.1 로컬-우선 → Python-폴백 3단 디스패치

createSlashHandler.ts가 슬래시 처리의 핵심이다(ui-tui/src/app/createSlashHandler.ts). 순서:

  1. 로컬 레지스트리 findSlashCommand로 클라이언트가 직접 처리(화면 조작이 필요한 것들). 등록은 app/slash/registry.ts가 core/session/ops/setup/debug 5그룹을 합친다(registry.ts:8-14).
  2. 없으면 카탈로그 별칭 매칭 — catalog.canon에서 정확/접두 매칭해 정규 이름으로 재귀(createSlashHandler.ts:48-79). 다중 매칭이면 ambiguous 메시지.
  3. 그래도 없으면 slash.exec RPC → 실패 시 command.dispatch RPC로 폴백(createSlashHandler.ts:81-128). 이로써 Python이 별칭·플러그인·스킬·레지스트리 명령을 소유하고 TUI는 중복 구현하지 않는다(README.md:225-230).

command.dispatch 응답 타입은 exec | plugin | alias | skill | send로, skill이면 "⚡ loading skill"을 띄우고 페이로드 메시지를 전송, alias면 타깃으로 재귀(createSlashHandler.ts:106-128). 스킬을 user 메시지로 주입하는 이 흐름은 [[00_hermes_overview]] §4의 prompt-캐시 보존 설계와 맞물린다.

6.2 클라이언트 로컬 슬래시 명령 (그룹별)

ui-tui/src/app/slash/commands/에서 추출한 실제 로컬 명령 이름:

그룹 파일 명령
core core.ts help quit(alias exit,q) update mouse(alias scroll) clear(alias new) redraw status resume title compact details(alias detail) fortune copy paste terminal-setup logs history save statusbar(alias sb) queue steer undo retry
session session.ts background model sessions image personality compress branch voice skin indicator yolo reasoning fast busy verbose usage
ops ops.ts stop reload-mcp reload browser rollback agents replay replay-diff reload-skills skills tools
setup setup.ts setup
debug debug.ts heapdump mem

/copy는 선택된 어시스턴트 응답을 OSC 52로 클립보드에 복사(README.md:219, lib/osc52.ts), /details [hidden|collapsed|expanded|cycle]는 thinking/tool 가시성 제어, /indicator는 스피너 스타일 전환, /yolo는 세션 단위 자동승인 토글. /mouse//redraw//skin 등은 순수 화면 조작이라 로컬에 남는다.

6.3 이벤트 표면 (스트리밍/도구)

게이트웨이→프런트 event 타입(README.md:236-258, server.py의 _emit/_status_update):

  • 스트리밍: message.start/message.delta({text, rendered?})/message.complete({text, usage, status}).
  • 추론: thinking.delta, reasoning.delta, reasoning.available — display.show_reasoning config로 노출 제어(server.py:921-922).
  • 도구: tool.start({tool_id, name, context}), tool.progress, tool.complete({duration_s, summary, todos?}). 진행 모드는 off|new|all|verbose(server.py:925-935). verbose면 args/result 텍스트를 redact+capping해 첨부(server.py:1504-1569).
  • 시스템: session.info, status.update, background.complete, error, gateway.stderr, gateway.protocol_error, gateway.start_timeout.

6.4 스트리밍 렌더

StreamingAssistant(components/streamingAssistant.tsx)가 라이브 영역을 그린다: 완료 세그먼트들 + 활성 도구 lane(trail) + 스트리밍 중 어시스턴트 행 + 보류 도구. 어시스턴트 출력은 페이로드에 ANSI가 있으면 messageLine.tsx가 그대로 출력, 아니면 markdown.tsx가 마크다운 서브셋(헤딩/리스트/인용/표/펜스 코드/diff 색/인라인 코드/링크)을 Ink 컴포넌트로 렌더(README.md:176-184). 마크다운 렌더는 Python tui_gateway/render.py가 보조(make_stream_renderer, render_diff, render_message, server.py:116).

7. 두 번째 표면 — 고전 CLI (cli.py)

hermes(--tui 없이)는 cli.py의 HermesCLI(~697KB)로 들어간다. 입력 표면 기술 스택이 Ink TUI와 완전히 다르다:

  • prompt_toolkit 고정 입력 영역: Application/Layout/HSplit/TextArea/KeyBindings/CompletionsMenu로 풀 TUI를 구성(cli.py:56-71). PasswordProcessor로 시크릿 마스킹, CursorShape 지원(cli.py:71).
  • rich 출력: Console/Panel/Markdown으로 배너·마크다운 렌더. CJK 폭은 wcwidth로 처리(cli.py:775-779, :1876-1901).
  • 스피너 스레드: spinner_loop가 데몬 스레드로 돌며 명령 실행 중 _invalidate(min_interval=0.1)로 리페인트, idle 시엔 0.2s 슬립만 하고 유휴 프롬프트를 매초 다시 그리지 않는다 — tmux/Ghostty가 포커스 변경 시 viewport 복원과 싸우며 입력 영역이 움직이는 문제를 피하기 위한 의도적 설계(cli.py:14450-14467).
  • 같은 COMMAND_REGISTRY(hermes_cli/commands.py)에서 슬래시를 파생 — Ink TUI와 CLI가 명령 정의를 공유([[00_hermes_overview]] §2.7).

즉 Hermes의 입력 표면은 "Ink(JSON-RPC) / prompt_toolkit(in-process)" 이중 구현이지만, 명령 정의(레지스트리)·세션 코어(AIAgent)·상태(SessionDB)는 공유한다.

8. 재사용 seam / 강결합 리스크

재사용 seam

  • @hermes/ink 포크 자체가 별도 npm 패키지(file: 의존)라 다른 Ink 앱으로 분리 가능 — AlternateScreen, ScrollBox, 마우스 4-프리셋, evictInkCaches는 범용성 높음.
  • tui_gateway JSON-RPC 핸들러가 stdio/WS 트랜스포트에 무관(write_json 다형성) → 새 클라이언트(iOS/웹)를 핸들러 재구현 없이 붙일 수 있음(ws.py가 증거).
  • 슬래시 3단 디스패치(로컬→카탈로그→RPC)는 "화면 명령은 클라가, 로직 명령은 서버가" 패턴의 깔끔한 분리.
  • react-compiler(react/compiler-runtime) 전제가 빌드 산출물에 박혀 있어 표준 React 이식을 제약(Claude Code와 동일 리스크).

강결합 리스크

리스크 설명
2-프로세스 수명 결합 프런트가 Python 자식의 spawn/kill/타임아웃/stderr 펌프까지 책임 — GatewayClient가 731줄에 달하는 생명주기 코드 덩어리(gatewayClient.ts)
세션 키 재앵커링 압축이 session_id를 회전시키면 게이트웨이가 session_key를 새 연속 세션으로 다시 묶어야 함(server.py:1252-1326) — 분산 상태의 취약점
슬래시 워커 별도 프로세스 세션마다 영속 HermesCLI 서브프로세스(_SlashWorker)를 또 띄움 → 프로세스 3중(Ink, gateway, slash worker)
이중 입력 표면 Ink와 prompt_toolkit 두 라인 에디터를 따로 유지 — 키바인딩 패리티를 테스트로 강제(slashParity.test.ts, terminalParity.test.ts)
DEC 모드 누출 alt-screen/마우스 트래킹이 크래시 시 호스트 셸을 오염 → resetTerminalModes + 무조건 teardown으로 방어하지만 본질적 위험

핵심 파일 / 영역 목록

  • ui-tui/src/entry.tsx — TTY 게이트 + 터미널 리셋 + 메모리 감시 + render
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/entry-exports.ts — 포크 공개 API
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/renderer.ts — 더블 버퍼 + blit 패스트패스
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/components/AlternateScreen.tsx — alt-screen + 마우스 4-프리셋
  • ui-tui/packages/hermes-ink/src/ink/layout/yoga.ts — Yoga 속성 매핑
  • ui-tui/src/gatewayClient.ts — child spawn / WS attach 브리지(731줄)
  • ui-tui/src/components/appLayout.tsx — 가상 스크롤 트랜스크립트 + INLINE_MODE
  • ui-tui/src/components/appChrome.tsx — StatusRule + 스크롤바 + SpawnHud
  • ui-tui/src/components/textInput.tsx — 자체 라인 에디터(1340줄)
  • ui-tui/src/app/createSlashHandler.ts — 3단 슬래시 디스패치
  • ui-tui/src/app/slash/commands/*.ts — 로컬 슬래시 명령 정의
  • tui_gateway/server.py — RPC 핸들러 레지스트리(6997줄) + _SlashWorker + _block
  • tui_gateway/ws.py — WebSocket 표면(동일 dispatch 재사용)
  • tui_gateway/entry.py, render.py, slash_worker.py — stdio 진입/렌더 브리지/슬래시 워커
  • cli.py — 고전 prompt_toolkit + rich REPL 표면

9. Claude Code TUI와의 비교

관점 Claude Code TUI ([[01_cc_tui]]) Hermes Ink TUI
언어/구조 JS(React), 622K 단일 번들 TS(React 19), ui-tui/ 별도 워크스페이스
렌더러 커스텀 Ink 포크(번들 내) @hermes/ink 분리 패키지(file: 의존)
레이아웃 Yoga Yoga(자체 native-ts/yoga-layout)
프로세스 모델 단일 프로세스(in-process) 2(+1) 프로세스: Ink ↔ JSON-RPC ↔ Python gateway (+ slash worker)
두뇌 위치 JS 안 Python(AIAgent/SessionDB), TS는 화면만
트랜스크립트 Static 출력 자체 가상 리스트(virtual history)
원격 표면 없음(로컬 TUI) WebSocket으로 동일 RPC 재노출(iOS/웹)
입력 표면 수 1 2(Ink + prompt_toolkit CLI)
슬래시 local/local-jsx 로컬→카탈로그→slash.exec/command.dispatch 3단 폴백

cli-jaw 비교 시사점

  • (a) 입력 표면 토폴로지 — Hermes는 "화면/두뇌 분리 + 원격화", cli-jaw는 "호스트 TUI 위임". Hermes는 자체 Ink 포크로 화면을 직접 그리고 두뇌(Python)를 JSON-RPC로 떼어내며, 그 프로토콜을 WebSocket으로 원격 클라이언트에 그대로 연다(tui_gateway/ws.py). cli-jaw는 자체 풀스크린 렌더러를 두지 않고 호스트 CLI(Claude Code 등)의 TUI 위에서 디스패치하는 메타-하니스다 — TUI 렌더링 차원은 의도적으로 호스트에 위임하고, cli-jaw의 차별점은 멀티-채널 디스패치/파일 메모리/PABCD 쪽에 있다. 즉 "TUI를 직접 만드느냐(Hermes) vs 호스트 TUI를 오케스트레이션하느냐(cli-jaw)"의 비대칭.

  • (b) 멀티채널과 멀티표면은 다른 축이다. cli-jaw의 멀티-채널은 Telegram 등 메시징 채널 다중화(텍스트 입출력)인 반면, Hermes의 "게이트웨이 표면"은 두 종류로 나뉜다 — [[00_hermes_overview]] §2의 메시징 게이트웨이(채널 다중화, cli-jaw와 같은 축)와, 본 문서의 TUI 프로토콜 원격화(WebSocket로 풀 인터랙티브 TUI를 원격 노출, cli-jaw에 없는 축). cli-jaw가 풍부한 인터랙티브 표면을 원격에 제공하려면 Hermes의 dispatch-트랜스포트-다형성(stdio/WS 동일 핸들러) 패턴이 직접적 참고점이다.

  • (c) busy 입력 정책의 동형성. Hermes의 queue|steer|interrupt(server.py:268-273) + 빈 Enter ×2 인터럽트 + 자동 큐 드레인은 cli-jaw의 /loop·ScheduleWakeup 폴링/큐잉과 같은 "턴이 진행 중일 때 다음 입력을 어떻게 다룰까" 문제를 푼다. cli-jaw의 MEMORY.md 지침("Never exit while waiting; use /loop or ScheduleWakeup to poll")은 Hermes의 /steer(실행 중 메시지 주입)·/queue와 개념상 사촌이다 — 단 Hermes는 이를 클라이언트 키바인딩+게이트웨이 이벤트로 1급 구현했다.

  • (d) 화면 명령 vs 로직 명령 분리. Hermes의 3단 슬래시 디스패치(로컬 화면 명령은 클라이언트가, 나머지는 command.dispatch로 Python에 위임)는 cli-jaw가 다수 호스트 CLI에 명령을 분배할 때 참고할 만한 경계 모델이다. cli-jaw는 슬래시/스킬을 하니스 레이어에서 라우팅하므로, "표시 전용 명령은 표면이, 부수효과 명령은 코어가 소유"하는 Hermes의 패턴을 채널-디스패치 정책에 대입할 수 있다.

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot (160_hermes-agent/) Current
Ink TUI + gateway Cited TS paths Hermes version bumps — check package.json
JSON-RPC surface Documented Transport fields may evolve

변경 기록

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent(NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1) TUI/입력 표면 차원 최초 작성. 2(+1)-프로세스 아키텍처(Ink ↔ JSON-RPC ↔ tui_gateway + slash worker), @hermes/ink 자체 포크(entry-exports/renderer 더블버퍼/AlternateScreen 마우스 4-프리셋/Yoga), GatewayClient spawn·WS attach 브리지, server.py 핸들러 레지스트리·풀 분리·트랜스포트 다형성·_SlashWorker·_block 프롬프트, WebSocket 표면(ws.py 동일 dispatch), App 트리(가상 스크롤 트랜스크립트/StatusRule/SpawnHud/4종 스피너), 자체 라인 에디터 키바인딩·큐/스티어/인터럽트·완성·블로킹 프롬프트, 3단 슬래시 디스패치 + 그룹별 로컬 명령표 + 이벤트 표면, 고전 prompt_toolkit+rich CLI 두 번째 표면, Claude Code TUI 비교표, cli-jaw 4항목 비교 포함.
20

Axis 02: Prompt & context

AXIS

002_prompt-context/02_hermes_prompt.md

Overview

Axis 02: Prompt & context — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 160_hermes-agent/ — agent/system_prompt.py, agent/prompt_builder.py (paths below).

  • 이 문서는 [[00_hermes_overview]]의 §4(시스템 프롬프트 조립)를 "프롬프트/컨텍스트 조립" 차원으로 확장해, NousResearch/hermes-agent(v0.15.1)의 시스템 프롬프트가 어떻게 한 번 빌드되어 세션 전체에서 재사용되는지, 컨텍스트 파일(AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules/.hermes.md)이 어떤 규칙으로 병합되는지, **각 프롬프트 fra

  • Hermes의 프롬프트 시스템은 [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]]와 근본적으로 다른 설계 철학을 갖는다. Claude Code가 system[] 배열에 섹션을 쌓고 __SYSTEM_PROMPT_DYNAMIC_BOUNDARY__로 캐시 경계를 긋는 반면, Hermes는 단일 문자열을 \n\n으로 연결해 만들고, 그 전체 문자열을 **세션당 한 번만 빌드해 `agent._cached_sy
  • 핵심 불변식 (agent/system_prompt.py:1-8, 73-78, 348-364):
  • "The agent's system prompt is built once per session and reused across
  • all turns — only context compression triggers a rebuild. This keeps
  • the upstream prefix cache warm."
  • 즉 Hermes는 prefix 캐시를 최대화하기 위해 프롬프트를 의도적으로 동결한다. 분/초 단위 타임스탬프조차 시스템 프롬프트에서 제거하고(날짜만 유지, system_prompt.py:326-332), 회상 컨텍스트·플러그인 컨텍스트·스킬 슬래시 명령·임시(ephemeral) 프롬프트는 전부 user 메시지나 API-call-time 주입으로 빼서 캐시된 시스템 프롬프트 바이트를 건드리지 않는다.
  • graph TD
  • subgraph Build["build_system_prompt() — 세션당 1회"]

  • C["context 계층(cwd 의존 컨텍스트 파일)"]

Topics covered

    1. 3계층 Prompt 모델 (stable / context / volatile)

  • 1.2 context 계층 — 주의할 ephemeral 제외 규칙
  • 1.3 volatile 계층 — frozen snapshot + 날짜 안정성

sequenceDiagram

    Comp->>Comp: agent._cached_system_prompt = new_system_prompt
    Note over Comp: parent_session_id 체인으로 세션 분할

Main analysis

Hermes Agent 프롬프트 / 컨텍스트 조립

분석 스냅샷: 160_hermes-agent/ — agent/system_prompt.py, agent/prompt_builder.py (paths below).

This document extends §4 (System Prompt Assembly) of [[00_hermes_overview]] to the "Prompt/Context Assembly" dimension, explaining how system prompts are built once and reused across sessions and contexts in NousResearch/hermes-agent (v0.15.1). Analyzes by what rules files (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules/.hermes.md) are merged, provenance and gating conditions of each prompt fragment, dynamic prompt builder for each model family, how context compression causes prompt rebuild, is analyzed based on the actual source file. The core files to be analyzed are agent/system_prompt.py (407 lines), agent/prompt_builder.py (1,507 lines), agent/prompt_caching.py (79 lines), agent/conversation_loop.py, agent/conversation_compression.py, agent/transports/chat_completions.py, It is tools/memory_tool.py.

Hermes' prompt system has a fundamentally different design philosophy than [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]]. While Claude Code stacks sections into a system[] array and draws cache boundaries with __SYSTEM_PROMPT_DYNAMIC_BOUNDARY__, Hermes builds a single string by concatenating it with \n\n, and caches that entire string in agent._cached_system_prompt, building it only once per session. It does not mark static/dynamic boundaries with runtime markers, but instead preserves the cache based on the principle of "don't put anything dynamic in the system prompt in the first place." 이것이 Hermes의 핵심 불변식(invariant)이다.

핵심 불변식 (agent/system_prompt.py:1-8, 73-78, 348-364):
  "The agent's system prompt is built once per session and reused across
   all turns — only context compression triggers a rebuild. This keeps
   the upstream prefix cache warm."

That is, Hermes intentionally freezes the prompt to maximize the prefix cache. Even minute/second timestamps are removed from the system prompt (retaining only the date, system_prompt.py:326-332), and all recall contexts, plugin contexts, skill slash commands, and ephemeral prompts are removed as user messages or API-call-time injections, leaving cached system prompt bytes untouched.

graph TD
    subgraph Build["build_system_prompt() — 세션당 1회"]
        S["stable 계층<br/>(prefix 캐시 핵심)"]
        C["context 계층<br/>(cwd 의존 컨텍스트 파일)"]
        V["volatile 계층<br/>(메모리 스냅샷·날짜)"]
    end
    S --> J["'\n\n'.join → 단일 문자열"]
    C --> J
    V --> J
    J --> Cache["agent._cached_system_prompt<br/>(세션 동안 동결)"]

    subgraph PerTurn["매 API 호출 (캐시 미접촉)"]
        Eph["ephemeral_system_prompt<br/>(call-time append)"]
        UserCtx["회상/플러그인/스킬슬래시<br/>→ user 메시지로 주입"]
    end

    Cache --> Send["effective_system 조립"]
    Eph --> Send
    Send --> CC["apply_anthropic_cache_control<br/>(system + last 3 msgs)"]
    UserCtx -.->|별도 메시지| CC
    CC --> API["LLM API"]

    Compress["컨텍스트 압축 이벤트"] -->|_invalidate_system_prompt| Cache

1. Three-tier prompt model (stable / context / volatile)

build_system_prompt_parts(agent, system_message) in agent/system_prompt.py creates prompts in three ordered hierarchies and returns them as a dict ({"stable":..., "context":..., "volatile":...}), and build_system_prompt() concatenates them with \n\n into one string. create(system_prompt.py:348-364). The hierarchy order is cache-friendly: most stable first, most volatile last.

tier Volatility Location (order of attachment) Content Source
stable immutable throughout the life of the process at the beginning (prefix) Identity, tool guide, operation guide for each model, skill index, environment/profile/platform hint system_prompt.py:84-280
context Intersession variation (cwd dependent) middle caller system_message + context file (AGENTS.md, etc.) system_prompt.py:282-299
volatile Changes per session/turn at the back MEMORY.md/USER.md frozen snapshot, external memory provider block, date/session/model/provider line system_prompt.py:301-339

If [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] wraps the dynamic section with Gg()/Gwq() and places it after DYNAMIC_BOUNDARY in the system prompt array, Hermes still puts the same volatile content (memory) in the volatile tail inside a single string, but lowers the date precision to days to achieve "byte stability for a day". The difference is that the cache boundary is managed not by explicit markers but by string stability itself.

1.1 stable layer — build order and gating

The stable layer is appended to stable_parts: List[str] in the following order (system_prompt.py:84-280). The key point is that all items are conditional, and the rule of “only insert a guide explaining the tool when the tool is loaded” prevents token waste.

# fragment Gating conditions Source Constants/Functions
1 Identity (SOUL.md or default) load_soul_identity or not skip_context_files → SOUL.md, if not, DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY prompt_builder.DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY:120, load_soul_md():1355
2 Hermes self-explanation guide Always HERMES_AGENT_HELP_GUIDANCE:130
3 Task Accomplishment/Non-Operation Guide _task_completion_guidance (default True) and valid_tool_names TASK_COMPLETION_GUIDANCE:285
4 Memory Guide "memory" in valid_tool_names MEMORY_GUIDANCE:136
5 Session Search Guide "session_search" in valid_tool_names SESSION_SEARCH_GUIDANCE:159
6 Skill Management Guide "skill_manage" in valid_tool_names SKILLS_GUIDANCE:165
7 Kanban Worker Guide _kanban_worker_guidance set (HERMES_KANBAN_TASK gate) KANBAN_GUIDANCE:174
8 computer-use guide "computer_use" in valid_tool_names COMPUTER_USE_GUIDANCE:393
9 Nous subscription block managed_nous_tools_enabled() and related tools exist build_nous_subscription_prompt():1273
10 tool-use forced guide _tool_use_enforcement analysis result (§3) TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE:250
11 Operation guide for each model Model name substring matching (Gemini/GPT/Grok, etc.) GOOGLE_..._GUIDANCE:370, OPENAI_..._GUIDANCE:308
12 Skill Index Any one of skills_list/skill_view/skill_manage exists build_skills_system_prompt():1039
13 Alibaba model name Walkaround agent.provider == "alibaba" system_prompt.py:202-209
14 Environment Hints (WSL/Termux) build_environment_hints() when not empty build_environment_hints():766
15 Python toolchain probe _environment_probe (default True), 1 line only for non-default environments tools/env_probe.get_environment_probe_line
16 Active Profile Hint always (default/named branch) system_prompt.py:242-267
17 Platform Hints agent.platform is in PLATFORM_HINTS or plugin registration PLATFORM_HINTS:442

Thanks to this gating structure, even for the same Hermes binary, (a) a session with the memory+skills tool enabled in the CLI and (b) a session with the limited token set of the webhook platform have completely different stable prompts — but freeze within each session.

1.2 context layer — ephemeral exclusion rules to watch out for

The context layer contains only two things (system_prompt.py:282-299):

  1. system_message passed by the caller (append as is)
  2. When skip_context_files is False, context files found with build_context_files_prompt(cwd, skip_soul)

The explicit comment is important here (system_prompt.py:285-286): ephemeral_system_prompt is not put in the context layer. It is only injected at API-call time to keep it outside the system prompt where it is cached/saved (see §5). Also, in gateway mode, use the TERMINAL_CWD environment variable as the context file search root rather than os.getcwd() (= hermes-agent installation directory) — otherwise the gateway will incorrectly load AGENTS.md from its own repo, wasting ~10k tokens (system_prompt.py:290-297).

1.3 volatile layer — frozen snapshot + date stability

The volatile layer (system_prompt.py:301-339) appends:

  • _memory_store.format_for_system_prompt("memory") — MEMORY.md frozen snapshot (when memory is enabled)
  • _memory_store.format_for_system_prompt("user") — USER.md profile (if active)
  • _memory_manager.build_system_prompt() — External memory provider block (if present)
  • Timestamp line: Conversation started: <day of week, day of month, year> + optional Session ID/Model/Provider

The reason the timestamp is in days and not in minutes is stated in the comments (system_prompt.py:326-331): minute precision overrides prefix-cache KV on all rebuild paths (compact boundaries, fresh-agent gateway turns, session resumption without save prompt). If you need the exact time, your model can call date in terminal (PR #20451 / @iamfoz credit). This is an alternative approach taken by Hermes to "reduce volatility" instead of [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] managing dynamic sections as separate cache keys.

2. Merge context files — first-match-wins priority

In this dimension, the biggest structural difference between Hermes and [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] is revealed. Claude Code loads all 5-layer CLAUDE.md (Managed→User→Project→Local→AutoMem) and merges them cumulatively. Hermes does the opposite — it loads only one project context type as first-match-wins (prompt_builder.build_context_files_prompt:1468-1507).

# build_context_files_prompt() — agent/prompt_builder.py:1490-1495
project_context = (
    _load_hermes_md(cwd_path)      # 1순위: .hermes.md / HERMES.md (git root까지 상향 탐색)
    or _load_agents_md(cwd_path)   # 2순위: AGENTS.md / agents.md (cwd only)
    or _load_claude_md(cwd_path)   # 3순위: CLAUDE.md / claude.md (cwd only)
    or _load_cursorrules(cwd_path) # 4순위: .cursorrules + .cursor/rules/*.mdc (cwd only)
)
Priority file Navigation range loader Remarks
1 .hermes.md / HERMES.md Navigate up to git root _load_hermes_md:1383 YAML frontmatter strip(_strip_yaml_frontmatter:99)
2 AGENTS.md / agents.md cwd only (no recursion) _load_agents_md:1406 Codex/Standard Compatible
3 CLAUDE.md / claude.md cwd only _load_claude_md:1422 Claude Code compatible
4 .cursorrules + .cursor/rules/*.mdc cwd only _load_cursorrules:1438 Cursor compatible, multiple .mdcs are stacked

Key implications:

  • Hermes unique format takes priority: Only .hermes.md is searched up to the git root, and the rest (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules) only look at the cwd directory. In other words, Hermes gives priority to its own format as first-class, and places other agent formats (AGENTS/CLAUDE/Cursor) as a fallback to “borrow if available.”
  • No cumulative merge: If AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md exist at the same time, only AGENTS.md is loaded. This is the exact opposite of Claude Code's “stack it all together” model. A design that chose simplicity and predictability.
  • SOUL.md is independent: ~/.hermes/SOUL.md is always included separately as the HERMES_HOME persona (build_context_files_prompt:1499-1503), and if it has already been loaded into the identity slot (stable #1), duplicate injection is prevented with skip_soul=True (system_prompt.py:91-97, 296-297).
  • Output Wrapper: The discovered context is wrapped in the header "# Project Context\n\nThe following project context files have been loaded and should be followed:\n\n" (prompt_builder.py:1507).

2.1 Context file security scanning (prompt injection defense)

All context files are scanned with _scan_context_content(content, filename) before entering the system prompt (prompt_builder.py:43-59). This function calls the shared threat pattern library in tools/threat_patterns.py with scope="context". If a match is made, the entire content is BLOCKed with the following placeholder:

[BLOCKED: <filename> contained potential prompt injection (<findings>). Content not loaded.]

The reason for the blocking is clear (comment prompt_builder.py:48-52): the context file is verbatimed into the system prompt without user intervention, so injections planted in AGENTS.md/.cursorrules of the cloned third-party repo should be prevented from directly gaining privileges. However, strict-scope patterns (SSH backdoor, persistent, exfil-URL) do not apply here — they are excessive for a normal repo containing security research/infrastructure documentation. This multi-layered defense links to [[00_hermes_overview]] §8.1 (Tirith).

2.2 Context file length cap and head/tail cutting

Each context source is capped at CONTEXT_FILE_MAX_CHARS = 20_000 characters (prompt_builder.py:875). When exceeded, _truncate_content() keeps head 70% + tail 20% and inserts a truncation marker in the middle (prompt_builder.py:876-877, 1343-1352):

[...truncated <filename>: kept <head>+<tail> of <total> chars. Use file tools to read the full file.]

Compare: [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] classifies 40,000 characters (Xg) as a "large file" and loads the entire file with a warning, while Hermes actually truncates at 20,000 characters and tells the model to "read the entire file with the file tool." Hermes is more aggressive with its token budget.

3. Dynamic model-aware prompt builder (model-aware fragments)

A notable feature of the Hermes prompt builder is that it dynamically selects prompt fragments by matching model family substrings. Because it is a multi-provider harness ([[00_hermes_overview]] §6.2), the same core runs Claude·GPT·Gemini·Grok·GLM·Qwen·DeepSeek, and the known failure modes of each model are corrected with family-specific guides.

3.1 tool-use forced gating logic

agent.tool_use_enforcement(config.yaml agent.tool_use_enforcement) interprets the value in 4 ways (system_prompt.py:150-177):

Setting value Action
True / "always"/"yes"/"on" Inject TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE into all models
False / "never"/"no"/"off" Never inject
list (e.g. ["claude","my-model"]) If the model name contains the corresponding substring, inject
"auto" (default) / Others Matching TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_MODELS hardcoding list

Hardcode automatic target (prompt_builder.py:267):

TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_MODELS = ("gpt", "codex", "gemini", "gemma", "grok", "glm", "qwen", "deepseek")

In other words, Claude/Opus series basically do not receive mandatory tool-use guidance (since they are already good at calling tools). In contrast, GPT/Codex/Gemini/Grok/OpenModel get a mandatory block to "actually call the tool, not just describe it".

3.2 Cumulative injection of operation guides for each family

After tool-use enforcement is turned on, additional family guides are added depending on the model name (system_prompt.py:166-177):

if "gemini" in _model_lower or "gemma" in _model_lower:
    stable_parts.append(GOOGLE_MODEL_OPERATIONAL_GUIDANCE)   # 절대경로·verify-first·병렬호출·conciseness
if "gpt" in _model_lower or "codex" in _model_lower or "grok" in _model_lower:
    stable_parts.append(OPENAI_MODEL_EXECUTION_GUIDANCE)     # tool_persistence·mandatory_tool_use·verification XML 블록
  • OPENAI_MODEL_EXECUTION_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py:308-366) consists of <tool_persistence>, <mandatory_tool_use>, <act_dont_ask>, <prerequisite_checks>, <verification>, and <missing_context> XML sections. Provenance is traced in the comments as "Inspired by OpenAI GPT-5.4 prompting guide & OpenClaw PR #38953."
  • GOOGLE_MODEL_OPERATIONAL_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py:370-388) specifies "adapted from OpenCode's gemini.txt". Absolute path, dependency check, parallel tool call, non-interactive flag (-y/--non-interactive), etc.

What's interesting is the explicit comment (prompt_builder.py:305-307) that the OPENAI_ prefix means source, not exclusivity — xAI Grok also shows the same failure mode ("answer plan, no execution") and receives the same block.

3.3 system→developer role swap for each model

The most subtle model-specific handling is role swap. If DEVELOPER_ROLE_MODELS = ("gpt-5", "codex")(prompt_builder.py:440), change the role of the first message at the API boundary (agent/transports/chat_completions.py:257-266, 441-445) from "system" → "developer":

# chat_completions.py:259-266
if (sanitized and sanitized[0].get("role") == "system"
        and any(p in model_lower for p in DEVELOPER_ROLE_MODELS)):
    sanitized = list(sanitized)
    sanitized[0] = {**sanitized[0], "role": "developer"}

The reason why the internal representation is kept as "system" everywhere and swapped only right before the API is because the OpenAI new model gives stronger directive compliance weight to the developer role (comment prompt_builder.py:435-439). This contrasts with [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]], which is combined with a single vendor (Anthropic) and does not require role conversion.

3.4 Alibaba model name walkaround

When provider == "alibaba", the actual model ID is explicitly injected into the system prompt (system_prompt.py:202-209) to circumvent the bug in which the Alibaba Coding Plan API always returns "glm-4.7" regardless of the request model. This fragment safely enters the stable layer because the model and provider are fixed at creation time.

4. Prompt fragment provenance table

Organize the source, gating, and hierarchy of each fragment at a glance (all agent/prompt_builder.py constants, line notation).

fragment constant line tier Gating provenance / purpose
DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY 120 stable Fallback in absence of SOUL.md Nous Research Basic Identity
HERMES_AGENT_HELP_GUIDANCE 130 stable Always Hermes self-explanation → hermes-agent skill loading inducement
MEMORY_GUIDANCE 136 stable memory tool Rules for creating persistent memory (declarative facts only)
SESSION_SEARCH_GUIDANCE 159 stable session_search tool Inducing reminiscence of past sessions FTS5
SKILLS_GUIDANCE 165 stable skill_manage tool 5+ After calling the tool, skilling / patch guidance
KANBAN_GUIDANCE 174 stable HERMES_KANBAN_TASK env Multi-Agent Worker Lifecycle Protocol (Long Block)
TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE 250 stable §3.1 Gating Forced to “execute, not explain”
TASK_COMPLETION_GUIDANCE 285 stable task_completion_guidance (default True) “Don’t stop at the stub/No fabrication” for all models (including comments on Opus·DeepSeek failure cases)
OPENAI_MODEL_EXECUTION_GUIDANCE 308 stable gpt/codex/grok GPT-5.4 Guide·OpenClaw PR #38953 Source XML Block
GOOGLE_MODEL_OPERATIONAL_GUIDANCE 370 stable gemini/gemma OpenCode gemini.txt adaptation
COMPUTER_USE_GUIDANCE 393 stable computer_use tool macOS Background Control Workflow
DEVELOPER_ROLE_MODELS 440 (API boundary) gpt-5/codex system→developer role swap
PLATFORM_HINTS 442 stable agent.platform matching Channel-specific format/media rules (§4.1)
build_nous_subscription_prompt 1273 stable Nous subscription active Subscription Feature Availability Block
build_skills_system_prompt 1039 stable skills tools Skill Index (Tier 2 Cache, §4.2)
build_environment_hints 766 stable Detection of WSL/Termux, etc. Execution environment path conversion hint
build_context_files_prompt 1468 context skip_context_files=False AGENTS/CLAUDE/.cursorrules/.hermes.md (§2)

4.1 PLATFORM_HINTS — Prompt branching by channel

PLATFORM_HINTS dict(prompt_builder.py:442-...) injects markdown rendering availability, media delivery rules (MEDIA:/path tag), and length constraints for each ~16 channels into the system prompt. Example:

  • whatsapp/signal/sms/bluebubbles: "do not use markdown" (not rendered)
  • telegram: "No table syntax → preferred bullet/key:value, MEDIA: native attachment as tag"
  • cli: "Do not use MEDIA: tags — it appears as literal text in the terminal, only absolute paths are plain text."
  • cron: "No users, no questions asked, fully autonomous execution"

The plugin platform fills the same slot with PlatformEntry.platform_hint in gateway.platform_registry (system_prompt.py:272-280). This is the point where the platform_hint metadata mentioned in [[00_hermes_overview]] §2.2 is actually injected into the system prompt.

4.2 Skill Index — Tier 2 Cache + Conditional Exposure

build_skills_system_prompt()(prompt_builder.py:1039-...) creates a skills catalog as a compact index and places it in stable. The cache is on the second floor:

  1. in-process LRU dict — Key: (skills_dir, external_dirs, tools, toolsets, platform, disabled) (prompt_builder.py:1073-1085)
  2. disk snapshot .skills_prompt_snapshot.json — Verified with mtime/size manifest, survives process restart (prompt_builder.py:1087-1118)

If both are missed, the full file system is scanned and a snapshot is recorded (:1119-1158). Skills are exposed to the index with _skill_should_show() only when the conditions are met for the currently available tool/token set/platform (:1106-1111), so the prompt is not bloated with irrelevant skills.

5. Context injection — Asymmetric strategy that does not break the cache

Hermes' consistent principle is "Only what is stable is in the system prompt, and what changes is in user messages or call-time"**. To organize what goes where:

Content Injection location cache impact Source
Identity·Tool Guide·Model Guide System prompt stable cache prefix (freeze) system_prompt.py:84-280
Context files (AGENTS.md, etc.) System prompt context Freeze during session system_prompt.py:290-299
MEMORY.md/USER.md System prompt volatile (frozen snapshot) Freeze at session start tools/memory_tool.py:11-23, 129-173
ephemeral_system_prompt append at API-call time (not saved) outside cache conversation_loop.py:966-967
external memory recall (<memory-context>) user message cache uncontacted conversation_loop.py:952-953
plugin pre_llm_call context user message cache uncontacted conversation_loop.py:955-958
Skill slash command (/skill-name) user message cache uncontacted skill_commands.py, AGENTS.md:150
Tool definition (schema) API tools parameter separate system_prompt.format_tools_for_system_message:378

Core code (conversation_loop.py:950-969):

# 캐시된 프롬프트 + ephemeral만 시스템에. 회상/플러그인 컨텍스트는 user 메시지로.
effective_system = active_system_prompt or ""
if agent.ephemeral_system_prompt:
    effective_system = (effective_system + "\n\n" + agent.ephemeral_system_prompt).strip()
if effective_system:
    api_messages = [{"role": "system", "content": effective_system}] + api_messages

Comments nail invariants (conversation_loop.py:956-964): "system prompt modifications break the prompt cache prefix... built ONCE per session and replayed verbatim on every turn... sent as a single content string so the bytes are byte-stable."

5.1 frozen snapshot pattern (memory injection)

The frozen snapshot in tools/memory_tool.py:11-23, 117-173 is the highlight of context injection:

  • When a session starts, load_from_disk() reads MEMORY.md/USER.md and freezes it in _system_prompt_snapshot.
  • If you use the memory tool during a session, it is immediately reflected on the disk (durable), but the snapshot or system prompt is not changed → The entire session prefix cache is preserved.
  • Snapshots are updated when the next session starts.
  • Scans each entry for injection/promptware patterns at snapshot build time, replacing text with placeholder when hit (memory_tool.py:70, 133-173) — blocking the path where "contaminated memory enters the system prompt as a frozen snapshot."

This has the same purpose as [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] putting CLAUDE.md as a userContext chat message (cache preservation), but the method is different: Claude Code takes the memory out of the chat channel, Hermes puts it in the system prompt but freezes it on a per-session basis.

5.2 Anthropic prompt caching breakpoint

For Claude series models, apply_anthropic_cache_control() (agent/prompt_caching.py) sets up to 4 cache_control breakpoints (system prompt + last 3 non-system messages) with the system_and_3 layout with the same TTL (5m or 1h) (prompt_caching.py:48-78, call conversation_loop.py:981-985). The docstring states that multi-turn input token costs will be reduced by ~75%. The same goal that [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]] manages static/dynamic boundaries with prompt-caching-scope-2026-01-05 beta, Hermes has simplified it to a "system + last 3 turns" fixed layout.

6. Prompt rebuild caused by context compression

The only exception to the invariant is context compression (system_prompt.py:4-5: "only context compression triggers a rebuild"). Compression flow (agent/conversation_compression.py:425-496):

sequenceDiagram
    participant Loop as conversation_loop
    participant Comp as conversation_compression
    participant Mem as MemoryStore
    participant SP as system_prompt

    Loop->>Comp: compress_if_needed (토큰 임계 초과)
    Comp->>Comp: memory_manager.on_pre_compress(messages)
    Comp->>Comp: context_compressor.compress(...) — aux LLM 요약
    Comp->>SP: agent._invalidate_system_prompt()
    Note over SP,Mem: _cached_system_prompt=None<br/>memory_store.load_from_disk() (디스크 재로드)
    Comp->>SP: new_system_prompt = _build_system_prompt(system_message)
    Comp->>Comp: agent._cached_system_prompt = new_system_prompt
    Note over Comp: parent_session_id 체인으로 세션 분할

_invalidate_system_prompt()(system_prompt.py:367-375) does not simply clear the cache; it rereads memory from disk, so that the rebuilt prompt reflects this session's memory writes. In other words, compression is a boundary that "logically ends a session and splits it into a new session (parent_session_id chain, [[00_hermes_overview]] §5.3)", and only at this boundary are frozen snapshots and system prompts updated.

If compression fails (aux LLM fails to generate summary) or a concurrent compression lock is held, the existing _cached_system_prompt is maintained and the message is returned as is, skipping rebuild (conversation_compression.py:412-415, 458-462) — the cache preservation principle is maintained even on compression failure paths.

Compare: [[02_cc_prompt|Claude Code]]'s auto-compact puts a "dialog does not hit context limits" declaration in the system prompt, but Hermes treats compaction as a session split + prompt rebuild event instead of such a declaration. Hermes explicitly couples compression with the prompt lifecycle.

7. Implications of cli-jaw comparison

  • (a) The cache preservation philosophy is strong on the opposite axis. Hermes enforces a single invariant (system_prompt.py:1-8, conversation_loop.py:956-964) at the code level: “Build the system prompt once per session and freeze it, all changes are made at user message/call-time.” cli-jaw is a multi-CLI dispatch meta-harness, so it does not directly own prompt assembly but delegates it to each child CLI (Claude Code, etc.) — that is, prefix cache optimization at the cli-jaw level is the responsibility of the host CLI, and the context (MEMORY.md, channel metadata) added by cli-jaw can break the host's cache less by borrowing Hermes' "subtract to user message" pattern.
