         └─ synthesizer (verifier 완료까지 todo)

Main analysis

Hermes Agent Subagent / Collaboration

Analysis Snapshot: 160_hermes-agent/ — subagent/collaboration dispatch.

This document analyzes at the source level how Nous Research's open source self-improving agent Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1) delegates tasks to other agents, collaborates with external protocols, and improves itself as a meta-agent. Unlike Claude Code's integration of fork/teammate/advisor into a single Agent tool, Hermes has (1) delegate_task isolated subagent, (2) integration with editor/external agents with ACP (Agent Client Protocol) adapter, (3) delegation to heterogeneous backends with multi-protocol model adapter, (4) Kanban multi-agent board, and (5) Curator self-improvement. It has a five-pronged collaboration mechanism called meta-agent as a separate code path.

Let’s first point out three key differences:

  1. flat depth-1 isolation delegation: delegate_task (tools/delegate_tool.py) launches a child AIAgent with its own task_id (=its own terminal session)** as a token set without parent history and with the blocking tool removed. The default depth is MAX_DEPTH = 1 (parent 0 → child 1) and grandchildren are rejected.
  2. Adoption of the ACP standard: Hermes exposes itself to VS Code/Zed/JetBrains as an ACP server (acp_adapter/), while calling external ACP agents such as GitHub Copilot as a backend as an ACP client (agent/copilot_acp_client.py) — i.e., agent-to-agent bidirectionality is implemented as a standard protocol.
  3. Heterogeneous delegation + meta-agent: Delegating children can be routed to a different provider/model than the parent (e.g. cheaper and faster OpenRouter model) (delegation.provider), and Curator (agent/curator.py) is a background meta-agent that periodically cleans up the skill library by launching a forked AIAgent as an auxiliary model.

Reliability: ■■■■■ (Very High) — All analyzes were confirmed with actual Python sources. Core files: [[tools/delegate_tool.py]](2,802 lines), [[agent/curator.py]](1,801 lines), [[acp_adapter/server.py]](1,952 lines), [[acp_adapter/session.py]], [[agent/copilot_acp_client.py]], [[acp_registry/agent.json]].

graph TD
    subgraph Parent["부모 AIAgent (run_agent.py, depth=0)"]
        Loop["run_conversation 루프"]
        DT["delegate_task 호출"]
    end

    subgraph Delegate["delegate_task (tools/delegate_tool.py)"]
        Build["_build_child_agent<br/>(메인 스레드 구성)"]
        Strip["DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS 제거<br/>+ 토큰셋 부모 교집합"]
        Pool["ThreadPoolExecutor<br/>(max_concurrent=3)"]
        Run["_run_single_child<br/>(자체 task_id + 터미널 세션)"]
    end

    subgraph Child["자식 AIAgent (depth=1, leaf)"]
        CLoop["run_conversation"]
        CTerm["자체 터미널 백엔드<br/>(_resolve_container_task_id)"]
    end

    subgraph Hetero["이종 백엔드 위임"]
        Anth["anthropic_adapter"]
        Codex["codex_responses_adapter"]
        Gem["gemini_*_adapter"]
        Bed["bedrock_adapter / azure"]
        CopilotACP["copilot_acp_client (ACP 아웃바운드)"]
    end

    subgraph External["외부 협업 표준"]
        ACPsrv["acp_adapter (ACP 서버)<br/>VS Code / Zed / JetBrains"]
        Kanban["plugins/kanban (멀티에이전트 보드)"]
    end

    subgraph Meta["메타-에이전트"]
        Curator["agent/curator.py<br/>maybe_run_curator → forked AIAgent"]
    end

    Loop --> DT --> Build --> Strip --> Pool --> Run --> CLoop
    CLoop --> CTerm
    Run -->|"부모와 다른 provider/model"| Hetero
    CopilotACP -.->|"외부 ACP 에이전트 호출"| External
    Run -->|"요약 결과만 반환"| Loop
    Curator -.->|"보조 모델, 메인 캐시 미접촉"| Meta
    DT -.->|"느슨한 결합"| Kanban

1. delegate_task — Isolated subagent architecture

The module docstring in tools/delegate_tool.py clarifies the design:

"Spawns child AIAgent instances with isolated context, restricted toolsets, and their own terminal sessions. Supports single-task and batch (parallel) modes. The parent blocks until all children complete."

What each child receives (docstring):

  • New conversation (no parent history)
  • Self task_id (self terminal session, file ops cache)
  • Limited token set (blocking tools are always removed)
  • Focused system prompts (consisting of delegation goal + context)

"The parent's context only sees the delegation call and the summary result, never the child's intermediate tool calls or reasoning."

This is isomorphic to Claude Code's explicit subagent ("zero context, brief like a smart colleague"). Hermes has no fork (parent context inheritance) transformation and always starts with a clean context.

1.1 Blocked Tools (DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS)

tools/delegate_tool.py lines 45–53 define tools that children never can have:

DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS = frozenset([
    "delegate_task",  # 재귀 위임 금지
    "clarify",        # 사용자 상호작용 금지
    "memory",         # 공유 MEMORY.md 쓰기 금지
    "send_message",   # 크로스플랫폼 부작용 금지
    "execute_code",   # 자식은 스크립트 작성 대신 단계별 추론을 해야 함
])

This blocking is enforced in two layers. _strip_blocked_tools() (line 672) removes the token set containing only this tool (delegation, clarify, memory, code_execution) from the child token set. The schema description(_build_top_level_description, line 2574) also specifies "Leaf subagents (role='leaf', the default) CANNOT call: delegate_task, clarify, memory, send_message, execute_code." to the model.

The rationale for each block is written directly in the code comments — the clarify ban prevents the child from user interaction, the memory ban prevents writing to the shared MEMORY.md, the send_message ban prevents cross-platform side effects, and the execute_code ban is a design intent that says "children should reason step by step, not write scripts."

1.2 Depth limits — MAX_DEPTH = 1 and orchestrator role

# tools/delegate_tool.py line 133-137
MAX_DEPTH = 1  # flat by default: parent (0) -> child (1); grandchild rejected
_MIN_SPAWN_DEPTH = 1
_MAX_SPAWN_DEPTH_CAP = 3

The default is flat — a parent (depth 0) floats a child (depth 1), and depth-1 children cannot delegate any more. delegate_task() (line 1960) checks depth immediately upon calling:

depth = getattr(parent_agent, "_delegate_depth", 0)
max_spawn = _get_max_spawn_depth()
if depth >= max_spawn:
    return json.dumps({"error": "Delegation depth limit reached ..."})

However, there are two opt-in extensions:

mechanism Action Reliability
delegation.max_spawn_depth Clamping with [1,3] (_get_max_spawn_depth, line 394). 2 or more enable nested delegation ■■■■■
role="orchestrator" A child can spawn its own workers by maintaining a delegation token set (_build_child_agent line 967) ■■■■■
delegation.orchestrator_enabled Global kill switch — If False, the orchestrator is quietly demoted to leaf (_get_orchestrator_enabled, line 432) ■■■■■

role is demoted to leaf only in two places (lines 904–913): when the kill switch is off or when the child depth is greater than or equal to max_spawn. The orchestrator child's system prompt (_build_child_system_prompt, line 569) is appended with a "Subagent Spawning (Orchestrator Role)" block modeled after OpenClaw's buildSubagentSystemPrompt, instructing it when to delegate (2+ independent subtasks) and when not (single mechanical task, no overall goal pass-through). Depth notes are written based on actual config values ​​so that the model does not invent overlapping capabilities that do not exist.

1.3 Own terminal backend — task_id isolation

The mechanism by which each child has "its own terminal session" is the task_id. In _run_single_child (lines 1476–1510):

# 안정적 subagent_id를 자식의 task_id로 재사용
child_task_id = _subagent_id or f"subagent-{task_index}-{_uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
...
return child.run_conversation(user_message=goal, task_id=child_task_id)

This task_id becomes the sandbox/container key for the terminal backend. _resolve_container_task_id(task_id) (line 975) in tools/terminal_tool.py is "Map a tool-call task_id to the container/sandbox key used by..." — The top-level agent falls into "default" with task_id=None, but each subagent passes its own task_id, so Get a separate terminal working directory and state (sandbox, file ops cache). The schema description also states "Each subagent gets its own terminal session (separate working directory and state)." (line 2582).

When combined with the tools/environments/(local/docker/ssh/modal/daytona/singularity) terminal backend abstraction described in the §00 overview, the subagent uses the same backend type as the parent, but receives an isolated instance with an independent task_id key.

There is also cross-agent file state tracking (tools/file_state.py), which adds a warning to the result summary if a child modifies a file that the parent has already read (lines 1722–1754):

[NOTE: subagent modified files the parent previously read — re-read before editing: ...]

1.4 Delegate processing and result return in agent loop

The parent's synchronous loop (run_conversation in §00) calls delegate_task with handle_function_call and blocks until all children are completed. There are two execution modes (delegate_task, line 2091):

  • Single task (n_tasks == 1): Run _run_single_child directly without a thread pool.
  • batch (parallel): Concurrent execution with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_children) (_get_max_concurrent_children, default 3, line 132). It polls with _cf_wait(..., timeout=0.5, FIRST_COMPLETED) and checks for parent interrupts every cycle, so even if the child stops, the parent is not blocked forever (lines 2123–2165).

Thread-safe composition is the key trick: every child AIAgent is first constructed on the main thread as a _build_child_agent (lines 2055–2086), and then executed on a worker thread. The reason is that constructing AIAgent() overwrites the process global model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names — the parent's tool names are saved first (line 2049) and restored after configuring all children (line 2089).

The child worker thread is injected with a non-interactive authorization callback (lines 1492–1500). The worker does not have access to stdin owned by the parent's prompt_toolkit TUI, so falling back to input() for dangerous commands will result in a deadlock. Therefore, depending on the delegation.subagent_auto_approve config, install the _subagent_auto_deny (default, safe) or _subagent_auto_approve (opt-in YOLO) callback as ThreadPoolExecutor(initializer=...).

Result return type — The entry dict created by _run_single_child (line 1684):

{task_index, status, summary, api_calls, duration_seconds, model,
 exit_reason, tokens:{input,output}, tool_trace, _child_role, _child_cost_usd}

status is completed / failed / interrupted / timeout / error. exit_reason tells the parent how it ended with completed / max_iterations / interrupted. Before returning to the parent, _child_role/_child_cost_usd is popped from the dict (lines 2257–2259), and the child cost is added to the total cost of the parent session (lines 2286–2299, ported to Kilo-Org/kilocode#9448). The final return is {"results": [...], "total_duration_seconds": ...} JSON (line 2303).

1.5 Timeout·heartbeat·interrupt

  • Hard Timeout: Each child runs in a separate ThreadPoolExecutor and is capped with _get_child_timeout() (default 600 seconds, line 367).
  • heartbeat: _run_single_child propagates the child's activity to the parent's _touch_activity every 30 seconds (_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL), preventing the gateway's inactivity timeout from firing during delegation (line 1369). If the child does not progress (idle 15 cycles = 450 seconds / in-tool 40 cycles = 1200 seconds), it is determined to be stale and the heartbeat stops.
  • 0-API-call diagnostic: If the child times out before the first LLM request arrives, _dump_subagent_timeout_diagnostic (line 1177) dumps the worker thread stack·config·prompt size to ~/.hermes/logs/subagent-timeout-*.log (issue #14726).
  • Interrupt propagation: interrupt_subagent(subagent_id) (line 188) finds the child in the module-global _active_subagents registry and calls agent.interrupt(), which recursively extends to the grandchildren. Since Python cannot hard kill a worker thread, it is a cooperative interrupt that stops at the next iteration boundary.

1.6 Observability — Progress callbacks and live trees

_build_child_progress_callback (line 683) relays child tool calls to the parent display. Two paths: CLI prints a tree view line (├─ 🔀 ...) above the spinner, and the gateway places five tool names in each and passes them to the parent progress callback. The subagent_id/parent_id/depth/model identity kwarg is carried on all events, allowing TUI to reconstruct the live spawn tree and route branch-specific control (kill/pause) to subagent_id. The module-global _active_subagents registry (line 152) and the set_spawn_paused/is_spawn_paused kill switch (line 158) are consumed by gateway RPCs (delegation.pause, delegation.status, subagent.interrupt).

The DelegateEvent enum (line 532) normalizes progress event types (task_spawned/task_progress/task_thinking/tool_started/tool_completed), and the _LEGACY_EVENT_MAP (line 555) normalizes outdated strings (tool.started, etc.). _extract_output_tail (line 224) extracts the last N tool results from the child conversation and supplies them to the "Output" section of the TUI overlay (cc-swarm-parity feature).

1.8 Post-completion processing — hooks and memory notifications

After all children are finished, post-processing occurs serially in the parent thread (lines 2240–2299). The subagent_stop hook is fired once for each child (line 2268), which is called on the parent thread rather than the worker thread, so that the hook writer does not have to worry about concurrency (connected to the declarative shell hook subagent_stop event in §00). parent_session_id/child_role/child_summary/child_status/duration_ms are passed to the hook.

Additionally, the parent's memory provider is notified of the delegation result — parent_agent._memory_manager.on_delegation(task, result, child_session_id) (line 2228). This allows external memory providers (such as honcho/mem0 in §00) to learn the goal and summary of the delegated task. The child session is chained to the parent session by parent_session_id (_build_child_agent line 1129) and persisted in SessionDB, so the delegation tree can be traced after the fact with session_search.

1.7 Delegation tool schema and dynamic description

DELEGATE_TASK_SCHEMA (line 2660) defines goal/context/toolsets/tasks(batch)/role/acp_command/acp_args parameters. The description field is a placeholder and every get_definitions() call _build_dynamic_schema_overrides (line 2638) is regenerated to reflect the user's actual limits (max_concurrent_children, max_spawn_depth, orchestrator on/off). This is to ensure that the model does not self-limit to the framework default (3/1) (comment on line 2489). The model supplied max_iterations is ignored and the config value is authoritative (line 1982).

A notable guide to the schema description (lines 2552–2573):

  • Prohibit durable tasks: "delegate_task runs SYNCHRONOUSLY inside the parent turn ... Children cannot continue in the background." → Instructs tasks that must survive beyond the turn to use cronjob or terminal(background=True).
  • Avoid language contamination: If the user writes in non-English, specify it in context — otherwise the child will respond in English, polluting the parent's final answer.
  • Self-report verification: "Subagent summaries are SELF-REPORTS, not verified facts." — For operations with external side effects (HTTP POST, remote writing, file creation), have the child return a verifiable handle (URL/ID/absolute path/HTTP status) and have the parent verify it directly.

Registration is self-registered with registry.register(name="delegate_task", toolset="delegation", ...) (line 2783) in tools/registry.py, and check_fn is always True (no external dependencies, line 564).

2. Role specialization

Hermes' delegation does not have a set of predefined roles like Claude Code's built-in agent types (Explore/Plan/general-purpose), but only two flat roles (_normalize_role, line 312):

role delegate_task holds Additional delegation Use
leaf (default) ❌ Not possible Focused Worker — Perform one task with a clean context
orchestrator ✅ Yes (within depth/kill-switch limits) Decomposes the goal, spawns its own workers in parallel, and synthesizes the results

The substance of role specialization is Token Set. The toolsets parameter allows you to give the child a set of tools, such as ['terminal','file'] (code), ['web'] (research), and ['browser'] (web interaction), narrowed down to the intersection of the tools the parent has (_build_child_agent lines 945–961, "subagent must not gain tools the parent lacks"). The MCP token set is preserved according to inherit_mcp_toolsets (default True) (_preserve_parent_mcp_toolsets, line 501). In other words, a "role" is not a static catalog, but rather a narrow set of permissions that are dynamically synthesized at the time of call.

The exact algorithm for token set narrowing (lines 926–968):

  1. Derive the parent’s valid token set. If enabled_toolsets is specified, it is as is. If None (= all tools enabled), it is converted from valid_tool_names to model_tools.get_toolset_for_tool() — to prevent tools (e.g. web) disabled by the parent from leaking to the child.
  2. When the child requests toolsets, use _expand_parent_toolsets (line 470) to expand the parent's composite token set (e.g. hermes-cli) into individual token set names and then intersect them — preventing rejection by simple name comparisons such as "web" != "hermes-cli".
  3. If _get_inherit_mcp_toolsets() is True, append preserves the parent MCP token set.
  4. Remove token set containing only blocking tools with _strip_blocked_tools (line 672).
  5. If effective_role == "orchestrator", the delegation token set removed in step 4 is unconditionally re-added (line 967) — because orchestrator capabilities are granted by role, not parent token set membership (the design basis of the test_intersection_preserves_delegation_bound test).

The available token set hint string (_TOOLSET_LIST_STR, line 130) shown by the child to the model consists of debugging/safe/delegation/moa/rl and hermes-* composite·all-blocking tokensets (lines 122–129).

Richer role specializations are in Kanban (§4) outside of delegate_task — workers in kanban_swarm.py receive a profile (profile=role) and a skills list.

3. ACP Adapter/Registry — Agent-to-Agent Standard

ACP (Agent Client Protocol) is the biggest structural difference that Hermes has compared to cli-jaw. Implemented in both directions.

3.1 Inbound — Expose Hermes as an ACP server

acp_adapter/ makes Hermes an ACP stdio JSON-RPC server and integrates it into the VS Code/Zed/JetBrains editor. Module configuration:

file Role lines
acp_adapter/entry.py CLI entry point — load .env, logging to stderr (stdout is only for sending ACP), start server after MCP discovery 266
acp_adapter/server.py HermesACPAgent — ACP method router 1,952
acp_adapter/session.py ACP Session ↔ AIAgent mapping, SessionDB persistent 628
acp_adapter/tools.py Tool Bridge (Hermes Tools ↔ ACP) 1,291
acp_adapter/permissions.py / edit_approval.py Approved 168 / 286
acp_adapter/events.py / auth.py Events / Certifications 279 / 79

main() in entry.py runs the server with asyncio.run(acp.run_agent(agent, use_unstable_protocol=True)) (line 257). The liveness probes (ping/health/healthcheck) are outside the ACP schema, so they return JSON-RPC -32601, but _BenignProbeMethodFilter (line 46) suppresses stderr traceback noise — because clients like acp-bridge treat -32601 as "agent alive".

As the session.py docstring shows, ACP sessions are persistent in the shared SessionDB (~/.hermes/state.db), so they survive process restarts and appear in session_search. When the editor reconnects after idle/restart, load_session/resume_session finds the session in the DB and restores the entire conversation. There is also WSL path translation (_win_path_to_wsl, _translate_acp_cwd, ​​lines 29–50) to handle scenarios where the Windows editor launches WSL Hermes.

The ACP method surface exposed by HermesACPAgent (server.py line 516) is rich — initialize(821) / authenticate(855) / new_session(1069) / load_session(1086) / resume_session(1130) / fork_session(1176) / list_sessions(1196) / prompt(1243) / cancel(1162) / set_session_model(1882) / set_session_mode(1916) / set_config_option(1932). In other words, the editor (client) controls session creation, resumption, fork, cancellation, model conversion, and mode change through ACP RPC. Interestingly, the single registry of slash commands from §00 is reused here, so that within an ACP session /help·/model·/tools·/context·/reset·/compact·/steer·/queue·/version are processed as _handle_slash_command (line 1629) — i.e. CLI/Gateway/ACP share the same command surface. _replay_session_history(979) flows the conversation back to the editor upon resumption.

Approval Bridge: Hermes' critical command approval is externalized as ACP request_permission. _OPTION_ID_TO_HERMES (line 21) in acp_adapter/permissions.py maps the ACP option id (allow_once/allow_session/allow_always/deny/deny_always) to the Hermes approval result string (once/session/always/deny). acp_adapter/edit_approval.py (286 lines) handles the file edit approval policy separately (_edit_approval_policy_for_state, server.py line 562). In other words, the native permission UI pattern for each channel seen in §00 is consistently implemented as the permission primitive of the ACP protocol in the editor.

3.2 Registry — agent.json

acp_registry/agent.json contains ACP agent registry metadata:

{
  "id": "hermes-agent",
  "name": "Hermes Agent",
  "version": "0.15.1",
  "distribution": {
    "uvx": { "package": "hermes-agent[acp]==0.15.1", "args": ["hermes-acp"] }
  }
}

Distributed as uvx hermes-agent[acp]==0.15.1 and executed with the hermes-acp command. This standard metadata allows ACP-compatible editors/orchestrators to automatically discover and launch Hermes.

3.3 Outbound — Calling an external ACP agent as a backend

The opposite direction is more interesting. agent/copilot_acp_client.py is an OpenAI compatible shim that forwards Hermes requests to the copilot --acp subprocess. docstring:

"This adapter lets Hermes treat the GitHub Copilot ACP server as a chat-style backend. Each request starts a short-lived ACP session, sends the formatted conversation as a single prompt, collects text chunks, and converts the result back into the minimal shape Hermes expects from an OpenAI client."

This is directly connected to delegate_task. The delegate_task schema takes acp_command/acp_args parameters (DELEGATE_TASK_SCHEMA line 2752), allowing non-ACP parents to spawn ACP child agents. If override_acp_command is set in _build_child_agent (lines 1054–1058), it forces the provider to be copilot-acp and initializes CopilotACPClient. In other words, it is a true agent-to-agent delegation based on standard protocols, where the Hermes subagent becomes another vendor's agent called the GitHub Copilot CLI, performs tasks, and returns only the summary to the parent.

The schema gates this feature conservatively — "Do NOT set this unless the user has explicitly told you a specific ACP-compatible CLI is installed and configured."(line 2760). agent/copilot_acp_client.py also features the deprecated gh copilot extension and defensive code (lines 36–48) that identifies the new @github/copilot CLI by its stderr fingerprint.

4. Multi-protocol model adapter — a form of heterogeneous delegation

The Wire Protocol adapters seen in §00 are themselves a form of “delegation to heterogeneous backends.” Hermes places the following adapters at its core:

adapter file protocol
agent/anthropic_adapter.py Anthropic Messages
agent/codex_responses_adapter.py OpenAI Codex Responses
agent/gemini_native_adapter.py / gemini_cloudcode_adapter.py Gemini
agent/bedrock_adapter.py AWS Bedrock
agent/azure_identity_adapter.py Azure
agent/copilot_acp_client.py ACP (Copilot)

delegate_task uses this as a delegation unit. _resolve_delegation_credentials (line 2345), if delegation.provider or delegation.base_url is set, routes the child to a completely different provider:model pair than the parent — "This lets subagents run on a completely different provider:model pair (e.g. cheap/fast model on OpenRouter while the parent runs on Nous Portal)."

Key bug-defense details: api_mode is not inherited when the child uses a different provider than the parent (lines 1025–1036, Bug #20558/PR #20563). MiniMax uses anthropic_messages and DeepSeek uses chat_completions, so inheriting the parent mode results in 404. Therefore, if the providers are different, it is forcibly redirected with api_mode=None or automatically detected using URL heuristics (_detect_api_mode_for_url, /anthropic suffix → anthropotic_messages) (lines 2387–2408).

Additionally, the child inherits/shares the parent's fallback chain (_fallback_chain, line 1083) and credential pool (_resolve_child_credential_pool, line 2312) to recover from rate-limit and credential depletion like the parent. If it is the same provider, it shares the parent pool (cooldown/rotation synchronization); if it is a different provider, it loads that provider's pool.

5. Loosely coupled collaboration — Kanban multi-agent board

If delegate_task is a synchronous, turn-bound delegation, plugins/kanban/ is loosely coupled collaboration that survives beyond turns. The tools/kanban_tools.py docstring explains the difference — Kanban tools are registered in the model schema only if they are dispatcher-spawned workers with the HERMES_KANBAN_TASK environment variable set, or if their profile explicitly turns on the kanban tokenset (a regular hermes chat sees 0 Kanban tools).

Why the tool is implemented as a Python tool instead of a shell call (docstring):

  1. Backend portability — Workers using Docker/Modal/SSH terminal backends cannot run hermes kanban inside a container (hermes is not installed). The tool runs in an agent Python process, so it always hits ~/.hermes/kanban.db.
  2. No shell quoting footgun, 3. Structured error.

The swarm topology in hermes_cli/kanban_swarm.py writes a small task graph to the existing Kanban kernel without introducing a separate scheduler:

planning root (즉시 완료)
    ├─ parallel specialist workers (ready)
    └─ verifier (모든 워커 완료까지 todo)
         └─ synthesizer (verifier 완료까지 todo)

Shared blackboard is also intentionally low-tech — a structured JSON comment on the root task (prefixed with [swarm:blackboard]). All status goes into the existing task_comments/task_events lines, and the dashboard, notifier, slash command, and dispatcher continue to operate without the new service. SwarmWorkerSpec(profile/title/body/skills/priority/max_runtime_seconds) contains worker role specializations. The Kanban tool family is kanban_*(show/list/complete/block/heartbeat/comment/create/link/unblock), where workers update the board.

6. Curator — Self-improvement meta-agent

The Curator in agent/curator.py (1,801 lines) is a background meta-agent that periodically organizes the skills created by the agent. strict invariants in module docstring:

  • Only touches agent-created skills (see tools/skill_usage.is_agent_created)
  • Never auto-deletes — only archives. Archive is recoverable.
  • Pinned skills bypass all auto-transitions
  • Uses the auxiliary client; never touches the main session's prompt cache

6.1 Inactivity-based triggers (no cron daemon)

maybe_run_curator() (line 1782) is the public entry point, called from the session-start hook. gate(should_run_now, line 199):

  • curator.enabled == True (default ON)
  • not paused
  • last_run_at is before interval_hours (default 7 days = 168 hours)

First run behavior: If last_run_at is not present (fresh install), it does not run immediately, but is seeded with "now" and postponed for an interval — this is to avoid auto-transforming the library at the first gateway tick immediately after hermes update. Additionally, the call side applies a min_idle_hours (default 2 hours) idle gate (line 1793).

6.2 Stage 2 Review — Pure Transition + LLM Pass

run_curator_review (line 1388) works in two steps:

  1. Automatic state transitions (pure, no LLM) — apply_automatic_transitions (line 256) transitions active/stale/archived based on skill activity timestamp. stale_after_days(30) elapsed → stale, archive_after_days(90) elapsed → archive, reactivate if used again. Skip pinned skills.

  2. LLM integration pass — _run_llm_review (line 1641) launches forked AIAgent and executes CURATOR_REVIEW_PROMPT (line 330). This prompt indicates "umbrella-building consolidation" — grouping hundreds of narrow skills into a class-level umbrella skill with PREFIX CLUSTER (MERGE / CREATE / DEMOTE to references/templates/scripts) and archive siblings. "If you end the pass with fewer than 10 archives, you stopped too early."

6.3 Main Cache Contactless + Secondary Model

Review fork never touches the main session prompt cache (lines 1727–1729: disable recursive nudge with _memory_nudge_interval = 0, _skill_nudge_interval = 0). provider/model is resolved in auxiliary.curator.{provider,model} (auxiliary task slot) (_resolve_review_runtime, line 1576), and if provider: "auto", it falls back to the main model — the same plumbing as the task-specific auxiliary model pins seen in §00. fork consists of max_iterations=9999 (hundreds of skill sweeps take 50–100 API calls), quiet_mode=True, platform="curator", skip_memory=True, stdout/stderr is redirected to /dev/null (line 1736), and upon exit It becomes close().

6.4 Dry-run and reporting

The dry-run mode (line 1412) skips the automatic transition and instructs the LLM to "REPORT ONLY — DO NOT MUTATE" with CURATOR_DRY_RUN_BANNER (line 303). We only create REPORT.md without any actual mutate and let the user review what he could have done. Live execution uses ~/.hermes/logs/curator/{timestamp}/run.json + REPORT.md (line 989), and the archived skills are classified as consolidated (absorbed into umbrella) vs. pruned (simple discarded). The classification reconciles three signals (_reconcile_classification, line 768): (1) the absorbed_into declared by the model when calling skill_manage(delete) is authoritative, (2) the model's structured YAML block, and (3) the tool call heuristic audit. When integrating skills, the skill reference of the cron job that references it is also automatically rewritten (cron.jobs.rewrite_skill_refs, line 1105).

This is the essence of “self-improving” — the agent creates the skill itself with skill_manage, and a separate meta-agent called the Curator periodically integrates and archives it as an auxiliary model.

7. Summary comparison of collaboration mechanisms

mechanism Cohesion Lifespan Context backend Source
delegate_task (leaf) Strong coupling/synchronization Parent turn limited Quarantine (no parental history) parent or override provider tools/delegate_tool.py
delegate_task (orchestrator) Strong coupling/synchronization/nesting Parent turn limited Isolation + Self Walker 〃 〃 line 611
ACP outbound (copilot) Protocol Combining Single session quarantine External ACP Agent agent/copilot_acp_client.py
ACP Inbound (Server) Protocol Combining persistent (SessionDB) Editor Session Hermes himself acp_adapter/
Kanban swarm loose coupling·asynchronous Turn/Session Survival Share board (blackboard) dispatcher worker plugins/kanban/, kanban_swarm.py
Curator Meta·Background Interval Trigger forked, main cache untouched Auxiliary model agent/curator.py

8. Implications of cli-jaw comparison

  • (a) delegate_task (depth-1, own backend) vs cli-jaw multi-CLI employee dispatch. Hermes' delegate_task launches a child AIAgent to the thread pool in the same process, but isolates the terminal backend with its own task_id. Always remove DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS(delegate_task/clarify/memory/send_message/execute_code) and keep it flat with MAX_DEPTH=1 — context isolation, permission narrowing, and depth restrictions are baked into code in one function. cli-jaw is more of a harness-level model that dispatches separate CLI/employee processes — the isolation boundary where the core enforces "blocking tool set + tokenset parent intersection + role demotion + depth clamp" like Hermes is either not explicitly present in cli-jaw or is replaced by a harness permission prompt. What cli-jaw should note is that it has observability and accounting of delegation built into the core, like Hermes' child cost rollup (_child_cost_usd summation) and stale-heartbeat diagnosis (0-API-call dump).

  • (b) ACP is a standard that is not in cli-jaw. Hermes implements ACP (Agent Client Protocol) bi-directionally — it exposes itself as an ACP server to the editor (VS Code/Zed/JetBrains) with acp_adapter/ (distributing uvx with acp_registry/agent.json), and at the same time with agent/copilot_acp_client.py. Calls an external ACP agent, such as GitHub Copilot, as an OpenAI-compatible backend. The acp_command/acp_args parameters of delegate_task allow true agent-to-agent delegation, where a non-ACP parent spawns an ACP child (agent from another vendor). cli-jaw does not have such a standard protocol integration layer, so editor integration and external agent calls are ad-hoc — ACP adoption is a direct reference point for cli-jaw when trying to interoperate with the editor/external agent ecosystem.

  • (c) Heterogeneous delegation — Hermes is more mature. Hermes routes delegating children to a different provider:model** than the parent (e.g. Nous Portal for the parent, OpenRouter's cheap and fast model for the child) (delegation.provider/base_url), redirects api_mode by provider (MiniMax=anthropic_messages, DeepSeek=chat_completions), and even falls back chain and credential pool. Inherit/share. Anthropic/codex/gemini/bedrock/azure/ACP wire adapter is in the core, so delegation becomes distributed to heterogeneous backends. cli-jaw has a high degree of coupling with Claude (Anthropic) single provider, and Hermes' adapter/credential-pool separation is the blueprint to use the "parent is strong model, child is weak model" separation delegation pattern for cost optimization.

  • (d) Meta-agent self-improvement loop — unique to Hermes. The Curator in agent/curator.py launches a forked AIAgent with an inactivity trigger (no cron daemon), umbrella-ifying (consolidate/archive/patch) the agent creation skill as an auxiliary model — never auto-delete (archive only, recoverable), pinned bypass, no contact with main session prompt cache, dry-run preview, It is a complete background meta-agent with consolidated/pruned classification + automatic rewriting of cron skill references. Although cli-jaw has a skill ecosystem and scheduler (/loop, Cron* tool), the core mechanism corresponding to “meta-agent that autonomously maintains skills created by agents as a separate auxiliary model” is not visible — this is the most differentiated collaboration pattern that Hermes can give to cli-jaw.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1) first created at subagent/collaboration level. delegate_task isolation subagent (DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS, MAX_DEPTH=1, own terminal session based on task_id, main-thread configuration + worker execution, batch parallel ThreadPoolExecutor, heartbeat/timeout/interrupt, child cost rollup, dynamic schema), leaf/orchestrator role specialization, ACP bidirectional (acp_adapter inbound server + copilot_acp_client Outbound + acp_registry/agent.json + delegate_task acp_command bridge), multi-protocol model adapter heterogeneous delegation (delegation.provider routing, api_mode redirection, credential pool sharing), Kanban swarm loosely coupled collaboration, Curator self-improving meta-agent (inactive trigger, pure+LLM two-stage, secondary model fork, main cache no contact, dry-run, consolidated/pruned classification), including cli-jaw 4-item comparison.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 005_subagents-collab/05_hermes_subagent.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Hermes Agent 서브에이전트 / 협업

분석 스냅샷: 160_hermes-agent/ — subagent/collaboration dispatch.

이 문서는 Nous Research의 오픈소스 자기개선형 에이전트 Hermes Agent(NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1)가 작업을 어떻게 다른 에이전트에 위임(delegate)하고, 외부 프로토콜로 협업하며, 메타-에이전트로 자기 자신을 개선하는지를 소스 레벨에서 분석한다. Claude Code가 단일 Agent 도구로 fork/teammate/advisor를 통합한 것과 달리, Hermes는 (1) delegate_task 격리 서브에이전트, (2) ACP(Agent Client Protocol) 어댑터로 에디터/외부 에이전트와 통합, (3) 멀티-프로토콜 모델 어댑터로 이종(heterogeneous) 백엔드에 위임, (4) Kanban 멀티에이전트 보드, (5) Curator 자기개선 메타-에이전트라는 다섯 갈래의 협업 메커니즘을 별도의 코드 경로로 갖는다.

핵심 차별점 세 가지를 먼저 짚는다:

  1. flat depth-1 격리 위임: delegate_task(tools/delegate_tool.py)는 자식 AIAgent를 부모 히스토리 없이, 차단 도구를 제거한 토큰셋으로, 자체 task_id(=자체 터미널 세션) 로 띄운다. 기본 깊이는 MAX_DEPTH = 1(부모 0 → 자식 1)이며 손자는 거부된다.
  2. ACP 표준 채택: Hermes는 ACP 서버(acp_adapter/)로 자신을 VS Code/Zed/JetBrains에 노출하는 동시에, ACP 클라이언트(agent/copilot_acp_client.py)로 GitHub Copilot 같은 외부 ACP 에이전트를 백엔드처럼 호출한다 — 즉 에이전트-투-에이전트 양방향이 표준 프로토콜로 구현된다.
  3. 이종 위임 + 메타-에이전트: 위임 자식은 부모와 다른 provider/model(예: 더 싸고 빠른 OpenRouter 모델)로 라우팅될 수 있고(delegation.provider), Curator(agent/curator.py)는 보조 모델로 forked AIAgent를 띄워 스킬 라이브러리를 주기적으로 정리하는 백그라운드 메타-에이전트다.

신뢰도: ■■■■■ (매우 높음) — 모든 분석은 실제 Python 소스에서 확인했다. 핵심 파일: [[tools/delegate_tool.py]](2,802 lines), [[agent/curator.py]](1,801 lines), [[acp_adapter/server.py]](1,952 lines), [[acp_adapter/session.py]], [[agent/copilot_acp_client.py]], [[acp_registry/agent.json]].

graph TD
    subgraph Parent["부모 AIAgent (run_agent.py, depth=0)"]
        Loop["run_conversation 루프"]
        DT["delegate_task 호출"]
    end

    subgraph Delegate["delegate_task (tools/delegate_tool.py)"]
        Build["_build_child_agent<br/>(메인 스레드 구성)"]
        Strip["DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS 제거<br/>+ 토큰셋 부모 교집합"]
        Pool["ThreadPoolExecutor<br/>(max_concurrent=3)"]
        Run["_run_single_child<br/>(자체 task_id + 터미널 세션)"]
    end

    subgraph Child["자식 AIAgent (depth=1, leaf)"]
        CLoop["run_conversation"]
        CTerm["자체 터미널 백엔드<br/>(_resolve_container_task_id)"]
    end

    subgraph Hetero["이종 백엔드 위임"]
        Anth["anthropic_adapter"]
        Codex["codex_responses_adapter"]
        Gem["gemini_*_adapter"]
        Bed["bedrock_adapter / azure"]
        CopilotACP["copilot_acp_client (ACP 아웃바운드)"]
    end

    subgraph External["외부 협업 표준"]
        ACPsrv["acp_adapter (ACP 서버)<br/>VS Code / Zed / JetBrains"]
        Kanban["plugins/kanban (멀티에이전트 보드)"]
    end

    subgraph Meta["메타-에이전트"]
        Curator["agent/curator.py<br/>maybe_run_curator → forked AIAgent"]
    end

    Loop --> DT --> Build --> Strip --> Pool --> Run --> CLoop
    CLoop --> CTerm
    Run -->|"부모와 다른 provider/model"| Hetero
    CopilotACP -.->|"외부 ACP 에이전트 호출"| External
    Run -->|"요약 결과만 반환"| Loop
    Curator -.->|"보조 모델, 메인 캐시 미접촉"| Meta
    DT -.->|"느슨한 결합"| Kanban

1. delegate_task — 격리 서브에이전트 아키텍처

tools/delegate_tool.py의 모듈 docstring이 설계를 명확히 한다:

"Spawns child AIAgent instances with isolated context, restricted toolsets, and their own terminal sessions. Supports single-task and batch (parallel) modes. The parent blocks until all children complete."

각 자식이 받는 것(docstring):

  • 새 대화 (부모 히스토리 없음)
  • 자체 task_id (자체 터미널 세션, 파일 ops 캐시)
  • 제한된 토큰셋 (차단 도구는 항상 제거)
  • 집중된 시스템 프롬프트 (위임 목표 + 컨텍스트로 구성)

"The parent's context only sees the delegation call and the summary result, never the child's intermediate tool calls or reasoning."

이는 Claude Code의 명시적 서브에이전트("zero context, brief like a smart colleague")와 동형이다. Hermes는 fork(부모 컨텍스트 상속) 변형이 없고 항상 깨끗한 컨텍스트로 시작한다.

1.1 차단 도구 (DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS)

tools/delegate_tool.py line 45–53에서 자식이 절대 가질 수 없는 도구를 정의한다:

DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS = frozenset([
    "delegate_task",  # 재귀 위임 금지
    "clarify",        # 사용자 상호작용 금지
    "memory",         # 공유 MEMORY.md 쓰기 금지
    "send_message",   # 크로스플랫폼 부작용 금지
    "execute_code",   # 자식은 스크립트 작성 대신 단계별 추론을 해야 함
])

이 차단은 두 겹으로 강제된다. _strip_blocked_tools()(line 672)는 이 도구만 들어있는 토큰셋(delegation, clarify, memory, code_execution)을 자식 토큰셋에서 통째로 제거한다. 스키마 description(_build_top_level_description, line 2574)도 모델에게 "Leaf subagents (role='leaf', the default) CANNOT call: delegate_task, clarify, memory, send_message, execute_code." 라고 명시한다.

각 차단의 근거가 코드 주석에 그대로 적혀 있다 — clarify 금지는 자식이 사용자 상호작용을 못 하기 때문, memory 금지는 공유 MEMORY.md에 쓰지 못하게 하기 위함, send_message 금지는 크로스플랫폼 부작용 차단, execute_code 금지는 "자식은 스크립트를 쓰지 말고 단계별로 추론해야 한다"는 설계 의도다.

1.2 깊이 제한 — MAX_DEPTH = 1과 orchestrator role

# tools/delegate_tool.py line 133-137
MAX_DEPTH = 1  # flat by default: parent (0) -> child (1); grandchild rejected
_MIN_SPAWN_DEPTH = 1
_MAX_SPAWN_DEPTH_CAP = 3

기본은 flat이다 — 부모(depth 0)가 자식(depth 1)을 띄우고, depth-1 자식은 더 이상 위임할 수 없다. delegate_task()(line 1960)는 호출 즉시 깊이를 검사한다:

depth = getattr(parent_agent, "_delegate_depth", 0)
max_spawn = _get_max_spawn_depth()
if depth >= max_spawn:
    return json.dumps({"error": "Delegation depth limit reached ..."})

다만 두 가지 옵트인 확장이 있다:

메커니즘 동작 신뢰도
delegation.max_spawn_depth [1,3]로 클램핑(_get_max_spawn_depth, line 394). 2 이상이면 중첩 위임 활성 ■■■■■
role="orchestrator" 자식이 delegation 토큰셋을 유지해 자기 워커를 spawn 가능 (_build_child_agent line 967) ■■■■■
delegation.orchestrator_enabled 글로벌 kill switch — False면 orchestrator가 조용히 leaf로 강등(_get_orchestrator_enabled, line 432) ■■■■■

role은 두 곳에서만 leaf로 강등된다(line 904–913): kill switch가 꺼졌거나 자식 깊이가 max_spawn 이상일 때. orchestrator 자식의 시스템 프롬프트(_build_child_system_prompt, line 569)에는 OpenClaw의 buildSubagentSystemPrompt를 모델로 한 "Subagent Spawning (Orchestrator Role)" 블록이 붙어, 언제 위임하고(2+ 독립 서브태스크) 언제 안 하는지(단일 기계적 작업, 전체 목표 pass-through 금지)를 지시한다. 깊이 노트는 "literal truth (grounded in the passed config) so the LLM doesn't confabulate" — 즉 모델이 존재하지 않는 중첩 능력을 지어내지 않도록 실제 config 값에 기반해 작성된다.

1.3 자체 터미널 백엔드 — task_id 격리

각 자식이 "자체 터미널 세션"을 갖는 메커니즘은 task_id다. _run_single_child(line 1476–1510)에서:

# 안정적 subagent_id를 자식의 task_id로 재사용
child_task_id = _subagent_id or f"subagent-{task_index}-{_uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
...
return child.run_conversation(user_message=goal, task_id=child_task_id)

이 task_id가 터미널 백엔드의 샌드박스/컨테이너 키가 된다. tools/terminal_tool.py의 _resolve_container_task_id(task_id)(line 975)는 "Map a tool-call task_id to the container/sandbox key used by..." — 최상위 에이전트는 task_id=None으로 "default"에 떨어지지만, 각 서브에이전트는 자기 고유 task_id를 전달하므로 별도의 터미널 작업 디렉터리와 상태(샌드박스, 파일 ops 캐시)를 얻는다. 스키마 description도 "Each subagent gets its own terminal session (separate working directory and state)." 라고 명시한다(line 2582).

§00 개요에서 설명한 tools/environments/(local/docker/ssh/modal/daytona/singularity) 터미널 백엔드 추상화와 결합되면, 서브에이전트는 부모와 동일한 백엔드 종류를 쓰되 독립된 task_id 키로 격리된 인스턴스를 받는 구조다.

또한 cross-agent 파일 상태 추적(tools/file_state.py)이 있어, 자식이 부모가 이미 읽은 파일을 수정하면 결과 요약에 경고를 덧붙인다(line 1722–1754):

[NOTE: subagent modified files the parent previously read — re-read before editing: ...]

1.4 에이전트 루프의 위임 처리와 결과 반환

부모의 동기 루프(§00의 run_conversation)는 handle_function_call로 delegate_task를 호출하고, 모든 자식이 완료될 때까지 블록한다. 두 실행 모드가 있다(delegate_task, line 2091):

  • 단일 task (n_tasks == 1): 스레드 풀 없이 직접 _run_single_child 실행.
  • batch (병렬): ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_children)로 동시 실행(_get_max_concurrent_children, 기본 3, line 132). _cf_wait(..., timeout=0.5, FIRST_COMPLETED)로 폴링하며 매 사이클 부모 인터럽트를 체크해, 자식이 멈춰도 부모가 영원히 블록되지 않는다(line 2123–2165).

스레드-안전 구성이 핵심 트릭이다: 모든 자식 AIAgent는 먼저 메인 스레드에서 _build_child_agent로 구성되고(line 2055–2086), 그 다음에 워커 스레드에서 실행된다. 이유는 AIAgent() 생성이 model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names라는 프로세스 전역을 덮어쓰기 때문 — 부모의 도구 이름을 먼저 저장하고(line 2049), 모든 자식 구성 후 복원한다(line 2089).

자식 워커 스레드에는 비대화형 승인 콜백이 주입된다(line 1492–1500). 워커는 부모의 prompt_toolkit TUI가 소유한 stdin에 접근할 수 없으므로, 위험 명령에 대해 input()으로 폴백하면 데드락이 난다. 따라서 delegation.subagent_auto_approve config에 따라 _subagent_auto_deny(기본, 안전) 또는 _subagent_auto_approve(opt-in YOLO) 콜백을 ThreadPoolExecutor(initializer=...)로 설치한다.

결과 반환 형태 — _run_single_child가 만드는 entry dict(line 1684):

{task_index, status, summary, api_calls, duration_seconds, model,
 exit_reason, tokens:{input,output}, tool_trace, _child_role, _child_cost_usd}

status는 completed / failed / interrupted / timeout / error. exit_reason은 completed / max_iterations / interrupted로 부모에게 어떻게 끝났는지 알린다. 부모로 돌아가기 전에 _child_role/_child_cost_usd는 dict에서 pop되고(line 2257–2259), 자식 비용은 부모 세션 총비용에 합산된다(line 2286–2299, Kilo-Org/kilocode#9448 포팅). 최종 반환은 {"results": [...], "total_duration_seconds": ...} JSON이다(line 2303).

1.5 타임아웃·heartbeat·인터럽트

  • 하드 타임아웃: 각 자식은 별도 ThreadPoolExecutor에서 실행되며 _get_child_timeout()(기본 600초, line 367)으로 캡된다.
  • heartbeat: _run_single_child는 30초마다(_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL) 자식 활동을 부모의 _touch_activity로 전파해, 게이트웨이의 무활동 타임아웃이 위임 중에 발화하지 않게 한다(line 1369). 자식이 진전이 없으면(idle 15사이클=450초 / in-tool 40사이클=1200초) stale로 판정하고 heartbeat를 멈춘다.
  • 0-API-call 진단: 자식이 첫 LLM 요청 도달 전에 타임아웃하면 _dump_subagent_timeout_diagnostic(line 1177)이 워커 스레드 스택·config·프롬프트 크기를 ~/.hermes/logs/subagent-timeout-*.log에 덤프한다(issue #14726).
  • 인터럽트 전파: interrupt_subagent(subagent_id)(line 188)는 모듈 전역 _active_subagents 레지스트리에서 자식을 찾아 agent.interrupt()를 호출하며, 이는 손자까지 재귀한다. Python은 워커 스레드를 하드킬할 수 없으므로 다음 iteration 경계에서 멈추는 협력적 인터럽트다.

1.6 관찰성 — 진행 콜백과 라이브 트리

_build_child_progress_callback(line 683)은 자식 도구 호출을 부모 디스플레이로 중계한다. 두 경로: CLI는 스피너 위에 트리뷰 라인(├─ 🔀 ...)을 출력하고, 게이트웨이는 도구 이름을 5개씩 배치해 부모 progress 콜백으로 전달한다. subagent_id/parent_id/depth/model 정체성 kwarg가 모든 이벤트에 실려, TUI가 라이브 spawn 트리를 재구성하고 브랜치별 제어(kill/pause)를 subagent_id로 라우팅한다. 모듈 전역 _active_subagents 레지스트리(line 152)와 set_spawn_paused/is_spawn_paused kill switch(line 158)는 게이트웨이 RPC(delegation.pause, delegation.status, subagent.interrupt)가 소비한다.

DelegateEvent enum(line 532)이 진행 이벤트 타입(task_spawned/task_progress/task_thinking/tool_started/tool_completed)을 정형화하고, _LEGACY_EVENT_MAP(line 555)이 구식 문자열(tool.started 등)을 정규화한다. _extract_output_tail(line 224)은 자식 대화에서 마지막 N개 도구 결과를 뽑아 TUI 오버레이의 "Output" 섹션(cc-swarm-parity 기능)에 공급한다.

1.8 완료 후 처리 — 훅과 메모리 통지

모든 자식이 끝나면 부모 스레드에서 직렬로 후처리가 일어난다(line 2240–2299). 각 자식당 한 번씩 subagent_stop 훅이 발화하는데(line 2268), 이는 워커 스레드가 아닌 부모 스레드에서 호출되어 훅 작성자가 동시성을 신경 쓰지 않아도 되게 한다(§00의 선언적 셸 훅 subagent_stop 이벤트와 연결). 훅에는 parent_session_id/child_role/child_summary/child_status/duration_ms가 전달된다.

또한 부모의 메모리 프로바이더에 위임 결과가 통지된다 — parent_agent._memory_manager.on_delegation(task, result, child_session_id)(line 2228). 이로써 외부 메모리 프로바이더(§00의 honcho/mem0 등)가 위임 작업의 목표와 요약을 학습할 수 있다. 자식 세션은 parent_session_id로 부모 세션에 체인되어(_build_child_agent line 1129) SessionDB에 영속되므로, 위임 트리가 사후에 session_search로 추적 가능하다.

1.7 위임 도구 스키마와 동적 설명

DELEGATE_TASK_SCHEMA(line 2660)는 goal/context/toolsets/tasks(batch)/role/acp_command/acp_args 파라미터를 정의한다. description 필드는 placeholder이며 매 get_definitions() 호출마다 _build_dynamic_schema_overrides(line 2638)가 사용자의 실제 한도(max_concurrent_children, max_spawn_depth, orchestrator on/off)를 반영해 재생성한다. 이는 모델이 프레임워크 기본값(3/1)에 자기제한하지 않도록 하기 위함이다(line 2489 주석). 모델 제공 max_iterations는 무시되고 config 값이 권위적이다(line 1982).

스키마 description의 주목할 가이드(line 2552–2573):

  • durable 작업 금지: "delegate_task runs SYNCHRONOUSLY inside the parent turn ... Children cannot continue in the background." → 턴을 넘어 살아남아야 하는 작업은 cronjob 또는 terminal(background=True)을 쓰라고 지시.
  • 언어 오염 방지: 사용자가 비영어로 쓰면 context에 명시하라 — 안 하면 자식이 영어로 응답해 부모 최종 답을 오염시킨다.
  • 자기보고 검증: "Subagent summaries are SELF-REPORTS, not verified facts." — 외부 부작용(HTTP POST, 원격 쓰기, 파일 생성)이 있는 작업은 자식이 검증 가능한 핸들(URL/ID/절대경로/HTTP status)을 반환하게 하고 부모가 직접 검증하라.

등록은 tools/registry.py의 registry.register(name="delegate_task", toolset="delegation", ...)(line 2783)로 셀프-등록되며, check_fn은 항상 True(외부 의존성 없음, line 564)다.

2. 역할 특화 (role specialization)

Hermes의 위임은 Claude Code의 내장 에이전트 유형(Explore/Plan/general-purpose)처럼 사전 정의된 역할군이 아니라 두 개의 평면적 역할만 갖는다(_normalize_role, line 312):

role delegate_task 보유 추가 위임 용도
leaf (기본) ❌ 불가 집중 워커 — 한 작업을 깨끗한 컨텍스트로 수행
orchestrator ✅ 가능 (depth/kill-switch 한도 내) 목표를 분해해 자기 워커를 병렬로 spawn하고 결과 종합

역할 특화의 실체는 토큰셋이다. toolsets 파라미터로 자식에게 ['terminal','file'](코드), ['web'](리서치), ['browser'](웹 상호작용) 같은 도구 집합을 줄 수 있고, 부모가 가진 도구의 교집합으로 좁혀진다(_build_child_agent line 945–961, "subagent must not gain tools the parent lacks"). MCP 토큰셋은 inherit_mcp_toolsets(기본 True)에 따라 보존된다(_preserve_parent_mcp_toolsets, line 501). 즉 "역할"은 정적 카탈로그가 아니라 호출 시점에 동적으로 합성되는 권한 좁히기다.

토큰셋 좁히기의 정확한 알고리즘(line 926–968):

  1. 부모의 유효 토큰셋을 도출한다. enabled_toolsets가 명시돼 있으면 그대로, None(= 모든 도구 활성)이면 valid_tool_names에서 model_tools.get_toolset_for_tool()로 역산한다 — 부모가 비활성화한 도구(예: web)가 자식에 새지 않게 하기 위함.
  2. 자식이 toolsets를 요청하면 _expand_parent_toolsets(line 470)로 부모의 composite 토큰셋(예: hermes-cli)을 개별 토큰셋 이름으로 확장한 뒤 교집합한다 — "web" != "hermes-cli" 같은 단순 이름 비교로 거부되는 것을 막는다.
  3. _get_inherit_mcp_toolsets()가 True면 부모 MCP 토큰셋을 보존 append.
  4. _strip_blocked_tools(line 672)로 차단 도구만 든 토큰셋 제거.
  5. effective_role == "orchestrator"면 4단계에서 제거된 delegation 토큰셋을 무조건 재추가한다(line 967) — orchestrator 능력은 부모 토큰셋 멤버십이 아니라 role로 부여되기 때문(test_intersection_preserves_delegation_bound 테스트의 설계 근거).

자식이 모델에게 보이는 가용 토큰셋 힌트 문자열(_TOOLSET_LIST_STR, line 130)은 debugging/safe/delegation/moa/rl 및 hermes-* composite·전부-차단 토큰셋을 제외하고 구성된다(line 122–129).

더 풍부한 역할 특화는 delegate_task 밖의 Kanban(§4)에 있다 — kanban_swarm.py의 워커는 profile(프로파일=역할)과 skills 리스트를 받는다.

3. ACP 어댑터 / 레지스트리 — 에이전트-투-에이전트 표준

ACP(Agent Client Protocol)는 Hermes가 cli-jaw 대비 갖는 가장 큰 구조적 차별점이다. 양방향으로 구현된다.

3.1 인바운드 — Hermes를 ACP 서버로 노출

acp_adapter/는 Hermes를 ACP stdio JSON-RPC 서버로 만들어 VS Code/Zed/JetBrains 에디터에 통합한다. 모듈 구성:

파일 역할 lines
acp_adapter/entry.py CLI 진입점 — .env 로드, 로깅을 stderr로(stdout은 ACP 전송 전용), MCP 발견 후 서버 기동 266
acp_adapter/server.py HermesACPAgent — ACP 메서드 라우터 1,952
acp_adapter/session.py ACP 세션 ↔ AIAgent 매핑, SessionDB 영속 628
acp_adapter/tools.py 도구 브리지 (Hermes 도구 ↔ ACP) 1,291
acp_adapter/permissions.py / edit_approval.py 승인 168 / 286
acp_adapter/events.py / auth.py 이벤트 / 인증 279 / 79

entry.py의 main()은 asyncio.run(acp.run_agent(agent, use_unstable_protocol=True))로 서버를 돌린다(line 257). liveness 프로브(ping/health/healthcheck)는 ACP 스키마 밖이라 JSON-RPC -32601을 반환하되, _BenignProbeMethodFilter(line 46)가 stderr 트레이스백 노이즈를 억제한다 — acp-bridge 같은 클라이언트가 -32601을 "agent alive"로 취급하기 때문이다.

session.py docstring이 보여주듯, ACP 세션은 공유 SessionDB(~/.hermes/state.db)에 영속되어 프로세스 재시작을 견디고 session_search에 나타난다. 에디터가 idle/restart 후 재연결하면 load_session/resume_session이 DB에서 세션을 찾아 전체 대화를 복원한다. WSL 경로 번역(_win_path_to_wsl, _translate_acp_cwd, line 29–50)도 있어 Windows 에디터가 WSL Hermes를 띄우는 시나리오를 처리한다.

HermesACPAgent(server.py line 516)가 노출하는 ACP 메서드 표면은 풍부하다 — initialize(821) / authenticate(855) / new_session(1069) / load_session(1086) / resume_session(1130) / fork_session(1176) / list_sessions(1196) / prompt(1243) / cancel(1162) / set_session_model(1882) / set_session_mode(1916) / set_config_option(1932). 즉 에디터(클라이언트)가 세션 생성·재개·fork·취소·모델 전환·모드 변경을 모두 ACP RPC로 제어한다. 흥미롭게도 §00의 슬래시 명령 단일 레지스트리가 여기서도 재사용되어, ACP 세션 안에서 /help·/model·/tools·/context·/reset·/compact·/steer·/queue·/version이 _handle_slash_command(line 1629)로 처리된다 — 즉 CLI/게이트웨이/ACP가 같은 명령 표면을 공유한다. _replay_session_history(979)는 재개 시 대화를 에디터로 다시 흘려보낸다.

승인 브리지: Hermes의 위험 명령 승인은 ACP request_permission으로 표면화된다. acp_adapter/permissions.py의 _OPTION_ID_TO_HERMES(line 21)가 ACP 옵션 id(allow_once/allow_session/allow_always/deny/deny_always)를 Hermes 승인 결과 문자열(once/session/always/deny)로 매핑한다. acp_adapter/edit_approval.py(286 lines)는 파일 편집 승인 정책을 별도로 다룬다(_edit_approval_policy_for_state, server.py line 562). 즉 §00에서 본 채널별 네이티브 승인 UI 패턴이 에디터에서도 ACP 프로토콜의 permission 프리미티브로 일관되게 구현된다.

3.2 레지스트리 — agent.json

acp_registry/agent.json이 ACP 에이전트 레지스트리 메타데이터를 담는다:

{
  "id": "hermes-agent",
  "name": "Hermes Agent",
  "version": "0.15.1",
  "distribution": {
    "uvx": { "package": "hermes-agent[acp]==0.15.1", "args": ["hermes-acp"] }
  }
}

uvx hermes-agent[acp]==0.15.1로 배포되어 hermes-acp 명령으로 실행된다. 이 표준 메타데이터 덕분에 ACP 호환 에디터/오케스트레이터가 Hermes를 자동 발견하고 띄울 수 있다.

3.3 아웃바운드 — 외부 ACP 에이전트를 백엔드처럼 호출

반대 방향이 더 흥미롭다. agent/copilot_acp_client.py는 OpenAI 호환 shim으로, Hermes 요청을 copilot --acp 서브프로세스로 포워딩한다. docstring:

"This adapter lets Hermes treat the GitHub Copilot ACP server as a chat-style backend. Each request starts a short-lived ACP session, sends the formatted conversation as a single prompt, collects text chunks, and converts the result back into the minimal shape Hermes expects from an OpenAI client."

이것이 delegate_task와 직접 연결된다. delegate_task 스키마는 acp_command/acp_args 파라미터를 받아(DELEGATE_TASK_SCHEMA line 2752), non-ACP 부모가 ACP 자식 에이전트를 spawn할 수 있다. _build_child_agent(line 1054–1058)에서 override_acp_command가 설정되면 provider를 copilot-acp로 강제하고 CopilotACPClient를 초기화한다. 즉 Hermes 서브에이전트가 GitHub Copilot CLI라는 다른 벤더의 에이전트가 되어 작업을 수행하고 요약만 부모로 반환하는, 표준 프로토콜 기반의 진정한 에이전트-투-에이전트 위임이다.

스키마는 이 기능을 보수적으로 게이트한다 — "Do NOT set this unless the user has explicitly told you a specific ACP-compatible CLI is installed and configured."(line 2760). agent/copilot_acp_client.py는 deprecated gh copilot 확장과 새 @github/copilot CLI를 stderr 핑거프린트로 구분하는 방어 코드(line 36–48)까지 갖췄다.

4. 멀티-프로토콜 모델 어댑터 — 이종 위임의 한 형태

§00에서 본 와이어 프로토콜 어댑터들은 그 자체로 "이종 백엔드에 대한 위임"의 한 형태다. Hermes는 다음 어댑터를 코어에 둔다:

어댑터 파일 프로토콜
agent/anthropic_adapter.py Anthropic Messages
agent/codex_responses_adapter.py OpenAI Codex Responses
agent/gemini_native_adapter.py / gemini_cloudcode_adapter.py Gemini
agent/bedrock_adapter.py AWS Bedrock
agent/azure_identity_adapter.py Azure
agent/copilot_acp_client.py ACP (Copilot)

delegate_task는 이를 위임 단위로 활용한다. _resolve_delegation_credentials(line 2345)는 delegation.provider 또는 delegation.base_url이 설정되면 자식을 부모와 완전히 다른 provider:model 쌍으로 라우팅한다 — "This lets subagents run on a completely different provider:model pair (e.g. cheap/fast model on OpenRouter while the parent runs on Nous Portal)."

핵심 버그-방어 디테일: api_mode는 자식이 부모와 다른 provider를 쓸 때 상속되지 않는다(line 1025–1036, Bug #20558/PR #20563). MiniMax는 anthropic_messages, DeepSeek은 chat_completions를 쓰므로 부모 모드를 상속하면 404가 난다. 따라서 provider가 다르면 api_mode=None으로 강제 재유도하거나, URL 휴리스틱(_detect_api_mode_for_url, /anthropic 접미사 → anthropic_messages)으로 자동 감지한다(line 2387–2408).

또한 자식은 부모의 fallback chain(_fallback_chain, line 1083)과 credential pool(_resolve_child_credential_pool, line 2312)을 상속/공유해 rate-limit과 자격증명 고갈을 부모처럼 복구한다. 같은 provider면 부모 pool을 공유(쿨다운/로테이션 동기화), 다른 provider면 그 provider의 pool을 로드한다.

5. 느슨한 결합 협업 — Kanban 멀티에이전트 보드

delegate_task가 동기적·턴-국한 위임이라면, plugins/kanban/은 턴을 넘어 살아남는 느슨한 결합 협업이다. tools/kanban_tools.py docstring이 차이를 설명한다 — 칸반 도구는 HERMES_KANBAN_TASK 환경변수가 설정된 dispatcher-spawned 워커이거나, 프로파일이 kanban 토큰셋을 명시적으로 켰을 때만 모델 스키마에 등록된다(일반 hermes chat은 칸반 도구를 0개 본다).

도구가 셸 호출 대신 Python 도구로 구현된 이유(docstring):

  1. 백엔드 이식성 — Docker/Modal/SSH 터미널 백엔드를 쓰는 워커는 컨테이너 안에서 hermes kanban을 실행할 수 없다(hermes 미설치). 도구는 에이전트 Python 프로세스에서 실행되므로 항상 ~/.hermes/kanban.db에 도달한다.
  2. 셸 쿼팅 footgun 없음, 3. 구조화된 에러.

hermes_cli/kanban_swarm.py의 swarm 토폴로지는 별도 스케줄러를 도입하지 않고 기존 Kanban 커널에 작은 task 그래프를 쓴다:

planning root (즉시 완료)
    ├─ parallel specialist workers (ready)
    └─ verifier (모든 워커 완료까지 todo)
         └─ synthesizer (verifier 완료까지 todo)

공유 blackboard도 의도적으로 low-tech다 — root task의 구조화된 JSON 코멘트([swarm:blackboard] 접두). 모든 상태가 기존 task_comments/task_events 행에 들어가 대시보드·notifier·슬래시 명령·dispatcher가 새 서비스 없이 계속 동작한다. SwarmWorkerSpec(profile/title/body/skills/priority/max_runtime_seconds)이 워커 역할 특화를 담는다. 칸반 도구군은 kanban_*(show/list/complete/block/heartbeat/comment/create/link/unblock)로 워커가 보드를 갱신한다.

6. Curator — 자기개선 메타-에이전트

agent/curator.py(1,801 lines)의 Curator는 에이전트가 만든 스킬을 주기적으로 정리하는 백그라운드 메타-에이전트다. 모듈 docstring의 strict invariants:

  • Only touches agent-created skills (see tools/skill_usage.is_agent_created)
  • Never auto-deletes — only archives. Archive is recoverable.
  • Pinned skills bypass all auto-transitions
  • Uses the auxiliary client; never touches the main session's prompt cache

6.1 비활성 기반 트리거 (no cron daemon)

maybe_run_curator()(line 1782)가 공개 진입점으로, 세션-시작 훅에서 호출된다. 게이트(should_run_now, line 199):

  • curator.enabled == True (기본 ON)
  • not paused
  • last_run_at이 interval_hours(기본 7일=168시간) 이전

최초 실행 거동: last_run_at이 없으면(fresh install) 즉시 실행하지 않고 "now"로 시드해 한 인터벌을 미룬다 — hermes update 직후 첫 게이트웨이 tick에서 라이브러리를 자동 변형하지 않기 위함이다. 추가로 호출 측에서 min_idle_hours(기본 2시간) idle 게이트를 적용한다(line 1793).

6.2 2단계 리뷰 — pure 전이 + LLM 패스

run_curator_review(line 1388)는 두 단계로 동작한다:

  1. 자동 상태 전이 (pure, no LLM) — apply_automatic_transitions(line 256)가 스킬 활동 타임스탬프 기반으로 active/stale/archived를 전이한다. stale_after_days(30) 경과 → stale, archive_after_days(90) 경과 → archive, 다시 쓰이면 reactivate. pinned 스킬은 건너뛴다.

  2. LLM 통합 패스 — _run_llm_review(line 1641)가 forked AIAgent 를 띄워 CURATOR_REVIEW_PROMPT(line 330)를 실행한다. 이 프롬프트는 "umbrella-building consolidation"을 지시한다 — 수백 개의 좁은 스킬을 PREFIX CLUSTER로 묶어 class-level umbrella 스킬로 통합(MERGE / CREATE / DEMOTE to references/templates/scripts)하고 사이블링을 archive한다. "If you end the pass with fewer than 10 archives, you stopped too early."

6.3 메인 캐시 미접촉 + 보조 모델

리뷰 fork는 메인 세션 prompt 캐시를 절대 건드리지 않는다(line 1727–1729: _memory_nudge_interval = 0, _skill_nudge_interval = 0로 재귀 nudge 비활성). provider/model은 auxiliary.curator.{provider,model}(보조 task 슬롯)에서 해석되며(_resolve_review_runtime, line 1576), provider: "auto"면 메인 모델로 폴백한다 — §00에서 본 task별 auxiliary 모델 핀과 동일한 plumbing이다. fork는 max_iterations=9999(수백 스킬 스윕은 50–100 API 호출 소요), quiet_mode=True, platform="curator", skip_memory=True로 구성되고, stdout/stderr는 /dev/null로 리다이렉트되며(line 1736) 종료 시 close()된다.

6.4 dry-run과 보고

dry-run 모드(line 1412)는 자동 전이를 건너뛰고 CURATOR_DRY_RUN_BANNER(line 303)로 LLM에게 "REPORT ONLY — DO NOT MUTATE"를 지시한다. 실제 mutate 없이 REPORT.md만 생성해 사용자가 무엇을 할 뻔했는지 검토하게 한다. 라이브 실행은 ~/.hermes/logs/curator/{타임스탬프}/run.json + REPORT.md를 쓰고(line 989), archive된 스킬을 consolidated(umbrella로 흡수) vs pruned(단순 폐기)로 분류한다. 분류는 세 신호를 화해시킨다(_reconcile_classification, line 768): (1) 모델이 skill_manage(delete) 호출 시 선언한 absorbed_into가 권위적, (2) 모델의 구조화 YAML 블록, (3) 도구 호출 휴리스틱 감사. 스킬 통합 시 이를 참조하던 cron job의 스킬 레퍼런스도 자동 재작성한다(cron.jobs.rewrite_skill_refs, line 1105).

이것이 "self-improving"의 실체다 — 에이전트가 skill_manage로 스스로 스킬을 만들고, Curator라는 별도 메타-에이전트가 보조 모델로 그것을 주기적으로 통합·아카이브한다.

7. 협업 메커니즘 요약 비교

메커니즘 결합도 수명 컨텍스트 백엔드 소스
delegate_task (leaf) 강결합·동기 부모 턴 국한 격리(부모 히스토리 없음) 부모 또는 override provider tools/delegate_tool.py
delegate_task (orchestrator) 강결합·동기·중첩 부모 턴 국한 격리 + 자기 워커 〃 〃 line 611
ACP 아웃바운드 (copilot) 프로토콜 결합 단발 세션 격리 외부 ACP 에이전트 agent/copilot_acp_client.py
ACP 인바운드 (서버) 프로토콜 결합 영속(SessionDB) 에디터 세션 Hermes 자신 acp_adapter/
Kanban swarm 느슨한 결합·비동기 턴/세션 생존 보드 공유(blackboard) dispatcher 워커 plugins/kanban/, kanban_swarm.py
Curator 메타·백그라운드 인터벌 트리거 forked, 메인 캐시 미접촉 보조(auxiliary) 모델 agent/curator.py

8. cli-jaw 비교 시사점

  • (a) delegate_task(depth-1, 자체 백엔드) vs cli-jaw 멀티-CLI employee 디스패치. Hermes의 delegate_task는 자식 AIAgent를 같은 프로세스 안에서 스레드 풀로 띄우되, 자체 task_id로 터미널 백엔드를 격리하고 DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS(delegate_task/clarify/memory/send_message/execute_code)를 항상 제거하며 MAX_DEPTH=1로 평면을 유지한다 — 컨텍스트 격리·권한 좁히기·깊이 제한이 한 함수에 코드로 박혀 있다. cli-jaw는 별도 CLI/직원(employee) 프로세스를 dispatch하는 하니스-레벨 모델에 가깝다 — Hermes처럼 "차단 도구 set + 토큰셋 부모 교집합 + role 강등 + 깊이 클램프"를 코어가 강제하는 격리 경계는 cli-jaw에 명시적으로 존재하지 않거나 하니스 권한 프롬프트로 대체된다. cli-jaw가 참고할 점은 Hermes의 자식 비용 롤업(_child_cost_usd 합산)과 stale-heartbeat 진단(0-API-call 덤프)처럼 위임의 관찰성·회계를 코어에 내장한 부분이다.

  • (b) ACP는 cli-jaw에 없는 표준. Hermes는 ACP(Agent Client Protocol)를 양방향으로 구현한다 — acp_adapter/로 자신을 에디터(VS Code/Zed/JetBrains)에 ACP 서버로 노출하고(acp_registry/agent.json로 uvx 배포), 동시에 agent/copilot_acp_client.py로 GitHub Copilot 같은 외부 ACP 에이전트를 OpenAI 호환 백엔드처럼 호출한다. delegate_task의 acp_command/acp_args 파라미터로 non-ACP 부모가 ACP 자식(다른 벤더 에이전트)을 spawn하는 진정한 에이전트-투-에이전트 위임이 가능하다. cli-jaw는 이런 표준 프로토콜 통합 계층이 없어 에디터 통합·외부 에이전트 호출이 ad-hoc하다 — ACP 채택은 cli-jaw가 에디터/외부 에이전트 생태계와 상호운용하려 할 때의 직접적 참고점이다.

  • (c) 이종 위임 — Hermes가 더 성숙. Hermes는 위임 자식을 부모와 다른 provider:model(예: 부모는 Nous Portal, 자식은 OpenRouter의 싸고 빠른 모델)로 라우팅하고(delegation.provider/base_url), api_mode를 provider별로 재유도하며(MiniMax=anthropic_messages, DeepSeek=chat_completions), fallback chain·credential pool까지 상속/공유한다. anthropic/codex/gemini/bedrock/azure/ACP 와이어 어댑터가 코어에 있어 위임이 곧 이종 백엔드 분산이 된다. cli-jaw는 Claude(Anthropic) 단일 provider 결합도가 높아, 비용 최적화를 위한 "부모는 강모델·자식은 약모델" 분리 위임 패턴을 쓰려면 Hermes의 어댑터/credential-pool 분리가 청사진이다.

  • (d) 메타-에이전트 자기개선 루프 — Hermes 고유. agent/curator.py의 Curator는 비활성 트리거(no cron daemon)로 forked AIAgent를 띄워, 보조 모델로 에이전트 생성 스킬을 umbrella-ification(consolidate/archive/patch)한다 — 절대 자동삭제 안 함(archive만, 복구 가능), pinned 우회, 메인 세션 prompt 캐시 미접촉, dry-run 미리보기, consolidated/pruned 분류 + cron 스킬 레퍼런스 자동 재작성까지 갖춘 완결된 백그라운드 메타-에이전트다. cli-jaw에는 스킬 생태계와 스케줄러(/loop, Cron* 도구)는 있으나, "에이전트가 만든 스킬을 별도 보조 모델로 자율 유지보수하는 메타-에이전트"에 해당하는 코어 메커니즘은 보이지 않는다 — 이는 Hermes가 cli-jaw에 줄 수 있는 가장 차별적인 협업 패턴이다.

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

변경 기록

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent(NousResearch/hermes-agent, v0.15.1) 서브에이전트/협업 차원 최초 작성. delegate_task 격리 서브에이전트(DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS, MAX_DEPTH=1, task_id 기반 자체 터미널 세션, 메인-스레드 구성+워커 실행, batch 병렬 ThreadPoolExecutor, heartbeat/타임아웃/인터럽트, 자식 비용 롤업, 동적 스키마), leaf/orchestrator 역할 특화, ACP 양방향(acp_adapter 인바운드 서버 + copilot_acp_client 아웃바운드 + acp_registry/agent.json + delegate_task acp_command 브리지), 멀티-프로토콜 모델 어댑터 이종 위임(delegation.provider 라우팅, api_mode 재유도, credential pool 공유), Kanban swarm 느슨한 결합 협업, Curator 자기개선 메타-에이전트(비활성 트리거, pure+LLM 2단계, 보조 모델 fork, 메인 캐시 미접촉, dry-run, consolidated/pruned 분류), cli-jaw 4항목 비교 포함.
24

Axis 06: App surfaces

AXIS

006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_hermes_surface.md

Overview

Axis 06: App surfaces — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 160_hermes-agent/ — app/server/client surfaces.

  • 이 문서는 NousResearch/hermes-agent(v0.15.1, acp_registry/agent.json)의 앱-서버 프로토콜 / 클라이언트 표면 / 세션 표면을 소스 기준으로 분석한다. Claude Code가 "프로토콜 부재의 단일 프로세스 CLI"였다면([[06_cc_surface]]), Hermes는 정반대 극단 — 단일 AIAgent 코어를 다섯 종류 이상의 와이어 프로토콜(다

  • Claude Code 분석의 핵심 명제는 "별도 앱서버가 없다(06_cc_surface.md:11)"였다. Hermes는 그 명제가 통째로 뒤집힌다. 코어(run_agent.py::AIAgent)는 동기 도구 루프 하나일 뿐이고, 가치의 상당 부분은 그 코어를 외부에 노출하는 표면 레이어에 박혀 있다. 표면을 여섯 축으로 분류하면:
  • | 다채널 게이트웨이 | python -m gateway.run / cli.py --gateway | 플랫폼별 native (Bot API 등) | HTTP webhook / long-poll / WS | 세션DB 영속 | gateway/run.py, gateway/platforms/ |
  • | OpenAI 호환 앱-서버 | api_server 플랫폼 (포트 8642) | OpenAI Chat/Responses + Hermes 확장 | aiohttp HTTP + SSE | 헤더 옵트인 | gateway/platforms/api_server.py |
  • | 웹 대시보드 | hermes dashboard (포트 9119) | REST(/api/*) + xterm WS | aiohttp + Vite SPA | 세션DB | web/, gateway/platforms/api_server.py |
  • | ACP 어댑터 | hermes acp / hermes-acp | Agent Client Protocol | JSON-RPC over stdio | ACP 세션 | acp_adapter/ |
  • | TUI 게이트웨이 | hermes --tui | 자체 JSON-RPC (session.*) | stdio 또는 WebSocket | 세션DB | tui_gateway/, ui-tui/ |
  • | MCP 서버 | mcp_serve.py | Model Context Protocol | stdio (FastMCP) | 읽기 위주 | mcp_serve.py |
  • 이 표 자체가 Claude Code와의 1차 대비점이다. CC는 행이 하나(subprocess stdin/stdout)뿐인데, Hermes는 여섯 행이며 그중 셋이 네트워크 노출 가능한 서버다.
  • graph TD

Topics covered

    1. 요약: Hermes는 "표면이 본체"인 Agent다

        GW["다채널 게이트웨이\ngateway/run.py\n~20 플랫폼"]
        API["OpenAI 호환 앱-서버\napi_server.py :8642\nHTTP + SSE"]
        WEB["웹 대시보드\n:9119 SPA + REST + xterm WS"]
        ACP["ACP 서버\nacp_adapter/server.py\nJSON-RPC/stdio"]
        TUI["TUI 게이트웨이\ntui_gateway/\nJSON-RPC: stdio | WS"]
        MCP["MCP 서버\nmcp_serve.py\nFastMCP/stdio"]
    end

    subgraph Clients["클라이언트"]
        Msg["Telegram/Discord/Slack/..."]
        OAIClient["OpenAI SDK / curl / 앱"]
        Browser["브라우저 (React 19 SPA)"]
        Editor["VS Code / Zed / JetBrains"]
        Ink["Ink TUI / iOS·web 클라이언트"]
        MCPHost["Claude Code / Cursor / Codex"]
    end

    Msg --> GW --> Agent
    OAIClient --> API --> Agent
    Browser --> WEB --> API
    Editor --> ACP --> Agent
    Ink --> TUI --> Agent
    MCPHost --> MCP --> SDB

README / docs

POST /v1/chat/completions   — OpenAI Chat Completions (api_server.py:4069, :1669)
POST /v1/responses          — OpenAI Responses API (stateful, previous_response_id)  (:4070)
GET  /v1/responses/{id}     — 저장된 응답 조회 (:4071)
DELETE /v1/responses/{id}   — 응답 삭제 (:4072)
GET  /v1/models             — 모델 목록 (:4055)
GET  /v1/capabilities       — 서버 능력 광고 (:4056, :1069)

README / docs

GET    /api/sessions                       — 세션 목록 (api_server.py:4060, :1305)
POST   /api/sessions                       — 빈 세션 생성 (:4061)
GET    /api/sessions/{id}                   — 세션 조회 (:4062)
PATCH  /api/sessions/{id}                   — 메타데이터 수정 (:4063)
DELETE /api/sessions/{id}                   — 세션 삭제 (:4064)
GET    /api/sessions/{id}/messages          — 메시지 히스토리 (:4065, :1422)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/fork              — SessionDB lineage로 분기 (:4066, :1439)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/chat              — 동기 1턴 (:4067, :1486)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/chat/stream       — SSE 스트리밍 턴 (:4068, :1530)

README / docs

POST /v1/runs                  — 비동기 에이전트 run 시작 (api_server.py:4083)
GET  /v1/runs/{id}             — run 상태 (:4084)
GET  /v1/runs/{id}/events      — 구조화 라이프사이클 이벤트 SSE 스트림 (:4085, :19)
POST /v1/runs/{id}/approval    — 도구 승인 결정 주입 (:4086)
POST /v1/runs/{id}/stop        — run 중단 (:4087)
GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/jobs[/{id}]  — cron job CRUD (:4074-4081)
POST /api/jobs/{id}/pause|resume|run    — job 라이프사이클 (:4079-4081)

README / docs

hermes dashboard           → 포트 9119, 빌드된 번들(web_dist/) 서빙 (hermes_cli/_parser.py:73)
  --stop / --status        → 데몬 관리 (_parser.py:74-75)
python -m hermes_cli.main web --no-open  + (web/ 에서) npm run dev
                           → Vite dev 서버(5173), /api 를 127.0.0.1:9119 로 프록시 (web/README.md)

README / docs

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Created: 표면별 진입 (api/gateway/acp/tui)
    Created --> Active: 첫 프롬프트 턴
    Active --> Active: tool_use 루프 (run_conversation)
    Active --> WaitingApproval: 승인 필요 도구
    WaitingApproval --> Active: 승인 주입 (/v1/runs/{id}/approval | 채널 버튼 | ACP)
    Active --> Forked: fork/branch (parent_session_id)
    Forked --> Active
    Active --> Handoff: handoff_state 설정
    Handoff --> Active: 다른 플랫폼이 _process_handoff
    Active --> Idle: 응답 완료
    Idle --> Active: 같은 세션 재진입 (X-Hermes-Session-Id / session.resume / load_session)
    Idle --> Reset: SessionResetPolicy (daily/idle/both)
    Reset --> [*]

Main analysis

Hermes Agent app-server/client surface

Analysis Snapshot: 160_hermes-agent/ — app/server/client surfaces.

This document analyzes app-server protocol / client surface / session surface of NousResearch/hermes-agent (v0.15.1, acp_registry/agent.json) on a source-by-source basis. If Claude Code was a "protocol-less, single-process CLI" ([[06_cc_surface]]), Hermes is the opposite extreme — a multi-surface server architecture that simultaneously exposes a single AIAgent core to more than five different wire protocols (multi-channel gateway, OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE app-server, ACP JSON-RPC, TUI JSON-RPC(stdio+WebSocket), MCP). For full operational context, see [[00_hermes_overview]]. All citations are based on actual files/lines.

0. Summary: Hermes is a “surface-to-body” agent.

The key proposition of Claude Code analysis was "There is no separate app server (06_cc_surface.md:11)." Hermes turns the whole proposition upside down. The core (run_agent.py::AIAgent) is just one synchronous tool loop, and much of its value is embedded in the surface layer that exposes that core to the outside world. If we classify the surface into six axes:

surface Entry point protocol transfer Sangtaeseong location
Multi-channel gateway python -m gateway.run / cli.py --gateway Native by platform (Bot API, etc.) HTTP webhook / long-poll / WS Session DB persistent gateway/run.py, gateway/platforms/
OpenAI compatible app-server api_server platform (port 8642) OpenAI Chat/Responses + Hermes Extension aiohttp HTTP + SSE Header Opt-in gateway/platforms/api_server.py
Web Dashboard hermes dashboard (port 9119) REST(/api/*) + xterm WS aiohttp + Vite SPA Session DB web/, gateway/platforms/api_server.py
ACP adapter hermes acp / hermes-acp Agent Client Protocol JSON-RPC over stdio ACP Session acp_adapter/
TUI Gateway hermes --tui own JSON-RPC (session.*) stdio or WebSocket Session DB tui_gateway/, ui-tui/
MCP Server mcp_serve.py Model Context Protocol stdio (FastMCP) Focus on reading mcp_serve.py

This table itself is the first point of comparison with Claude Code. CC has only one row (subprocess stdin/stdout), while Hermes has six rows, three of which are network-exposed servers.

graph TD
    subgraph Core["에이전트 코어 (단일 공유)"]
        Agent["AIAgent.run_conversation()\n(run_agent.py)"]
        SDB["SessionDB\n(hermes_state.py, SQLite+FTS5)"]
        Agent --> SDB
    end

    subgraph Surfaces["표면 레이어 (다중 프로토콜)"]
        GW["다채널 게이트웨이\ngateway/run.py\n~20 플랫폼"]
        API["OpenAI 호환 앱-서버\napi_server.py :8642\nHTTP + SSE"]
        WEB["웹 대시보드\n:9119 SPA + REST + xterm WS"]
        ACP["ACP 서버\nacp_adapter/server.py\nJSON-RPC/stdio"]
        TUI["TUI 게이트웨이\ntui_gateway/\nJSON-RPC: stdio | WS"]
        MCP["MCP 서버\nmcp_serve.py\nFastMCP/stdio"]
    end

    subgraph Clients["클라이언트"]
        Msg["Telegram/Discord/Slack/..."]
        OAIClient["OpenAI SDK / curl / 앱"]
        Browser["브라우저 (React 19 SPA)"]
        Editor["VS Code / Zed / JetBrains"]
        Ink["Ink TUI / iOS·web 클라이언트"]
        MCPHost["Claude Code / Cursor / Codex"]
    end

    Msg --> GW --> Agent
    OAIClient --> API --> Agent
    Browser --> WEB --> API
    Editor --> ACP --> Agent
    Ink --> TUI --> Agent
    MCPHost --> MCP --> SDB

1. OpenAI compatible app-server (api_server.py)

This is the component that most accurately corresponds to Codex's app-server in Hermes. APIServerAdapter (api_server.py:680) in gateway/platforms/api_server.py launches aiohttp web server (api_server.py:49 from aiohttp import web) to receive OpenAI format requests and routes them to AIAgent (api_server.py:684-685 docstring). The default port is DEFAULT_PORT = 8642 (api_server.py:66), overridden by the API_SERVER_PORT environment variable (api_server.py:694).

1.1 Endpoint surface

Routes are registered in _setup_routes (api_server.py:4052-4087). It is divided into three families.

(A) OpenAI compatible — for drop-in clients (/v1/*)

POST /v1/chat/completions   — OpenAI Chat Completions (api_server.py:4069, :1669)
POST /v1/responses          — OpenAI Responses API (stateful, previous_response_id)  (:4070)
GET  /v1/responses/{id}     — 저장된 응답 조회 (:4071)
DELETE /v1/responses/{id}   — 응답 삭제 (:4072)
GET  /v1/models             — 모델 목록 (:4055)
GET  /v1/capabilities       — 서버 능력 광고 (:4056, :1069)

/v1/chat/completions is stateless by default, but you can opt in to session continuity** with the X-Hermes-Session-Id header and long-term memory scoping with the **X-Hermes-Session-Key header (api_server.py:5-6 docstring). /v1/responses continues the state with previous_response_id according to OpenAI Responses semantics (api_server.py:6). In other words, unlike Claude Code, which can only subprocess due to “no SDK (06_cc_surface.md:273)”, Hermes provides a surface that works by pointing to the OpenAI SDK.

(B) Hermes Session Resource API — for rich clients (/api/sessions/*)

GET    /api/sessions                       — 세션 목록 (api_server.py:4060, :1305)
POST   /api/sessions                       — 빈 세션 생성 (:4061)
GET    /api/sessions/{id}                   — 세션 조회 (:4062)
PATCH  /api/sessions/{id}                   — 메타데이터 수정 (:4063)
DELETE /api/sessions/{id}                   — 세션 삭제 (:4064)
GET    /api/sessions/{id}/messages          — 메시지 히스토리 (:4065, :1422)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/fork              — SessionDB lineage로 분기 (:4066, :1439)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/chat              — 동기 1턴 (:4067, :1486)
POST   /api/sessions/{id}/chat/stream       — SSE 스트리밍 턴 (:4068, :1530)

/api/sessions/{id}/fork directly exposes the parent_session_id lineage primitive of SessionDB (api_server.py:1440 "branch via current SessionDB primitives"). This is the same mechanism as the compression split chain in [[00_hermes_overview]] §5.3, exposed to HTTP.

(C) Asynchronous Run + Job API — for long run/acknowledge loops (/v1/runs/*, /api/jobs/*)

POST /v1/runs                  — 비동기 에이전트 run 시작 (api_server.py:4083)
GET  /v1/runs/{id}             — run 상태 (:4084)
GET  /v1/runs/{id}/events      — 구조화 라이프사이클 이벤트 SSE 스트림 (:4085, :19)
POST /v1/runs/{id}/approval    — 도구 승인 결정 주입 (:4086)
POST /v1/runs/{id}/stop        — run 중단 (:4087)
GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/jobs[/{id}]  — cron job CRUD (:4074-4081)
POST /api/jobs/{id}/pause|resume|run    — job 라이프사이클 (:4079-4081)

/v1/runs/{id}/events flows structured lifecycle events to SSE and **out-of-band approval decisions are injected back into /v1/runs/{id}/approval — an externalization of Claude Code's WaitingApproval state ([[06_cc_surface]] §2.2) into a network protocol. The active run stream is managed by run_id -> asyncio.Queue (api_server.py:707). /api/jobs/* exposes the cron scheduler of [[00_hermes_overview]] §7.1 as REST.

1.2 Streaming (SSE)

Streaming is implemented with text/event-stream. /api/sessions/{id}/chat/stream sets Content-Type: text/event-stream with web.StreamResponse (api_server.py:1638), 30 seconds keepalive(CHAT_COMPLETIONS_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS = 30.0, Pull an event from the queue (api_server.py:1651) with api_server.py:69). /v1/capabilities advertises the chat_completions_streaming / responses_streaming / session_chat_streaming flag (api_server.py:1100-1112).

1.3 Authentication/Security Middleware

  • Bearer token: _check_auth(api_server.py:843) verifies the Authorization: Bearer <token> header. The key is the API_SERVER_KEY environment variable or the adapter extra["key"] (api_server.py:696). Compare using hmac.compare_digest(api_server.py:857) to prevent timing attacks. This is the local-first default value that passes without authentication (api_server.py:851) if the key is not set.
  • Session key gating: X-Hermes-Session-Key (long-term memory scoping) is only allowed** when API key authentication is turned on (api_server.py:905-912) — Intentional combination to prevent unauthenticated callers from accessing other people's memory.
  • CORS / body limits / security headers / idempotency: cors_middleware(api_server.py:510), body_limit_middleware(api_server.py:547), security_headers_middleware(api_server.py:574), _IdempotencyCache (api_server.py:584, Idempotency-Key header, 1000 ea. 300 seconds TTL). CORS allow-headers include Authorization, Content-Type, Idempotency-Key (api_server.py:504).
  • Response Store: ResponseStore (api_server.py:342) stores /v1/responses results in SQLite and narrows file permissions (_tighten_file_permissions, api_server.py:395).

1.4 Intentional absence — "borrowing protocols" instead of own SDK

The Hermes app-server does not create its own SDK (generated client). Instead, it opts for OpenAI wire compatibility and recycles the entire existing OpenAI SDK ecosystem as a client — a "protocol borrowing" strategy as opposed to Codex/Gemini's code generation of its own SDK (06_cc_surface.md:311). This is not “the SDK is intentionally missing,” but is a stronger absence of “the SDK was replaced with someone else’s.”

2. Web dashboard (browser surface)

web/ is Vite + React 19 + TypeScript SPA (web/package.json, web/README.md). The backend is the same api_server.py, and the build output falls into hermes_cli/web_dist/, where aiohttp serves it as a static SPA (web/README.md "serves as a static SPA"). It is enclosed in the Python package as package-data in pyproject.toml.

2.1 Two execution paths

hermes dashboard           → 포트 9119, 빌드된 번들(web_dist/) 서빙 (hermes_cli/_parser.py:73)
  --stop / --status        → 데몬 관리 (_parser.py:74-75)
python -m hermes_cli.main web --no-open  + (web/ 에서) npm run dev
                           → Vite dev 서버(5173), /api 를 127.0.0.1:9119 로 프록시 (web/README.md)

Note that the dashboard port (9119) and the OpenAI API port (8642) are different — the former is a management/monitoring SPA, and the latter is a programmatic API. Settings control theme (default/midnight/ember/mono/cyberpunk/rose), token analysis visibility, and OAuth gate in the dashboard section of config.yaml (hermes_cli/config.py:1243).

2.2 Terminal + Chat in the browser

web/package.json depends on the @xterm/xterm v6 + webgl/fit/unicode11/web-links add-on — which means it renders a real-time terminal in the browser (web/package.json dependencies). web/src/components/ contains full chat UI components such as ChatSidebar.tsx, ToolCall.tsx, Markdown.tsx, SlashPopover.tsx, ModelPickerDialog.tsx, OAuthLoginModal.tsx, PlatformsCard.tsx, and the page StatusPage/ConfigPage/EnvPage (web/README.md) provides status, dynamic settings editing, and API key management. In other words, it is not just a transfer of Claude Code's TUI to the browser, but a full web console complete with session, tool call, model selection, and platform management. (It is a first-class frontend with visual effects thanks to @react-three/fiber·gsap·unicode-animations dependencies.)

2.3 Public distribution/OAuth gate

config.yaml dashboard.oauth (hermes_cli/config.py:1265-1288) defines an OAuth gate that fires when --host is set and --insecure is not set. The Nous Portal plugin can read client_id/portal_url and force redirect_uri after a reverse proxy with HERMES_DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_URL (Fly.io deployment scenario specified in comments). This shows that the Hermes dashboard assumes public hosting as level 1 — an operating surface that CC does not have.

3. ACP Adapter (Editor Integration Surface)

acp_adapter/ exposes Hermes as a Agent Client Protocol server to VS Code / Zed / JetBrains (acp_adapter/server.py:1 docstring). Crucially different from Claude Code's IDE integration, which was "a thin wrapper (06_cc_surface.md:277) that wraps CLI as a subprocess", it implements the standard protocol (ACP JSON-RPC over stdio).

3.1 Transmission and hygiene

acp_adapter/entry.py loads ~/.hermes/.env, sends logging to stderr, and then launches the ACP server — because stdout is only for ACP JSON-RPC transport ([[00_hermes_overview]] §9). The liveness probes (ping/health) are outside the ACP schema, so they return -32601, but stderr traceback noise is suppressed.

3.2 ACP method surface

RPC implemented by HermesACPAgent(acp.Agent) (acp_adapter/server.py:445):

method line Role
initialize server.py:821 Protocol Version Negotiation + Ability Advertising
authenticate server.py:855 Only accept method_ids that match the advertised provider
new_session / load_session / resume_session :1069/:1086/:1130 Session creation/restore/resume
fork_session / list_sessions :1176/:1196 Branch·List
prompt :1243 Main prompt turn
cancel :1162 Cancel turn in progress
set_session_model / set_session_mode / set_config_option :1882/:1916/:1932 Zed model picker·mode·setting

initialize(server.py:821) advertises AgentCapabilities(load_session=True, prompt_capabilities=PromptCapabilities(image=True), session_capabilities=SessionCapabilities(fork, list, resume)) (server.py:843-852) — i.e. image input/session fork/list/resume at the protocol level. Declare. agent_info=Implementation(name="hermes-agent", version=HERMES_VERSION)(server.py:842).

3.3 Security model and resource processing

ACP is a stdio-only, local-trust threat model, so it only accepts method_ids that match the provider advertised by authenticate (server.py:855-870 comment). The ACP resource (file URI/embedded/image) sent by the client is converted to an OpenAI multimodal content part with an inline upper limit of _MAX_ACP_RESOURCE_BYTES = 512 KiB (server.py:91) (server.py:211-358). Distribution is registered as uvx hermes-agent[acp]==0.15.1 → hermes-acp in acp_registry/agent.json.

4. TUI Gateway (Dual Transport JSON-RPC)
