Main analysis

Hermes Agent Guardian / Approved

Analysis Snapshot: 160_hermes-agent/ — guardian and approval.

Analysis target: Nous Research NousResearch/hermes-agent (Python, ~1,984 .py). Version 0.15.1. Core files: tools/tirith_security.py (external tirith pre-run scanner wrapper), tools/approval.py (risk pattern matching + approval orchestration + smart approval + persistent allowlist), gateway/platforms/base.py (media forwarding denylist + recency-window + strict mode), gateway/run.py (channel authorization/HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT bridge), gateway/pairing.py (DM pairing). Related documents: [[00_hermes_overview]], [[12_cc_guardian]]

Hermes' approval system is also different from Claude Code's rule-based 5-mode permission engine ([[12_cc_guardian]]) or Codex's AI risk-scoring guardian. The key is two layers of pre-exec gates: (1) the external Rust binary tirith scans the command for content-level threats (homograph URL, pipe-to-interpreter, terminal injection) and determines it as an exit code, and (2) its own regular expression DANGEROUS_PATTERNS + HARDLINE_PATTERNS scans the command for structure-level threats (rm -rf /, fork bomb, sudo password guess) The two results are combined into one acknowledgment prompt in check_all_command_guards() and surfaced in the native UI per channel (CLI input / gateway yes-no button). Here, media delivery defense (credential denylist + recency-window + strict mode) and channel authorization/pairing at the gateway output are added to form a multi-layered defense.

1. tirith pre-launch security scan

tools/tirith_security.py is a wrapper that launches an external tirith binary as a subprocess to scan commands. The module docstring specifies the core contract: "Exit code is the verdict source of truth: 0 = allow, 1 = block, 2 = warn. JSON stdout enriches findings/summary but never overrides the verdict."

1.1 Configuration and fail-open

_load_security_config() (L68) reads the security section of config.yaml and overrides it with env var:

defaults = {
    "tirith_enabled": True,
    "tirith_path": "tirith",
    "tirith_timeout": 5,
    "tirith_fail_open": True,    # 스캐너 실패시 통과 (가용성 우선)
}
# 각각 TIRITH_ENABLED / TIRITH_BIN / TIRITH_TIMEOUT / TIRITH_FAIL_OPEN 로 오버라이드

tirith_fail_open: True (default) is a availability-first philosophy — pass commands if the scanner itself dies. This is the biggest comparison point with [[12_cc_guardian]]'s Tengu chain (fail-closed with ask if it does not pass the check) or Codex guardian (risk≥80→ask).

1.2 exit-code determination (check_command_security)

check_command_security(command) (L696) is the main API. {"action":"allow"} immediately if inactive/unsupported platform. Otherwise, run tirith check --json --non-interactive --shell posix -- <command> with subprocess.run(timeout=...) (L731). exit code mapping:

exit code action Meaning
0 allow No threat
1 block Block recommendation (enter approval flow)
2 warn Alert (enter approval flow)
Other allow if fail-open, otherwise block unexpected exit code

After the decision, the JSON stdout is parsed to fill in findings/summary (L775~), but if JSON parsing fails, only the description, not the verdict, is degraded (L783) — the exit code always takes precedence. Operational failure handling follows the fail_open setting:

except OSError as exc:        # FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, exec format error
    if fail_open: return {"action": "allow", ...}      # L747
    return {"action": "block", "summary": "tirith spawn failed (fail-closed)"}
except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    if fail_open: return {"action": "allow", ...}      # L756
    return {"action": "block", "summary": "tirith timed out (fail-closed)"}

1.3 Automated installation and supply chain verification

If tirith is not in your PATH/config path, it automatically downloads from the GitHub release to $HERMES_HOME/bin/tirith (_install_tirith, L355). Downloads always verify the SHA-256 checksum (_verify_checksum, L304) and, if cosign is in the PATH, also verify the provenance signature (the release workflow GitHub Actions identity) (_verify_cosign, L264). The cosign identity is a regular expression pinned to the release workflow:

_COSIGN_IDENTITY_REGEXP = f"^https://github.com/{_REPO}/\\.github/workflows/release\\.yml@refs/tags/v"

If cosign verification explicitly fails (False), the installation is stopped due to suspicion of tampering (L409), but if cosign is not found (None) or the execution itself is broken, it proceeds only with SHA-256. The installation runs on a daemon thread (_background_install, L566) and does not block startup, and failures are cached with a 24h TTL disk marker (_mark_install_failed) to prevent retrying each command. Windows has no tirith build (_detect_target is None), so it quietly falls only to a pattern-matching guard (is_platform_supported, L244).

The path resolution state machine (_resolve_tirith_path, L458) is meticulous: if the user sets an explicit path (_is_explicit_path, L453), that path is authoritative and will never automatically download other binaries. The default "tirith" is (1) PATH lookup → (2) $HERMES_HOME/bin/tirith → (3) GitHub automatic installation. The failure reason tag (_install_failure_reason) determines whether it is retryable or not — in particular, "cosign_missing" automatically clears the disk marker (_is_install_failed_on_disk, L163) if cosign later appears in the PATH and retries without restarting the process. The spawn/timeout warnings are deduped with _warn_once (L113), preventing fail-open tirith misconfigurations from filling errors.log with hundreds of lines for every command.

1.4 Suppress .app TLD false-positives

If the warn decision consists of only one lookalike_tld finding for the .app gTLD, demoted to allow (_is_app_tld_finding, L806). .app is a gTLD commonly used by normal production services, so the “confuse with file extension” heuristic produces false positives in regular API calls.

2. Risk pattern matching (tools/approval.py)

tools/approval.py is "the single source of truth for the dangerous command system" according to the module docstring — it contains pattern detection, per-session approval status, prompts, smart approvals, and a persistent allowlist.

2.1 Three layer pattern

tier constant Action Is it possible to bypass it?
Hardline HARDLINE_PATTERNS (L203, 12) Unconditional blocking ❌ yolo/mode=off/cron doesn't work either
sudo stdin guard _SUDO_STDIN_RE(L250) Unconditional blocking ❌
Dangerous DANGEROUS_PATTERNS (L321, 47) Request for approval ✅ yolo/smart-approve can be passed

Hardline is a minimal set containing only catastrophic commands with "no recovery path" (note L178): recursive delete of root/system directories (rm -rf /, /home, /etc, etc.), format mkfs, overwrite raw block devices (dd of=/dev/sd*, > /dev/nvme*), fork bomb(:(){ :|:& };:), kill -1, system shutdown/reboot (shutdown/reboot/halt/poweroff, init 0/6, systemctl poweroff). Shutdown/reboot patterns are anchored with _CMDPOS (L194, command start position regular expression — consumes sudo/env/exec/nohup wrappers) to prevent false positives for echo reboot or grep 'shutdown' log. Commentaries call this “floor below yolo” — the philosophy that yolo entrusts agents with files/services, not erases disks or powers down.

sudo stdin guard(_check_sudo_stdin_guard, L255) blocks if sudo -S (stdin password) appears when SUDO_PASSWORD is not set — an attack vector where LLM brute-forces candidate passwords to stdin and sees "Sorry, try again".

Dangerous (47) are risks that can be passed with permission: rm -r, chmod 777, SQL DROP/DELETE without WHERE/TRUNCATE, curl|sh, python -c, overwriting credentials/system files (> /etc/, tee/sed into _SENSITIVE_WRITE_TARGET), git reset --hard/push --force, docker container lifecycle, and prevent self-termination (pkill hermes, kill $(pgrep -f hermes), hermes gateway stop — prevent gateway from killing itself).

2.2 Sensitive Path Regular Expression Fragments

Credential/system file write detection combines reusable regular expression fragments (L122-159). _SENSITIVE_WRITE_TARGET (L152) includes system config (/etc/ + macOS /private/etc mirror), raw devices (/dev/sd), ~/.ssh, $HERMES_HOME/.env, shell rc files (.bashrc/.zshrc/.profile, etc.), credentials Group the files (.netrc/.pgpass/.npmrc/.pypirc) with OR. Includes a variant to match shell extension bypass ($HOME, ${HERMES_HOME}), and on macOS, /etc is a symlink to /private/etc, so /private/etc/sudoers writes are prevented from bypassing the plain /etc/ check with _MACOS_PRIVATE_SYSTEM_PATH (L145) — Comments It states that it is inspired by "dangerous path protection" in Claude Code 2.1.113. These fragments are reused in the tee/redirect/cp/mv/sed-inplace pattern to maintain consistency when adding new patterns.

2.3 Obfuscation normalization and key alias

_normalize_command_for_detection (L464) prevents obfuscation bypass by removing ANSI escapes (strip_ansi), removing null bytes, and applying Unicode NFKC normalization (fullwidth Latin, etc.) before matching. All patterns are added to re.IGNORECASE | It is pre-compiled with re.DOTALL (DANGEROUS_PATTERNS_COMPILED, L431) to remove re.compile fan-out in hot-path. The authorization key is a human-readable description, but _PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES (L442) bidirectionally maps to older versions of regular expression-derived keys to maintain compatibility with existing command_allowlist items.

2.4 Skip container backend

The docker/singularity/modal/daytona environment types cannot touch the host, so they all pass the authorization layer (L940, L1171). vercel_sandbox is additionally exempted from check_execute_code_guard (L1482).

3. Approval orchestration and flow

3.1 Integrated guards check_all_command_guards

The main entry point that terminal_tool.py calls just before command execution is check_all_command_guards(command, env_type, approval_callback) (L1161). Collect the tirith and dangerous-command results and present them as a single unified approval request — to prevent a gateway force=True rerun from showing only one check and bypassing the others (note L1166).

Decision order (priority = security strength):

1. 컨테이너 백엔드 → 통과                                    (L1171)
2. Hardline 매칭 → 무조건 BLOCK (yolo도 못 뚫음)            (L1178)
3. Sudo stdin guard → 무조건 BLOCK                          (L1188)
4. _YOLO_MODE_FROZEN / 세션 yolo / approvals.mode==off → 통과 (L1197)
5. 비대화·비게이트웨이·비ask 컨텍스트:
     - cron 세션 + cron_mode==deny → BLOCK                  (L1208)
     - 그 외 → 통과 (auto-approve, 경고 로그)               (L1223)
6. [Phase 1] tirith 스캔 + dangerous 탐지 → warnings 수집
7. [Phase 2.5] approvals.mode==smart → 보조 LLM 위험판정     (L1270)
8. [Phase 3] 채널별 승인 프롬프트 (gateway/ask/CLI)

3.2 YOLO mode freeze (prompt-injection defense)

_YOLO_MODE_FROZEN(L29) reads and freezes os.getenv("HERMES_YOLO_MODE") once at the time of module import. The comment explains why: reading the environment variable on each call creates a prompt-injection privilege escalation path that allows any skill running within the process to set this variable and immediately bypass all permission checks. Session-specific yolo (enable_session_yolo, L601) is managed separately for gateway /yolo and is not process-global.

3.3 Smart Approve (_smart_approve)

When approvals.mode: "smart", _smart_approve(command, description) (L878) asks auxiliary LLM (auxiliary_client.call_llm(task="approval")) to evaluate the risk before asking the user. The prompt takes the single words APPROVE/DENY/ESCALATE for the “Security Reviewer” role. The annotation states that it is inspired by OpenAI Codex's Smart Approvals guardian subagent (openai/codex#13860).

  • APPROVE → Passes after automatically granting session approval (absorbs false-positives such as python -c "print('hello')")
  • DENY → Block immediately
  • ESCALATE (uncertain or LLM call failed, L920) → fall-through to manual prompt

3.4 Session state and persistent allowlist

Approval status is managed thread-safely (_lock, L500): _session_approved (per-session set), _session_yolo (per-session), _permanent_approved (global). Gateway session keys are isolated per-thread with contextvars(_approval_session_key, L35) and are not racy in concurrent execution of executor threads.

Permanent allowlist(command_allowlist) is a pattern where the user chooses always. load_permanent_allowlist(L677) loads from config.yaml when importing a module (L1624), and save_permanent_allowlist(L695) saves it again. However, tirith finding is never permanently registered — Broad permanent allowlisting is inappropriate for content-level security finding, so even if always is selected, it is only processed as session (L1366, L1442; in CLI, [a]lways option itself is hidden with allow_permanent=not has_tirith, L1410).

3.5 Gateway callback and blocking wait

In a gateway/ask context, the synchronous agent thread is blocked until the user responds. Each session has a _ApprovalEntry(threading.Event, L515) queue in _gateway_queues(L525), and a callback registered with register_gateway_notify(L529) bridges sync→async. _await_gateway_decision(L1060) is the key wait loop:

  • Fire pre_approval_request hook → Send to user with notify_cb → Wait for Event
  • Default timeout 300 seconds (gateway_timeout), polling in 1 second slices and sending a heartbeat with touch_activity_if_due every ~10s to prevent the gateway watchdog from killing waiting agents (L1138)
  • /approve(resolve_gateway_approval, L554) resolves the oldest in FIFO, and /approve all resolves all.
  • post_approval_response hook utterance (choice: once/session/always/deny/timeout)

Consent contract(L1333): timeout (non-response) and explicit deny both hard halt. The BLOCKED message explicitly blocks the agent's common avoidance path — "Do NOT retry, do NOT rephrase, do NOT attempt the same outcome via a different command. Silence is not consent." (Issue #24912).

3.6 CLI interactive prompt and stdin-deadlock guard

In the CLI path, prompt_dangerous_approval (L710) brings up the [o]nce / [s]ession / [a]lways / deny prompt. The default timeout is 60 seconds (_get_approval_timeout, L857), and input is received as a daemon thread + thread.join(timeout=...), and if there is no response, it is fail-closed with deny (L794). An interesting safeguard is prompt_toolkit conflict avoidance (L748): in an interactive CLI session, if prompt_toolkit owns the terminal and no acknowledge callbacks are registered on this thread, the input() fallback will experience an invisible 60s deadlock (#15216) where it will never see Enter — if it detects this, it will immediately deny and log heavily. Threads that require approval must first install set_approval_callback() (an established pattern in delegate_tool/run_agent).

The legacy single check entry point check_dangerous_command (L925) also remains — the dangerous-only path before tirith integration, following the same hardline floor → yolo → pattern detection → session approval sequence. When a dangerous command is encountered in a non-interactive/non-gateway context, it auto-approve (L983) with a warning log. This is the intended behavior for headless script/test compatibility, and approval is forced by setting HERMES_INTERACTIVE/HERMES_GATEWAY_SESSION.

3.7 execute_code separate guard

check_execute_code_guard(code, env_type) (L1455), called by tools/code_execution_tool.py (L1104), guards arbitrary local Python — scripts can call process/file APIs directly with subprocess/os.system/ctypes to bypass terminal()/DANGEROUS_PATTERNS Because (#30882). In gateway/ask, the entire script is approved one-shot, and the approval is never persisted (since each script is a distinct arbitrary code, L1616).

3.8 cron approval

approvals.cron_mode (default deny) governs the user absence context. The cron session is never a gateway-approval context (_is_gateway_approval_context, L102) — because if it were, it would be infinitely blocked due to pending approval without a listener. If deny, the dangerous command is blocked and instructions are given to find an alternative (L1208).

3.9 Approval flow diagram

graph TD
    A["terminal() / execute_code()"] --> B["check_all_command_guards()"]
    B --> C{"컨테이너 백엔드?<br/>docker/modal/..."}
    C -->|"예"| PASS["통과 (approved)"]
    C -->|"아니오"| D{"Hardline 매칭?"}
    D -->|"예"| HB["무조건 BLOCK<br/>(yolo도 못 뚫음)"]
    D -->|"아니오"| SG{"sudo -S 추측?"}
    SG -->|"예"| HB
    SG -->|"아니오"| Y{"YOLO / mode=off?"}
    Y -->|"예"| PASS
    Y -->|"아니오"| NI{"비대화·비GW·비ask?"}
    NI -->|"cron+deny"| CB["BLOCK (cron 무인)"]
    NI -->|"그 외"| PASS
    NI -->|"대화/GW/ask"| P1["Phase1: tirith 스캔<br/>+ DANGEROUS 탐지"]
    P1 --> W{"warnings 있음?"}
    W -->|"없음"| PASS
    W -->|"있음"| SM{"mode==smart?"}
    SM -->|"APPROVE"| PASS
    SM -->|"DENY"| SD["BLOCK (smart)"]
    SM -->|"ESCALATE / manual"| ASK["채널별 승인 요청"]
    ASK -->|"게이트웨이"| GQ["_await_gateway_decision<br/>(Event 큐 + heartbeat)"]
    ASK -->|"CLI"| CLI["prompt_dangerous_approval<br/>(input, 60s)"]
    GQ --> R{"응답"}
    CLI --> R
    R -->|"once"| PASS
    R -->|"session"| SS["approve_session"] --> PASS
    R -->|"always (비tirith)"| AP["approve_permanent<br/>+ command_allowlist 저장"] --> PASS
    R -->|"deny / timeout"| DB["BLOCK<br/>(Silence is not consent)"]

4. Media delivery defense (gateway/platforms/base.py)

The gateway also protects on the output side — preventing host secrets from being leaked when sending local file paths emitted by the model as native attachments. The key is validate_media_delivery_path(path) (L1002), which returns an absolute path if safe, or None otherwise.

4.1 Three Gates

steps Action
Allowlist (unconditional trust) MEDIA_DELIVERY_SAFE_ROOTS (L840, Hermes managed cache + HERMES_MEDIA_ALLOW_DIRS operator-specified) subsurface mode independent pass (L1050)
Denylist (block unconditionally) _MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_PREFIXES (L875) + block if $HOME subcredential directory (L890)
Strict mode If off, anything outside the denylist will pass, if on, only allowlist/recency will pass

Symbolic links are resolved with resolve(strict=True) before checking (L1041) to prevent symlink bypass.

4.2 denylist (credentials/system path)

_MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_PREFIXES = ("/etc","/proc","/sys","/dev","/root",
                                   "/boot","/var/log","/var/lib","/var/run")
_MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_HOME_SUBPATHS = (".ssh",".aws",".gnupg",".kube",
    ".docker",".config",".azure",".gcloud","Library/Keychains")
# 추가로 $HERMES_HOME/.env, auth.json, credentials/ 명시 차단 (L957~)

This denylist is applied unconditionally even if a recency-window is passed — explicit prompt-injection exfil paths such as MEDIA:/etc/passwd and MEDIA:~/.ssh/id_rsa are always denied (L1059).

4.3 recency-window + strict mode

_MEDIA_DELIVERY_TRUST_RECENT_DEFAULT_SECONDS = 600   # 10분

The default is non-strict mode (L936 annotation): symmetry with inbound (since it accepts any document uploaded by the user), any regular file outside the denylist is allowed to be forwarded. strict mode (gateway.strict: True or HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT=1) is for public gateways, which means that files are (a) Hermes cache, (b) operator allowlist root, or (c) freshly created files inside the recency-window (matplotlib plot, pandoc PDF, etc. are created seconds before delivery, but /etc/passwd·id_rsa has mtime It must be in day/month units → outside the window) to pass (L1067). The strict purpose is explicit: "prompt injection from one user shouldn't be able to exfiltrate the host's secrets to that same user."

gateway/run.py reads gateway.strict in config.yaml and bridges it to HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT env (L940~943).

5. Permissions/pairing by channel

5.1 Authorization (_is_user_authorized)

The gateway checks first the inbound message in _handle_message ([[00_hermes_overview]] §2.3). Some adapters (WeCom/Weixin/Yuanbao/QQBot) process config-driven access such as dm_policy/group_policy inside the adapter, so the gateway's env-based allowlist check is skipped with the "already authorized at intake" flag so that it does not default-deny again (base.py L1663~1676).

5.2 Unauthorized DM processing (_get_unauthorized_dm_behavior)

_get_unauthorized_dm_behavior(L6797) in gateway/run.py determines how to handle unauthorized DM. Solving order:

  1. Specify platform-specific unauthorized_dm_behavior config → always takes precedence
  2. Global explicit config → next priority
  3. If allowlist is set (TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS, etc., GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS) → Default "ignore" — This is a signal that the operator has intentionally restricted access, so sending pairing codes to unknown contacts is noisy and risks information leakage (#9337)
  4. config-driven dm_policy: pairing → "pair", allowlist/disabled → "ignore"
  5. If there is neither allowlist nor explicit config → "pair" (open-gateway default)

Unauthorized callers on group channels are quietly ignored.

5.3 Pairing (gateway/pairing.py)

If the policy is "pair", the pairing code is presented to the unauthorized DM sender, and the bot owner approves it in the CLI. Security property (PairingManager, docstring L9):

properties value
code alphabet ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789 (8 characters, excluding 0/O/1/I)
create secrets.choice(L236, cryptographically random number)
expired CODE_TTL_SECONDS = 3600 (1 hour)
Pending limit per platform MAX_PENDING_PER_PLATFORM = 3
rate limit _is_rate_limited(L227) — Pairing spam defense
Save ~/.hermes/platforms/pairing/{platform}-{pending,approved}.json (_secure_write)

is_approved(L144) checks approval by matching the normalized user_id alias, and _approve_user(L162) adds it to the approval list.

6. Summary of defense layers

tier location What's stopping fail operation
Channel Authorization/Pairing gateway/run.py, gateway/pairing.py Unauthorized caller default-deny / ignore
Hardline blocks approval.py HARDLINE_PATTERNS Irrecoverable Catastrophe Order Unconditionally blocked (regardless of yolo)
sudo stdin guard approval.py _SUDO_STDIN_RE Password brute-force Unconditional blocking
tirith content scan tirith_security.py homograph/pipe-to-sh/terminal injection fail-open (default)
Dangerous pattern approval.py DANGEROUS_PATTERNS Structural Risk Command Request for approval (possible to bypass yolo/smart)
Smart Approval approval.py _smart_approve false-positive absorption escalate→manual
Media Delivery base.py validate_media_delivery_path secret exfiltration denylist block + (strict)recency

cli-jaw comparison implications

  • (a) Pre-execution scan + approval callback vs interface readonly + permission prompt. Hermes agent core defends itself: tirith_security.check_command_security (external Rust scanner, exit-code determination) and approval.check_all_command_guards (regex hardline/dangerous + gateway Event queue callback) are embedded directly in the tool execution path. cli-jaw harness protects the agent — it performs the same control at the harness layer with the Bash tool's readonly interface, settings.json allow/deny rules, and harness-level permission prompts (update-config skill). Hermes' defense extends to non-interactive channels such as gateway/cron/ACP, but cli-jaw's defense is tied to the harnessed interactive session.

  • (b) fail-open vs fail-closed asymmetry. Hermes tirith defaults to tirith_fail_open: True, so when the scanner dies, commands are passed (availability priority) — However, HARDLINE_PATTERNS/sudo-stdin guard forms an asymmetric defense by laying a fail-closed floor that even yolo·mode=off cannot penetrate. [[12_cc_guardian]] Claude Code's Tengu chain is fail-closed by asking if it does not pass, and the cli-jaw harness permission prompt is also close to fail-closed by asking the user when a rule does not match. In other words, Hermes intentionally chose fail-open for “availability of long-running gateway daemon” but supplemented it with a catastrophic floor design.

  • (c) Separation of content vs. structure threats. Hermes separates tirith (content level: homograph URL, pipe-to-interpreter, terminal injection) and its own regex (structure level: rm -rf /, fork bomb, SQL DROP) into two engines and combines them into a single prompt in check_all_command_guards. cli-jaw does not have a core mechanism equivalent to an external Rust security scanner, and relies on harness rules + model/user judgment to determine dangerous commands — Hermes' tirith bisection model is a reference point for cli-jaw when adding content injection defenses.

  • (d) Output stage exfiltration defense. Hermes' validate_media_delivery_path (denylist + recency-window + strict mode) is an explicit public-gateway defense that blocks "one user's prompt injection from stealing host secrets to the same user" at the output attachment stage. cli-jaw is ad-hoc with multi-channel output attachment, and there is no core gate corresponding to these recency-window-based credential exfil defenses or delegated to harness permissions — if you're running a public bot, Hermes' gateway.strict + denylist pattern is a directly portable reference.

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent v0.15.1) First created in Guardian/Approval dimension. tirith pre-execution scan (check_command_security exit-code 0/1/2 decision + fail-open + cosign/SHA-256 automatic installation), 3-layer risk pattern (HARDLINE/sudo-stdin/DANGEROUS) + NFKC normalization + key alias, check_all_command_guards 8-level decision + YOLO freeze + smart approval (auxiliary LLM) + gateway Event queue callback/consent contract + execute_code one-shot guard + cron_mode, media delivery defense (denylist + recency-window 600s + strict mode), channel authorization/_get_unauthorized_dm_behavior/pairing (secrets 8 characters/1h TTL/rate limit), defense layer summary table, cli-jaw 4-item comparison included.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 012_guardian-approval/12_hermes_guardian.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Hermes Agent 가디언 / 승인

분석 스냅샷: 160_hermes-agent/ — guardian and approval.

분석 대상: Nous Research NousResearch/hermes-agent (Python, ~1,984 .py). 버전 0.15.1. 핵심 파일: tools/tirith_security.py(외부 tirith 사전실행 스캐너 래퍼), tools/approval.py(위험패턴 매칭 + 승인 오케스트레이션 + 스마트승인 + 영구 allowlist), gateway/platforms/base.py(미디어 전달 denylist + recency-window + strict mode), gateway/run.py(채널 인가/HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT 브리지), gateway/pairing.py(DM 페어링). 관련 문서: [[00_hermes_overview]], [[12_cc_guardian]]

Hermes의 승인 시스템은 Claude Code의 규칙기반 5-mode 퍼미션 엔진([[12_cc_guardian]])이나 Codex의 AI risk-scoring guardian과 또 다르다. 핵심은 두 층의 사전실행(pre-exec) 게이트다: (1) 외부 Rust 바이너리 tirith가 명령의 콘텐츠 수준 위협(homograph URL, pipe-to-interpreter, 터미널 인젝션)을 스캔하고 exit code로 판정하며, (2) 자체 정규식 DANGEROUS_PATTERNS + HARDLINE_PATTERNS가 구조 수준 위협(rm -rf /, fork bomb, sudo 비밀번호 추측)을 잡는다. 두 결과는 check_all_command_guards()에서 하나의 승인 프롬프트로 합쳐져 채널별 네이티브 UI(CLI input / 게이트웨이 yes-no 버튼)로 표면화된다. 여기에 게이트웨이 출력단의 미디어 전달 방어(자격증명 denylist + recency-window + strict mode)와 채널 인가/페어링이 더해져 다층 방어를 이룬다.

1. tirith 사전실행 보안 스캔

tools/tirith_security.py는 외부 tirith 바이너리를 서브프로세스로 띄워 명령을 스캔하는 래퍼다. 모듈 docstring이 핵심 계약을 명시한다: "Exit code is the verdict source of truth: 0 = allow, 1 = block, 2 = warn. JSON stdout enriches findings/summary but never overrides the verdict."

1.1 설정과 fail-open

_load_security_config()(L68)이 config.yaml의 security 섹션을 읽고 env var로 오버라이드한다:

defaults = {
    "tirith_enabled": True,
    "tirith_path": "tirith",
    "tirith_timeout": 5,
    "tirith_fail_open": True,    # 스캐너 실패시 통과 (가용성 우선)
}
# 각각 TIRITH_ENABLED / TIRITH_BIN / TIRITH_TIMEOUT / TIRITH_FAIL_OPEN 로 오버라이드

tirith_fail_open: True(기본)는 가용성 우선 철학이다 — 스캐너 자체가 죽으면 명령을 통과시킨다. 이것이 [[12_cc_guardian]]의 Tengu 체인(체크 통과 못 하면 ask로 fail-closed)이나 Codex guardian(risk≥80→ask)과 가장 큰 비교점이다.

1.2 exit-code 판정 (check_command_security)

check_command_security(command)(L696)이 메인 API다. 비활성/미지원 플랫폼이면 즉시 {"action":"allow"}. 그 외에는 tirith check --json --non-interactive --shell posix -- <command>를 subprocess.run(timeout=...)로 실행한다(L731). exit code 매핑:

exit code action 의미
0 allow 위협 없음
1 block 차단 권고 (승인 흐름으로 진입)
2 warn 경고 (승인 흐름으로 진입)
그 외 fail-open이면 allow, 아니면 block unexpected exit code

판정 후 JSON stdout을 파싱해 findings/summary를 채우지만(L775~), JSON 파싱 실패는 verdict가 아닌 설명만 degrade시킨다(L783) — exit code가 항상 우선이다. 운영 실패 처리는 fail_open 설정을 따른다:

except OSError as exc:        # FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, exec format error
    if fail_open: return {"action": "allow", ...}      # L747
    return {"action": "block", "summary": "tirith spawn failed (fail-closed)"}
except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    if fail_open: return {"action": "allow", ...}      # L756
    return {"action": "block", "summary": "tirith timed out (fail-closed)"}

1.3 자동 설치와 공급망 검증

tirith가 PATH/설정 경로에 없으면 GitHub 릴리스에서 $HERMES_HOME/bin/tirith로 자동 다운로드한다(_install_tirith, L355). 다운로드는 항상 SHA-256 체크섬을 검증하고(_verify_checksum, L304), cosign이 PATH에 있으면 provenance 서명(릴리스 워크플로 GitHub Actions 신원)까지 검증한다(_verify_cosign, L264). cosign 신원은 릴리스 워크플로에 핀된 정규식이다:

_COSIGN_IDENTITY_REGEXP = f"^https://github.com/{_REPO}/\\.github/workflows/release\\.yml@refs/tags/v"

cosign 검증이 명시적으로 실패(False)하면 변조 의심으로 설치를 중단하지만(L409), cosign이 없거나(None) 실행 자체가 깨지면 SHA-256만으로 진행한다. 설치는 데몬 스레드(_background_install, L566)에서 돌아 기동을 막지 않으며, 실패는 24h TTL 디스크 마커(_mark_install_failed)로 캐싱해 매 명령 재시도를 막는다. Windows는 tirith 빌드가 없어(_detect_target이 None) 조용히 패턴매칭 가드로만 떨어진다(is_platform_supported, L244).

경로 해석 상태기계(_resolve_tirith_path, L458)는 세심하다: 사용자가 명시 경로를 설정하면(_is_explicit_path, L453) 그 경로가 authoritative이고 절대 다른 바이너리를 자동 다운로드하지 않는다. 기본 "tirith"는 (1) PATH lookup → (2) $HERMES_HOME/bin/tirith → (3) GitHub 자동설치 순. 실패 사유 태그(_install_failure_reason)는 retryable 여부를 구분한다 — 특히 "cosign_missing"은 cosign이 나중에 PATH에 나타나면 디스크 마커를 자동으로 비워(_is_install_failed_on_disk, L163) 프로세스 재시작 없이 재시도한다. spawn/timeout 경고는 _warn_once(L113)로 dedupe되어, fail-open tirith 오설정이 매 명령마다 errors.log를 수백 줄로 채우는 것을 막는다.

1.4 .app TLD false-positive 억제

warn 판정이 오직 .app gTLD에 대한 lookalike_tld finding 하나로만 구성되면 allow로 강등한다(_is_app_tld_finding, L806). .app은 정상 production 서비스가 많이 쓰는 gTLD라 "파일 확장자와 혼동" 휴리스틱이 일반 API 호출에서 오탐을 낸다.

2. 위험패턴 매칭 (tools/approval.py)

tools/approval.py는 모듈 docstring상 "the single source of truth for the dangerous command system"이다 — 패턴 탐지, 세션별 승인 상태, 프롬프트, 스마트승인, 영구 allowlist를 모두 담는다.

2.1 세 계층의 패턴

계층 상수 동작 우회 가능?
Hardline HARDLINE_PATTERNS(L203, 12개) 무조건 차단 ❌ yolo/mode=off/cron도 못 뚫음
Sudo stdin guard _SUDO_STDIN_RE(L250) 무조건 차단 ❌
Dangerous DANGEROUS_PATTERNS(L321, 47개) 승인 요청 ✅ yolo/smart-approve 통과 가능

Hardline은 "복구 경로가 없는" 재앙적 명령만 담는 최소 집합이다(주석 L178): 루트/시스템 디렉토리 재귀 삭제(rm -rf /, /home, /etc 등), mkfs 포맷, raw 블록 디바이스 덮어쓰기(dd of=/dev/sd*, > /dev/nvme*), fork bomb(:(){ :|:& };:), kill -1, 시스템 종료/재부팅(shutdown/reboot/halt/poweroff, init 0/6, systemctl poweroff). 종료/재부팅 패턴은 _CMDPOS(L194, 명령 시작 위치 정규식 — sudo/env/exec/nohup 래퍼 소비)로 앵커해 echo reboot나 grep 'shutdown' log 오탐을 막는다. 주석은 이를 **"yolo 아래의 바닥(floor below yolo)"**이라 부른다 — yolo는 에이전트에게 파일/서비스를 맡기는 것이지 디스크를 지우거나 전원을 끄게 하는 게 아니라는 철학.

Sudo stdin guard(_check_sudo_stdin_guard, L255)는 SUDO_PASSWORD가 설정 안 됐는데 sudo -S(stdin 비밀번호)가 나타나면 차단한다 — LLM이 후보 비밀번호를 stdin으로 흘리며 "Sorry, try again"을 보고 brute-force하는 공격 벡터.

Dangerous(47개)는 승인으로 통과 가능한 위험들: rm -r, chmod 777, SQL DROP/DELETE without WHERE/TRUNCATE, curl|sh, python -c, 자격증명/시스템 파일 덮어쓰기(> /etc/, tee/sed into _SENSITIVE_WRITE_TARGET), git reset --hard/push --force, docker 컨테이너 lifecycle, 그리고 자기종료 방지(pkill hermes, kill $(pgrep -f hermes), hermes gateway stop — 게이트웨이가 자기 자신을 죽이지 못하게).

2.2 민감 경로 정규식 프래그먼트

자격증명/시스템 파일 쓰기 탐지는 재사용 가능한 정규식 프래그먼트를 조합한다(L122~159). _SENSITIVE_WRITE_TARGET(L152)은 시스템 config(/etc/ + macOS /private/etc 미러), raw 디바이스(/dev/sd), ~/.ssh, $HERMES_HOME/.env, 셸 rc 파일(.bashrc/.zshrc/.profile 등), 자격증명 파일(.netrc/.pgpass/.npmrc/.pypirc)을 OR로 묶는다. 셸 확장 우회($HOME, ${HERMES_HOME})까지 매칭하도록 변형을 포함하며, macOS에서 /etc가 /private/etc의 심링크라 /private/etc/sudoers 쓰기가 plain /etc/ 체크를 우회하는 것을 _MACOS_PRIVATE_SYSTEM_PATH(L145)로 막는다 — 주석은 Claude Code 2.1.113의 "dangerous path protection"에서 영감을 받았다고 명시한다. 이 프래그먼트들이 tee/리다이렉션/cp/mv/sed-inplace 패턴에 재사용되어 새 패턴 추가시 일관성을 유지한다.

2.3 난독화 정규화와 키 alias

_normalize_command_for_detection(L464)이 매칭 전에 ANSI escape 제거(strip_ansi), null byte 제거, Unicode NFKC 정규화(fullwidth Latin 등)를 적용해 난독화 우회를 막는다. 모든 패턴은 모듈 로드 시점에 re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL로 사전 컴파일(DANGEROUS_PATTERNS_COMPILED, L431)되어 hot-path의 re.compile fan-out을 제거한다. 승인 키는 사람이 읽을 수 있는 description이지만, _PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES(L442)가 구버전 정규식 파생 키와 양방향 매핑해 기존 command_allowlist 항목 호환성을 유지한다.

2.4 컨테이너 백엔드 스킵

docker/singularity/modal/daytona 환경 타입은 호스트를 건드릴 수 없으므로 승인 레이어를 전부 통과한다(L940, L1171). vercel_sandbox는 check_execute_code_guard에서 추가로 면제된다(L1482).

3. 승인 오케스트레이션과 흐름

3.1 통합 가드 check_all_command_guards

terminal_tool.py가 명령 실행 직전에 호출하는 메인 진입점이 check_all_command_guards(command, env_type, approval_callback)(L1161)다. tirith와 dangerous-command 결과를 모아 단일 통합 승인 요청으로 제시한다 — 게이트웨이 force=True 재실행이 한 체크만 보여주고 다른 체크를 우회하는 것을 막기 위함이다(주석 L1166).

판정 순서(우선순위 = 보안 강도):

1. 컨테이너 백엔드 → 통과                                    (L1171)
2. Hardline 매칭 → 무조건 BLOCK (yolo도 못 뚫음)            (L1178)
3. Sudo stdin guard → 무조건 BLOCK                          (L1188)
4. _YOLO_MODE_FROZEN / 세션 yolo / approvals.mode==off → 통과 (L1197)
5. 비대화·비게이트웨이·비ask 컨텍스트:
     - cron 세션 + cron_mode==deny → BLOCK                  (L1208)
     - 그 외 → 통과 (auto-approve, 경고 로그)               (L1223)
6. [Phase 1] tirith 스캔 + dangerous 탐지 → warnings 수집
7. [Phase 2.5] approvals.mode==smart → 보조 LLM 위험판정     (L1270)
8. [Phase 3] 채널별 승인 프롬프트 (gateway/ask/CLI)

3.2 YOLO 모드 freeze (prompt-injection 방어)

_YOLO_MODE_FROZEN(L29)은 모듈 import 시점에 한 번 os.getenv("HERMES_YOLO_MODE")를 읽어 동결한다. 주석이 이유를 명시한다: 매 호출마다 환경변수를 읽으면 프로세스 안에서 도는 어떤 스킬이든 이 변수를 set해 모든 승인 체크를 즉시 우회할 수 있는 prompt-injection 권한상승 경로가 되기 때문. 세션별 yolo(enable_session_yolo, L601)는 게이트웨이 /yolo용으로 별도 관리되어 process-global이 아니다.

3.3 스마트 승인 (_smart_approve)

approvals.mode: "smart"일 때 _smart_approve(command, description)(L878)가 사용자에게 묻기 전에 보조 LLM(auxiliary_client.call_llm(task="approval"))에게 위험을 평가시킨다. 프롬프트는 "보안 리뷰어" 역할로 APPROVE/DENY/ESCALATE 한 단어를 받는다. 주석은 OpenAI Codex의 Smart Approvals guardian subagent(openai/codex#13860)에서 영감을 받았다고 명시한다.

  • APPROVE → 세션 승인 자동 부여 후 통과 (python -c "print('hello')" 같은 false-positive 흡수)
  • DENY → 즉시 차단
  • ESCALATE(불확실 또는 LLM 호출 실패, L920) → 수동 프롬프트로 fall-through

3.4 세션 상태와 영구 allowlist

승인 상태는 thread-safe하게 관리된다(_lock, L500): _session_approved(세션별 set), _session_yolo(세션별), _permanent_approved(전역). 게이트웨이 세션 키는 contextvars(_approval_session_key, L35)로 per-thread 격리되어 executor 스레드 동시 실행에서 racy하지 않다.

영구 allowlist(command_allowlist)는 사용자가 always를 고른 패턴이다. load_permanent_allowlist(L677)가 모듈 import 시 config.yaml에서 로드하고(L1624), save_permanent_allowlist(L695)가 다시 저장한다. 단, tirith finding은 절대 영구 등록되지 않는다 — 콘텐츠 수준 보안 finding에 broad permanent allowlisting은 부적절하므로 always를 골라도 session으로만 처리한다(L1366, L1442; CLI에서는 allow_permanent=not has_tirith로 [a]lways 옵션 자체를 숨김, L1410).

3.5 게이트웨이 콜백과 블로킹 대기

게이트웨이/ask 컨텍스트에서는 동기 에이전트 스레드가 사용자 응답까지 블록된다. 각 세션은 _gateway_queues(L525)에 _ApprovalEntry(threading.Event, L515) 큐를 갖고, register_gateway_notify(L529)로 등록된 콜백이 sync→async를 브리지한다. _await_gateway_decision(L1060)이 핵심 대기 루프다:

  • pre_approval_request 훅 발화 → notify_cb로 사용자에게 전송 → Event 대기
  • 기본 timeout 300초(gateway_timeout), 1초 슬라이스로 폴링하며 ~10s마다 touch_activity_if_due로 heartbeat를 흘려 게이트웨이 watchdog이 대기 중 에이전트를 죽이지 않게 함(L1138)
  • /approve(resolve_gateway_approval, L554)는 FIFO로 가장 오래된 것을, /approve all은 전부 해소
  • post_approval_response 훅 발화 (choice: once/session/always/deny/timeout)

Consent contract(L1333): timeout(미응답)과 explicit deny 둘 다 hard halt다. BLOCKED 메시지는 에이전트의 흔한 회피 경로를 명시적으로 막는다 — "Do NOT retry, do NOT rephrase, do NOT attempt the same outcome via a different command. Silence is not consent."(이슈 #24912).

3.6 CLI 인터랙티브 프롬프트와 stdin-deadlock 가드

CLI 경로에서는 prompt_dangerous_approval(L710)이 [o]nce / [s]ession / [a]lways / deny 프롬프트를 띄운다. 기본 timeout은 60초(_get_approval_timeout, L857)이며 입력은 데몬 스레드 + thread.join(timeout=...)로 받아 무응답시 deny로 fail-closed한다(L794). 흥미로운 안전장치는 prompt_toolkit 충돌 회피다(L748): 인터랙티브 CLI 세션에서 prompt_toolkit이 터미널을 소유 중인데 이 스레드에 승인 콜백이 등록돼 있지 않으면, input() fallback이 영원히 Enter를 못 보는 invisible 60s deadlock(#15216)이 발생한다 — 이를 감지하면 즉시 deny하고 크게 로깅한다. 승인이 필요한 스레드는 반드시 set_approval_callback()을 먼저 설치해야 한다(delegate_tool/run_agent의 확립된 패턴).

레거시 단일 체크 진입점 check_dangerous_command(L925)도 남아 있다 — tirith 통합 전의 dangerous-only 경로로, 동일한 hardline floor → yolo → 패턴탐지 → 세션승인 순서를 따른다. 비대화·비게이트웨이 컨텍스트에서 위험 명령을 만나면 경고 로그와 함께 auto-approve하는데(L983), 이는 헤드리스 스크립트/테스트 호환성을 위한 의도된 동작이며 HERMES_INTERACTIVE/HERMES_GATEWAY_SESSION을 set해야 승인이 강제된다.

3.7 execute_code 별도 가드

tools/code_execution_tool.py(L1104)가 호출하는 check_execute_code_guard(code, env_type)(L1455)는 임의 로컬 Python을 가드한다 — 스크립트가 subprocess/os.system/ctypes로 직접 프로세스/파일 API를 호출해 terminal()/DANGEROUS_PATTERNS를 우회하기 때문(#30882). 게이트웨이/ask에서 스크립트 전체를 1회용(one-shot) 승인하며, 승인은 절대 persist되지 않는다(매 스크립트가 distinct한 임의 코드이므로, L1616).

3.8 cron 승인

approvals.cron_mode(기본 deny)가 사용자 부재 컨텍스트를 다스린다. cron 세션은 절대 gateway-approval 컨텍스트가 아니다(_is_gateway_approval_context, L102) — 그랬다간 listener 없는 pending approval로 무한 블록되기 때문. deny면 위험 명령을 차단하고 대안을 찾으라 안내한다(L1208).

3.9 승인 흐름 다이어그램

graph TD
    A["terminal() / execute_code()"] --> B["check_all_command_guards()"]
    B --> C{"컨테이너 백엔드?<br/>docker/modal/..."}
    C -->|"예"| PASS["통과 (approved)"]
    C -->|"아니오"| D{"Hardline 매칭?"}
    D -->|"예"| HB["무조건 BLOCK<br/>(yolo도 못 뚫음)"]
    D -->|"아니오"| SG{"sudo -S 추측?"}
    SG -->|"예"| HB
    SG -->|"아니오"| Y{"YOLO / mode=off?"}
    Y -->|"예"| PASS
    Y -->|"아니오"| NI{"비대화·비GW·비ask?"}
    NI -->|"cron+deny"| CB["BLOCK (cron 무인)"]
    NI -->|"그 외"| PASS
    NI -->|"대화/GW/ask"| P1["Phase1: tirith 스캔<br/>+ DANGEROUS 탐지"]
    P1 --> W{"warnings 있음?"}
    W -->|"없음"| PASS
    W -->|"있음"| SM{"mode==smart?"}
    SM -->|"APPROVE"| PASS
    SM -->|"DENY"| SD["BLOCK (smart)"]
    SM -->|"ESCALATE / manual"| ASK["채널별 승인 요청"]
    ASK -->|"게이트웨이"| GQ["_await_gateway_decision<br/>(Event 큐 + heartbeat)"]
    ASK -->|"CLI"| CLI["prompt_dangerous_approval<br/>(input, 60s)"]
    GQ --> R{"응답"}
    CLI --> R
    R -->|"once"| PASS
    R -->|"session"| SS["approve_session"] --> PASS
    R -->|"always (비tirith)"| AP["approve_permanent<br/>+ command_allowlist 저장"] --> PASS
    R -->|"deny / timeout"| DB["BLOCK<br/>(Silence is not consent)"]

4. 미디어 전달 방어 (gateway/platforms/base.py)

게이트웨이는 출력단에서도 방어한다 — 모델이 내놓은 로컬 파일 경로를 네이티브 첨부로 보낼 때 호스트 비밀이 새지 않도록. 핵심은 validate_media_delivery_path(path)(L1002)이며 안전하면 절대경로, 아니면 None을 반환한다.

4.1 세 가지 게이트

단계 동작
Allowlist (무조건 신뢰) MEDIA_DELIVERY_SAFE_ROOTS(L840, Hermes 관리 캐시 + HERMES_MEDIA_ALLOW_DIRS 운영자 지정) 하위면 모드 무관 통과 (L1050)
Denylist (무조건 차단) _MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_PREFIXES(L875) + $HOME 하위 자격증명 디렉토리(L890) 하위면 차단
Strict mode off면 denylist 밖이면 통과, on이면 allowlist/recency만 통과

심볼릭 링크는 검사 전에 resolve(strict=True)로 풀려(L1041) symlink 우회를 막는다.

4.2 denylist (자격증명/시스템 경로)

_MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_PREFIXES = ("/etc","/proc","/sys","/dev","/root",
                                   "/boot","/var/log","/var/lib","/var/run")
_MEDIA_DELIVERY_DENIED_HOME_SUBPATHS = (".ssh",".aws",".gnupg",".kube",
    ".docker",".config",".azure",".gcloud","Library/Keychains")
# 추가로 $HERMES_HOME/.env, auth.json, credentials/ 명시 차단 (L957~)

이 denylist는 recency-window를 통과해도 무조건 적용된다 — MEDIA:/etc/passwd, MEDIA:~/.ssh/id_rsa 같은 명백한 prompt-injection exfil 경로는 항상 거부된다(L1059).

4.3 recency-window + strict mode

_MEDIA_DELIVERY_TRUST_RECENT_DEFAULT_SECONDS = 600   # 10분

기본은 non-strict 모드(L936 주석): inbound와 대칭으로(사용자가 올린 어떤 문서든 받으니) denylist 밖의 어떤 정규 파일이든 전달 허용. strict mode(gateway.strict: True 또는 HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT=1)는 공개 게이트웨이용으로, 파일이 (a) Hermes 캐시, (b) 운영자 allowlist 루트, 또는 (c) recency-window 안에 갓 생성된 파일(matplotlib plot, pandoc PDF 등은 전달 직전 초 단위로 생성되지만 /etc/passwd·id_rsa는 mtime이 일/월 단위 → window 밖)이어야만 통과한다(L1067). strict 목적은 명시적이다: "prompt injection from one user shouldn't be able to exfiltrate the host's secrets to that same user."

gateway/run.py가 config.yaml의 gateway.strict를 읽어 HERMES_MEDIA_DELIVERY_STRICT env로 브리지한다(L940~943).

5. 채널별 권한 / 페어링

5.1 인가 (_is_user_authorized)

게이트웨이는 인바운드 메시지를 _handle_message에서 인가 먼저 확인한다([[00_hermes_overview]] §2.3). 일부 어댑터(WeCom/Weixin/Yuanbao/QQBot)는 dm_policy/group_policy 등 config-driven 접근을 어댑터 내부에서 처리하므로, 게이트웨이의 env 기반 allowlist 체크가 그 뒤에 또 default-deny하지 않도록 "already authorized at intake" 플래그로 스킵한다(base.py L1663~1676).

5.2 미인가 DM 처리 (_get_unauthorized_dm_behavior)

gateway/run.py의 _get_unauthorized_dm_behavior(L6797)가 미인가 DM을 어떻게 처리할지 결정한다. 해결 순서:

  1. 플랫폼별 명시 unauthorized_dm_behavior config → 항상 우선
  2. 전역 명시 config → 차순위
  3. allowlist가 설정돼 있으면 (TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS 등, GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS) → 기본 "ignore" — 운영자가 의도적으로 접근을 제한한 신호이므로 모르는 연락처에 페어링 코드를 뿌리는 건 시끄럽고 정보 누출 위험(#9337)
  4. config-driven dm_policy: pairing → "pair", allowlist/disabled → "ignore"
  5. allowlist도 명시 config도 없으면 → "pair" (open-gateway 기본)

그룹 채널의 미인가 발신자는 조용히 무시한다.

5.3 페어링 (gateway/pairing.py)

미인가 DM 발신자에게 "pair" 정책이면 페어링 코드를 제시하고, 봇 소유자가 CLI에서 승인한다. 보안 속성(PairingManager, docstring L9):

속성 값
코드 알파벳 ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789 (8자, 0/O/1/I 제외)
생성 secrets.choice(L236, 암호학적 난수)
만료 CODE_TTL_SECONDS = 3600 (1시간)
플랫폼당 pending 상한 MAX_PENDING_PER_PLATFORM = 3
rate limit _is_rate_limited(L227) — 페어링 스팸 방어
저장 ~/.hermes/platforms/pairing/{platform}-{pending,approved}.json (_secure_write)

is_approved(L144)가 정규화된 user_id alias 매칭으로 승인 여부를 확인하고, _approve_user(L162)가 승인 목록에 추가한다.

6. 방어 계층 요약

계층 위치 무엇을 막나 fail 동작
채널 인가 / 페어링 gateway/run.py, gateway/pairing.py 미인가 발신자 default-deny / ignore
Hardline 블록 approval.py HARDLINE_PATTERNS 복구불가 재앙 명령 무조건 차단 (yolo 무관)
Sudo stdin guard approval.py _SUDO_STDIN_RE 비밀번호 brute-force 무조건 차단
tirith 콘텐츠 스캔 tirith_security.py homograph/pipe-to-sh/터미널 인젝션 fail-open(기본)
Dangerous 패턴 approval.py DANGEROUS_PATTERNS 구조적 위험 명령 승인 요청 (yolo/smart 우회 가능)
스마트 승인 approval.py _smart_approve false-positive 흡수 escalate→수동
미디어 전달 base.py validate_media_delivery_path 비밀 exfiltration denylist 차단 + (strict)recency

cli-jaw 비교 시사점

  • (a) 사전실행 스캔 + 승인콜백 vs 인터페이스 readonly + 권한 프롬프트. Hermes는 에이전트 코어가 자체 방어한다: tirith_security.check_command_security(외부 Rust 스캐너, exit-code 판정)와 approval.check_all_command_guards(정규식 hardline/dangerous + 게이트웨이 Event 큐 콜백)가 도구 실행 경로에 직접 박혀 있다. cli-jaw는 하니스가 에이전트를 방어한다 — Bash 도구의 readonly 인터페이스, settings.json allow/deny 규칙, 하니스 레벨 permission 프롬프트(update-config 스킬)로 같은 통제를 하니스 레이어에서 수행한다. Hermes의 방어는 게이트웨이/cron/ACP 등 비대화 채널까지 따라가지만, cli-jaw의 방어는 하니스가 붙은 인터랙티브 세션에 묶인다.

  • (b) fail-open vs fail-closed 비대칭. Hermes tirith는 tirith_fail_open: True가 기본이라 스캐너가 죽으면 명령을 통과시킨다(가용성 우선) — 단 HARDLINE_PATTERNS/sudo-stdin guard가 yolo·mode=off조차 못 뚫는 fail-closed 바닥을 깔아 비대칭 방어를 구성한다. [[12_cc_guardian]] Claude Code의 Tengu 체인은 통과 못 하면 ask로 fail-closed이고, cli-jaw 하니스 권한 프롬프트도 규칙 미매칭시 사용자에게 묻는 fail-closed에 가깝다. 즉 Hermes는 "장기실행 게이트웨이 데몬의 가용성"을 위해 의도적으로 fail-open을 택하되 catastrophic floor로 보완한 설계.

  • (c) 콘텐츠 vs 구조 위협의 분리. Hermes는 tirith(콘텐츠 수준: homograph URL, pipe-to-interpreter, 터미널 인젝션)와 자체 정규식(구조 수준: rm -rf /, fork bomb, SQL DROP)을 두 엔진으로 분리하고 check_all_command_guards에서 단일 프롬프트로 합친다. cli-jaw에는 외부 Rust 보안 스캐너에 해당하는 코어 메커니즘이 없고, 위험 명령 판단을 하니스 규칙 + 모델/사용자 판단에 의존한다 — Hermes의 tirith 양분 모델은 cli-jaw가 콘텐츠 인젝션 방어를 추가할 때 참고점.

  • (d) 출력단 exfiltration 방어. Hermes의 validate_media_delivery_path(denylist + recency-window + strict mode)는 "한 사용자의 prompt injection이 호스트 비밀을 같은 사용자에게 빼돌리는 것"을 출력 첨부 단계에서 차단하는 명시적 공개-게이트웨이 방어다. cli-jaw는 다채널 출력 첨부가 ad-hoc하고, 이런 recency-window 기반 자격증명 exfil 방어에 해당하는 코어 게이트가 부재하거나 하니스 권한에 위임된다 — 공개 봇을 운영한다면 Hermes의 gateway.strict + denylist 패턴이 직접 이식 가능한 참고 자료.

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

변경 기록

  • 2026-05-29: Hermes Agent(NousResearch/hermes-agent v0.15.1) 가디언/승인 차원 최초 작성. tirith 사전실행 스캔(check_command_security exit-code 0/1/2 판정 + fail-open + cosign/SHA-256 자동설치), 3계층 위험패턴(HARDLINE/sudo-stdin/DANGEROUS) + NFKC 정규화 + 키 alias, check_all_command_guards 8단계 판정 + YOLO freeze + 스마트승인(보조 LLM) + 게이트웨이 Event 큐 콜백/consent contract + execute_code one-shot 가드 + cron_mode, 미디어 전달 방어(denylist + recency-window 600s + strict mode), 채널 인가/_get_unauthorized_dm_behavior/페어링(secrets 8자/1h TTL/rate limit), 방어계층 요약표, cli-jaw 4항목 비교 포함.
31

Axis 13: Cross-cutting

AXIS

013_etc/13_hermes_etc.md

Overview

Axis 13: Cross-cutting — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 이 문서는 [[00_hermes_overview]]가 다룬 본류 차원(기동·게이트웨이·도구·시스템 프롬프트·메모리·에이전트 루프·턴 생존 실행·보안·ACP·플러그인·관찰성)에 **포함되지 않은 잔여 교차 관심사(cross-cutting concerns)**를 소스 기반으로 정리한다. 분석 대상은 NousResearch/hermes-agent 저장소(`/Users/jun/developer/codex/hermes-agent

  • 모듈 docstring(라인 1~47)에 설계가 명문화돼 있다. 사용자 작업 디렉토리에 .git을 만들지 않고, ~/.hermes/checkpoints/store/에 단일 공유 bare-ish git 저장소를 두어 모든 프로젝트의 blob을 git의 content-addressing으로 dedup한다.
  • ~/.hermes/checkpoints/
  • store/ — 단일 공유 git 저장소 (HEAD, config, objects/)
  • indexes/ — 프로젝트별 git index
  • legacy-/ — pre-v2 프로젝트별 shadow 아카이브
  • shadow 상태는 GIT_DIR + GIT_WORK_TREE + GIT_INDEX_FILE 환경변수 조합으로 격리되어 "사용자 프로젝트 디렉토리에 git 상태가 새지 않는다"(라인 3839). prune_checkpoints가 retention_days 초과 stale ref를 삭제하고 git gc --prune=now로 객체를 회수하며, 크기 캡으로 프로젝트별 오래된 체크포인트를 떨군다(라인 444
  • CC가 프롬프트 텍스트의 command-injection 탐지로 막는 자리를, Hermes는 타입 레벨 검증으로 막는다. _validate_commit_hash()(라인 155~)는 -로 시작하는 값이 git 플래그로 해석되는 것을 차단하기 위해 440 hex(short/full SHA-1/SHA-256)만 통과시키고(라인 147), _validate_file_path()(라인 171)가 경로를 검증한다.
  • 소스 설치는 git rev-parse --short=8로 라이브 git 리비전을 보고하지만(라인 23), Docker 이미지는 .dockerignore가 .git을 제외하므로 빌드 시 $HERMES_GIT_SHA 빌드-arg를 <project_root>/.hermes_build_sha에 기록해 폴백한다(라인 6~11, 33). get_build_sha(short=8)이 단일 진입점이며 IO/디코딩 오류 시

Topics covered

    1. Git / VCS Integration
  • 1.1 Shadow Git 체크포인트 (tools/checkpoint_manager.py)
  • 1.2 Git 인자 인젝션 방어
  • 1.3 빌드 SHA 추적 (hermes_cli/build_info.py)
    1. 모델 라우팅 / 멀티 프로토콜 어댑터 (가장 성숙한 차원)
  • 2.1 어댑터 인벤토리 (agent/)

Source code snippets

README / docs

~/.hermes/checkpoints/
    store/                  — 단일 공유 git 저장소 (HEAD, config, objects/)
        indexes/<hash16>    — 프로젝트별 git index
    legacy-<timestamp>/     — pre-v2 프로젝트별 shadow 아카이브

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant GW as GatewayRunner (백그라운드 스레드)
    participant T as cron.scheduler.tick()
    participant L as .tick.lock (파일 락)
    participant J as jobs.json
    participant A as AIAgent / no-agent 경로

    loop 매 60초
        GW->>T: tick()
        T->>L: 락 획득 시도
        alt 락 보유 중(다른 프로세스)
            L-->>T: skip (중복 방지)
        else 락 획득
            T->>J: due job 조회 (croniter)
            loop due job 직렬 실행
                alt 순수 스크립트 job
                    T->>A: no-agent 경로 (AIAgent 미생성)
                else 에이전트 job
                    T->>A: AIAgent 실행 (인젝션 스캔 통과 시)
                end
                A-->>T: output → cron/output/{job_id}/{ts}.md
            end
            T->>L: 락 해제
        end
    end

README / docs

approval:
  dangerous_header: "⚠️  위험한 명령: {description}"
  allowed_session:  "      ✓ 이 세션에서 허용됨"
gateway:
  draining: "⏳ 재시작 전에 활성 에이전트 {count}명을 정리하는 중..."

Main analysis

Hermes Agent Remaining Cross Interest

This document organizes on a source-by-source basis the remaining cross-cutting concerns** not included in the main dimensions covered by [[00_hermes_overview]] (startup, gateway, tools, system prompts, memory, agent loop, turn-surviving execution, security, ACP, plug-ins, observability). The analysis target is the Python body (~1,984 .py) of the NousResearch/hermes-agent repository (/Users/jun/developer/codex/hermes-agent), and the analysis standard version is 0.15.1 of pyproject.toml. The target of comparison is [[13_cc_etc]](Claude Code), which analyzes the same dimension as a deobfuscation bundle.

Unlike Claude Code analysis, which was tied to webpack bundle decompilation and ended up as “unverifiable/absent” in some areas (testing infrastructure, i18n), Hermes is plain text open source, so the actual implementation can be cited directly. Therefore, this document explicitly contrasts whether the space vacated by CC was filled by Hermes in the same dimension.

1. Git/VCS integration

Hermes' Git integration is fundamentally different from CC's "prompt safety protocol + session start state collection" approach. Hermes has a checkpointing system built into its core that uses an actual git object repository.

1.1 Shadow Git checkpoint (tools/checkpoint_manager.py)

The design is specified in the module docstring (lines 1-47). Instead of creating a .git in the user's working directory, have a single shared bare-ish git repository in ~/.hermes/checkpoints/store/, deduping all project's blobs to git's content-addressing.

~/.hermes/checkpoints/
    store/                  — 단일 공유 git 저장소 (HEAD, config, objects/)
        indexes/<hash16>    — 프로젝트별 git index
    legacy-<timestamp>/     — pre-v2 프로젝트별 shadow 아카이브

The shadow state is isolated by the combination of GIT_DIR + GIT_WORK_TREE + GIT_INDEX_FILE environment variables, so that "git state does not leak to user project directories" (lines 38-39). prune_checkpoints deletes stale refs exceeding retention_days, reclaims objects with git gc --prune=now, and drops old checkpoints per project with a size cap (lines 44-47). Activation is controlled by the checkpoints config flag or the --checkpoints CLI flag (line 11).

1.2 Git argument injection defense

Where CC blocks command-injection detection in the prompt text, Hermes blocks it with type level verification. _validate_commit_hash() (line 155~) only passes 440 hex (short/full SHA-1/SHA-256) to prevent values ​​starting with - from being interpreted as git flags (line 147), and _validate_file_path() (line 171) verifies the path. .git/ itself is excluded from checkpoint tracking (line 102).

1.3 Build SHA tracking (hermes_cli/build_info.py)

The source install reports the live git revision with git rev-parse --short=8 (line 23), but the Docker image falls back by writing the $HERMES_GIT_SHA build-arg to <project_root>/.hermes_build_sha at build time because .dockerignore excludes .git (lines 6-11, 33). get_build_sha(short=8) is the single entry point and returns None on IO/decode errors (lines 20, 36).

Compared to CC: CC's strengths were worktree detection + PR creation prompt + safety protocols, but it did not handle actual VCS objects. Hermes goes the opposite direction — it has a checkpoint/revert infrastructure that uses git directly as the backend repository.

2. Model routing/multiprotocol adapter (most mature dimension)

An overwhelmingly mature area of Hermes' remaining interests. While CC was at the level of refining 4 providers with delete client.batches, Hermes has 6 types of dedicated adapters for each wire protocol + automatic detection + multi-stage fallback + auxiliary model pins for each task in the core.

2.1 Adapter Inventory (agent/)

adapter file wire protocol LOC
agent/anthropic_adapter.py Anthropic Messages 2,267
agent/codex_responses_adapter.py OpenAI Codex Responses 1,260
agent/bedrock_adapter.py AWS Bedrock 1,277
agent/gemini_native_adapter.py Gemini (native) 971
agent/gemini_cloudcode_adapter.py Gemini (CloudCode/CLI) 909
agent/azure_identity_adapter.py Azure Identity 555
agent/auxiliary_client.py Secondary (side-LLM) routing 5,629

api_mode is "chat_completions" | "codex_responses" | It branches into "anthropic_messages", and AIAgent of run_agent.py takes a branch for each mode just before the call (run_agent.py:913, 3072, 3166, 3219).

2.2 Forced routing heuristic for each model

Protocols are enforced not only by pure URL suffixes but also by model name patterns. _model_requires_responses_api() (run_agent.py:1160) strips the vendor prefix because the GPT-5.x model is rejected as unsupported_api_for_model in /v1/chat/completions, then detects the gpt-5 prefix and sends it to the Responses API (lines 1168-1172). _provider_model_requires_responses_api() (lines 1174-) makes a provider-specific exception: Nous serves GPT-5.x with OpenAI-compatible chat completions, so /v1/responses does not 404 — force (lines 1182-1185). Copilot is delegated to separate logic (_should_use_copilot_responses_api) (lines 1186 to 1193).

_max_tokens_param() (lines 1196~) branches to max_completion_tokens for OpenAI new model/Azure OpenAI/Copilot, and to max_tokens for others (lines 1205 to 1207).

2.3 Multi-stage fallback chain (hermes_cli/fallback_config.py)

get_fallback_chain(config) (line 51~) creates a valid chain that merges new and old config keys. The ordering of fallback_providers as the primary source of truth is preserved, and legacy fallback_model entries are added after them if they are not duplicates (based on _entry_identity) (lines 52-72).

The subtleties of the fallback trigger are in _pool_may_recover_from_rate_limit() (run_agent.py:207~239). If a single credential pool (Gemini OAuth, Vertex service account, etc.) hits 429, there is nowhere to turn, so it falls back immediately instead of waiting for the pool cooldown (lines 216-220). Google CloudCode/Gemini CLI is account level throttle, so multi-entry pools share the same quota window → rotation is meaningless, so it falls back immediately (lines 222-224, 235-238, issue #11314·#13636).

2.4 Cloud authentication by adapter (Bedrock / Azure / Gemini)

Each adapter directly absorbs its own cloud's credential chain — deeper than CC's reliance on external Azure VSCode credentials and MSAL.

  • Bedrock (agent/bedrock_adapter.py): Supports AWS credential chain (IAM role, SSO profile, env, instance profile) and cross-region inference profile (docstring lines 9 to 15). boto3 was removed from [all] extras (PR #24220·#24515) and _require_boto3() (lines 61~) lazy import — to avoid having to bake boto3 into the EKS base image (lines 41~43, 53).
  • Azure (agent/azure_identity_adapter.py): Uses the DefaultAzureCredential chain of azure-identity (line 4), and is only activated when model.auth_mode = entra_id and coexists with the AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY user (lines 11-13, 29). The public entry point build_token_provider returns a zero-arg callable created with get_bearer_token_provider and plugged into OpenAI(api_key=token_provider, ...), and the OpenAI SDK is called before each request → token renewal is transparent (lines 13-17). The Foundry inference scope limits its purpose to not leak pins (lines 43-47) and token minting into the logging path by Microsoft documentation values ​​(lines 43-47).
  • Gemini CloudCode (agent/gemini_cloudcode_adapter.py): Sends to cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com/v1internal:generateContent (line 5), processes OAuth with agent/google_oauth.py, and bidirectionally translates OpenAI tool-call/result to Gemini contents (_translate_tool_call_to_gemini line 86, _build_gemini_contents line 133, _normalize_thinking_config line 238).

2.5 Auxiliary model pin (agent/auxiliary_client.py)

The provider/model for each task, such as curator/vision/embedding/title/session_search, can be pinned in the auxiliary section of config.yaml, and the interpretation order is in _resolve_auto. Largest among adapters at 5,629 LOC — the key is to have a separate client path that does not touch the main session prompt cache. Together with the error classifier in §3 and i18n in §11, it is the central axis of the Hermes design where “the core handles multiple backends first-class.”

3. Error handling/recovery pattern

While CC was an AWS-SDK style TokenBucket + header-based backoff, Hermes is a classifier-centric design that classifies errors into semantic categories and maps recovery strategies.

3.1 FailoverReason classification system (agent/error_classifier.py)

The FailoverReason enum (lines 24~) categorizes “Why it failed → How to recover” into 21 types:

Category representative reason Recovery Strategy
Certification auth / auth_permanent Update/Rotate/Suspend
Billing billing (402) / rate_limit (429) Rotate immediately / Rotate after backoff
server overloaded (503/529) / server_error (500/502) Backoff/Retry
transfer timeout Regenerate client + retry
Context context_overflow / payload_too_large (413) / image_too_large Compression/reduction (not failover)
Model/Policy model_not_found (404) / provider_policy_blocked / content_policy_blocked Other model fallback/no retry
request format format_error (400) / invalid_encrypted_content / multimodal_tool_content_unsupported Retry after strip
Provider specific thinking_signature / long_context_tier / oauth_long_context_beta_forbidden / llama_cpp_grammar_pattern Retry after patching by provider

Classification is a multi-step fall-through of classify_api_error() (line 438~) → _classify_by_status (725) → _classify_by_error_code (1040) → _classify_by_message (1095), and _RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS (line 118) · _AUTH_PATTERNS (333) · _TIMEOUT_MESSAGE_PATTERNS (355) etc. Message pattern matching is the last net. What is noteworthy is that, like image_too_large·multimodal_tool_content_unsupported, multimodal/provider compatibility failures are modeled as recoverable categories — a depth that was not present in CC's classification.

3.2 Prevent retry amplification (agent/nous_rate_guard.py)

Nous Portal once 429 causes up to 9 API calls per turn (3 SDK retries CLI, gateway, cron, and auxiliary clients use ~/.hermes/rate_limits/nous.json as atomic_replace and check this status before requesting. A design that targets the realistic problems of a distributed daemon environment (gateway).

3.3 Stream error synthesis (run_agent.py:255)

Some Codex-style Responses backends (xAI subscription/quota failure, incorrect tool-call relay) throw type=error SSE frames instead of HTTP 4xx. _StreamErrorEvent composites it into the .body/.status_code shape and causes the _summarize_api_error/entitlement detector to process it in the same path (lines 255-267).

4. Telemetry / Observability

Here, the philosophies of CC and Hermes are completely opposite. CC had 1P (Anthropic self-collection) + 3P (OTEL) dual telemetry embedded in the core. Hermes has no external transmission telemetry in the core — usage accounting is in the local database and external tracing is in an opt-in plugin.

4.1 Local usage accounting (agent/insights.py)

InsightsEngine (lines 93~) reads the token/cache/cost accounting columns (input/output/cache_read/cache_write/reasoning tokens, estimated/actual cost) from the sessions/messages table to create model/platform decomposition, activity trends, and session metrics (hermes usage). _estimate_cost() (line 41), _has_known_pricing() (line 36), and ASCII _bar_chart() (line 85) complete the cost analysis locally, without any external transfer.

4.2 Opt-in External Observation — Langfuse Plugin

External tracing exists only in plugins/observability/langfuse/(__init__.py, plugin.yaml). There is no opentelemetry/OTEL/posthog import in the core codebase — i.e. the default build is telemetry-free, and users who need to observe it must enable the plugin. Privacy first, open source is asymmetric.

4.3 Log Separation (hermes_logging.py)

Since setup_logging() is a profile, gateway.log is separated from agent.log(INFO+)/errors.log(WARNING+)/gateway. Instead of sending it to an external collector, it is only dropped as a file in ~/.hermes/<profile>/logs/.

Compared to CC: There are remote collection/experimentation platforms like CC's tengu_* event catalog/GrowthBook/Statsig in Hermes core. This is not a defect, but a design choice for self-hosted OSS — the same goes for §8 feature flags.

5. Authentication / Secret Management

5.1 External Secret Source — Bitwarden (hermes_cli/env_loader.py)

If CC's authentication strength is "8 levels of token source priority + 25 types of secret pattern detection," Hermes's differentiation is external secret manager integration. _apply_external_secret_sources(home_path) (lines 250~) reads the bitwarden section of the config and pulls keys from the Bitwarden Secrets Manager with agent.secret_sources.bitwarden.apply_bitwarden_secrets (lines 277~307). get_secret_source(env_var) (line 42) labels the key source as "bitwarden", and format_secret_source_suffix() (line 68) displays the source in the status output. The test tests/test_bitwarden_secrets.py·tests/test_env_loader_secret_sources.py verifies this.

5.2 .env automatic sanitization

_sanitize_loaded_credentials() (line 102)·_sanitize_env_file_if_needed() (line 159) detects illegal characters mixed in credentials (_format_offending_chars, line 87) and automatically cleans .env. _load_dotenv_with_fallback() (line 146) is responsible for loading fallback.

5.3 Provider authentication·OAuth (hermes_cli/auth.py)

ProviderConfig (line 151), get_anthropic_key() (line 483), and _resolve_api_key_provider_secret() (line 563) are responsible for provider-specific key resolution, and has_usable_secret() (line 551) is responsible for rejecting bean/placeholder values. Z.ai/Kimi even does automatic endpoint detection (detect_zai_endpoint, line 624). The authentication store protects multiprocess concurrency with a file lock (_file_lock, line 952; _auth_store_lock, line 1024) and has a global/profile dual store (_global_auth_file_path, line 877). OAuth tracing avoids exposing the original token by leaving only the token fingerprint (_token_fingerprint, line 826). The authentication file is also loaded when acp_adapter starts (acp_adapter/entry.py).

Separately, agent/redact.py is responsible for secret masking, and hermes_cli/secrets_cli.py/secret_prompt.py is responsible for secret CLI/dialogue input.

6. Multimodal (vision / TTS / image/video creation / transcription)

While CC's multimodal only focuses on "attaching images + creating screenshots," Hermes' tools include creation/analysis bidirectional + voice pipeline.

tools file Role direction
tools/vision_tools.py image analysis (vision_analyze) input
tools/image_generation_tool.py create image output
tools/video_generation_tool.py video creation output
tools/tts_tool.py + tools/neutts_synth.py TTS synthesis (including NeuTTS local) output
tools/transcription_tools.py Voice Transcription input

The supported image MIME is determined as magic bytes by _detect_image_mime_type() (line 108~) of tools/vision_tools.py: PNG (line 114), JPEG (116), GIF (118), and WebP (122). URL input is verified with _validate_image_url() (line 77), and _download_image() (line 130) blocks redirect SSRF with _ssrf_redirect_guard (line 150) and retries three times. Extension-based fallback is _determine_mime_type() (line 233, defaults to image/jpeg if unknown). Provider routing is separated into agent/image_gen_provider.py/tts_provider.py/video_gen_provider.py/transcription_provider.py.

7. Event — Cron 60 Second Tick (cron/scheduler.py)

The gateway calls tick() every 60 seconds on a background thread (docstring lines 4-5). Prevent multi-process duplication of ticks with file lock ~/.hermes/cron/.tick.lock (line 7, 170).

Two key optimizations:

  • No-agent tick path: Pure script jobs do not incur AIAgent/SessionDB mechanism costs (lines 1229, 1327). empty stdout = "No reports this tick" (line 1289).
  • Serialization: tick() serially executes jobs that modify process-global runtime state (workdir, etc.) to avoid polluting the environment (lines 186, 1448). The MCP connection short-circuits an already connected server (line 1608).

The job timeout is controlled by HERMES_CRON_TIMEOUT (default 600s) and incorrect values ​​fall back to default (lines 1661-1677). Prevention of fd leaks (MCP connection leaks every tick) is specified (line 1839). If the injection scanner finds suspicious input, it refuses to run the agent for that tick (line 1367).

sequenceDiagram
    participant GW as GatewayRunner (백그라운드 스레드)
    participant T as cron.scheduler.tick()
    participant L as .tick.lock (파일 락)
    participant J as jobs.json
    participant A as AIAgent / no-agent 경로

    loop 매 60초
        GW->>T: tick()
        T->>L: 락 획득 시도
        alt 락 보유 중(다른 프로세스)
            L-->>T: skip (중복 방지)
        else 락 획득
            T->>J: due job 조회 (croniter)
            loop due job 직렬 실행
                alt 순수 스크립트 job
                    T->>A: no-agent 경로 (AIAgent 미생성)
                else 에이전트 job
                    T->>A: AIAgent 실행 (인젝션 스캔 통과 시)
                end
                A-->>T: output → cron/output/{job_id}/{ts}.md
            end
            T->>L: 락 해제
        end
    end

8. Feature flags/gates

While CC was a GrowthBook + Statsig remote flag, Hermes' "flags" are determined locally and statically with config dotpath gates — there is no remote experimentation platform.

The core mechanism is gateway_config_gate in hermes_cli/commands.py. In CommandDef add gateway_config_gate: str | There is a field called None (line 57), which declares that "this config dotpath will only be exposed by the gateway if it is truthy" (e.g. display.tool_progress_command, line 139). _resolve_config_gates() (lines 378~) resolves the dotpath (line 396) to pick out only the truthy gates, and _is_gateway_available() (lines 407~) causes gates to override cli_only (line 411). In other words, whether or not a command is exposed is determined by a static evaluation of the user config — not an A/B rollout, but more of a feature enable/disable switch.

Token sets/plugins/skills are also enabled with the same pattern (config truthy or environment variable gate, e.g. HASS_TOKEN · HERMES_KANBAN_TASK · HERMES_LANGUAGE).

9. Packaging/Distribution

If CC's strength was "zero dependency single bundle + AUTHORIZED publish guard", Hermes is multi-stage Docker + s6-overlay process supervision + extensive optional extras.

9.1 Dockerfile — 3-stage + s6-overlay

Dockerfile (lines 1~) uses 3 sha-pinned stages: uv 0.11.6-python3.13-trixie (line 1), node:22-bookworm-slim (line 9 — Debian trixie bundle nodejs 20.x is 2026-04 EOL, so copy node/npm/corepack from upstream), debian:13.4 (line 10). bookworm-slim was intentionally chosen to link to glibc 2.36 (lines 5-8). Install system dependencies (ripgrep, ffmpeg, git, docker-cli, openssh-client, etc.) as a single layer (lines 31 to 34).

PID 1 is transitioning tini → s6-overlay (lines 16-23): s6-svscan non-blocking reaps zombies to SIGCHLD and supervises main hermes, dashboard, and gateways per profile. multi-arch maps BuildKit TARGETARCH (amd64/arm64) inline to the s6 tarball arch string (x86_64/aarch64) (line 30~). The Playwright browser is placed in /opt/hermes/.playwright for volume overlay survival (lines 13-14). entrypoint.sh, cont-init.d, s6-rc.d, main-wrapper.sh, and hermes-exec-shim.sh are included in docker/.

9.2 PyPI packaging (pyproject.toml)

hermes-agent v0.15.1, setuptools build. The optional-dependencies are very granular (line 69~), so the user installs only the channels/features they need: messaging(telegram/discord/slack)·slack·matrix·feishu·wecom·dingtalk·sms·homeassistant(channel), acp(agent-client-protocol 0.9.0)·mcp·voice·bedrock(boto3)·google·web(fastapi/uvicorn)·termux(Android), and all which bundles them all. All dependencies are precise == pin (see Supply Chain Security, §10) and narrow down the attack surface by extras. The entry point is hermes/hermes-acp in [project.scripts] (lines 214-217). A nix flake (flake.nix) and termux constraints (constraints-termux.txt) are also provided.

10. Test infrastructure (cells where CC was “unverifiable”)

The CC analysis ended with "N/A" because there were no tests in the bundle. Hermes is the complete opposite with 1,326 tests .py (about 67% of the total 1,984 tests).

10.1 Hermetic test invariants (tests/conftest.py)

The conftest docstring (lines 1-25) enforces the invariant:

  1. Credential env blocking — Unset _API_KEY/_TOKEN/_SECRET/_PASSWORD/_CREDENTIALS before each test to block local development key leakage.
  2. HERMES_HOME Isolation — Isolate get_hermes_home() with per-test tempdir (but not HOME — breaking CI subprocess). Using Path.home() / ".hermes" directly is treated as a bug.
  3. Deterministic runtime — TZ=UTC, LANG=C.UTF-8, PYTHONHASHSEED=0.
  4. Block session leaks — Prevent inheritance of HERMES_SESSION_*.

10.2 pytest settings + CI

pyproject.toml (lines 242-253): testpaths=["tests"], per-test 30s hard cap (pytest-timeout, signal method), exclude integration tests with default -m 'not integration'. Composed of directories by category (tests/agent·gateway·hermes_state·acp·providers·e2e·stress·integration·by plugin). The official runner is scripts/run_tests.sh.

17 workflows in .github/workflows/: tests.yml·lint.yml·docker-lint.yml·docker-publish.yml·osv-scanner.yml·supply-chain-audit.yml ·uv-lockfile-check.yml·skills-index-freshness.yml·history-check.yml·upload_to_pypi.yml·nix.yml etc. i18n catalog parity is guaranteed by tests/agent/test_i18n.py.

11. Internationalization / i18n (where CC was "absent")

CC was concluded as “lack of i18n framework, only English”. Hermes has a real i18n system with a 16 language catalog — the starkest contrast.

11.1 Catalog (locales/*.yaml)

16 locales: en (source of truth)·zh·zh-hant·ja·ko·de·es·fr·it·pt·ru·tr·uk·af·ga·hu. Each file is a dotted-key YAML, replacing the {placeholder} token with str.format.

11.2 Intentionally thin scope (agent/i18n.py)

The docstring (lines 1-28) specifies the scope: Translate only the highest-impact static strings, such as acknowledgment prompts, minor gateway slash responses, and restart drain notifications. Agent-generated output, logs, tracebacks, tool output, and slash command descriptions are kept in English (lines 4 to 7, en.yaml header is also specified in the same way). This is a pragmatic decision that weighs “maintenance cost of the entire translation vs. user-perceived impact.”

Language interpretation order (lines 22-27): lang= argument > HERMES_LANGUAGE env > config display.language > en. _normalize_lang() (lines 97~) absorbs aliases (chinese→zh, traditional-chinese→zh-hant, Korean→ko) and local tags (zh-CN→zh) (lines 50~81), and falls back to en for unknown values. Missing keys are in English, or if English is not present, the key path itself is returned (lines 230-238), so a broken catalog never crashes. The catalog is per-language cached (lines 83-84, 121-153), display.language is @lru_cache (line 166-) to reduce hot-pass costs, and runtime-invalidated with reset_language_cache() (line 186).

11.3 Korean presence check (locales/ko.yaml)

approval:
  dangerous_header: "⚠️  위험한 명령: {description}"
  allowed_session:  "      ✓ 이 세션에서 허용됨"
gateway:
  draining: "⏳ 재시작 전에 활성 에이전트 {count}명을 정리하는 중..."

In addition, website/i18n·web/src/i18n exists separately, so the document/dashboard frontend is also multilingual (a separate layer from the agent core).

12. Maturity assessment and CC comparison

area Hermes Maturity Key differences from CC
Git/VCS ★★★★☆ CC=prompt safe+worktree, Hermes=shadow-git objectstore checkpoint
model routing ★★★★★ CC=4 provider delete trim, Hermes=6 adapter+auto detection+multi-stage fallback+aux pin
error handling ★★★★★ CC=TokenBucket backoff, Hermes=21 types of semantic classifier+crosssession rate guard
telemetry ★★★☆☆ CC=1P+3P core built-in, Hermes=local accounting+opt-in Langfuse plugin (telemetry-free default)
Authentication/Secret ★★★★☆ CC = 8-level priority + pattern detection, Hermes = Bitwarden external source + file lock dual storage
multimodal ★★★★☆ CC=attachment+screenshot, Hermes=vision/create/TTS/transcription bi-directional+SSRF guard
Event (cron) ★★★★☆ CC=internal task, Hermes=gateway 60s tick+filelock+no-agent path
Feature flag ★★☆☆☆ CC=GrowthBook/Statsig remote, Hermes=config dotpath static gate (no remote experiments)
Packaging ★★★★★ CC=zero dependency npm bundle, Hermes=3-stage Docker+s6-overlay+subdivision extras
test ★★★★★ CC=Not Confirmable (N/A), Hermes=1,326 Test+Hermetic conftest+17 CI Workflow
i18n ★★★★☆ CC=absence (English single), Hermes=16 locale+intentional thinscope+alias normalization

Key findings

  1. CC's blank spaces are almost completely filled in Hermes — however, it may appear to be open source plain text. Test·i18n indicates that CC is likely to be present in the original repository, but was not seen in the bundle. Since Hermes is OSS, there is a large asymmetry in the possibility of analysis in that it can directly quote reality.

  2. Opposite philosophy in telemetry and feature flags. CC built the remote collection/experimentation platform (tengu_*, GrowthBook) into the core, while Hermes removed both from the core (local accounting + opt-in plugins + config static gates). Privacy-first choices for self-hosted OSS.

  3. Error handling targets the reality of distributed daemons. Cross-session file state in nous_rate_guard, single-pool/account-level throttle fallback branches are details that are only meaningful in a gateway multiprocess environment — concerns that are not present in CC (mainly single CLI sessions).

  4. Model routing depth is overwhelming. Model name pattern forced routing (GPT-5→Responses), exceptions for each provider (Nous/Copilot), and even auxiliary model pins for each task — treat multiple backends as first-class citizens.

cli-jaw comparison implications

  • (a) i18n — cli-jaw reference 1st place. Hermes' agent/i18n.py chose intentionally thin scope, which means "not full translation, but only highest-impact static strings (approval prompt/gateway response) to 16 locales, fallback to English in case of missing, and absolutely no crash with key fallback." cli-jaw is primarily user-facing in Korean, but its harness-level strings are fixed in English — Hermes' dotted-key YAML + _normalize_lang alias normalization + @lru_cache hot-pass pattern is a direct blueprint that will give cli-jaw a great deal of value for money.

  • (b) Telemetry/feature flags — asymmetry is suggestive. Hermes left the core telemetry-free, opting for observations with Langfuse plugins and feature switches with gateway_config_gate (config dotpath static evaluation). cli-jaw also has the same philosophy as it focuses on local settings (settings.json hook, update-config skill) rather than remote collection at the harness level — the difference is that Hermes has a first-class mechanism for “statically gating command exposure with config truthy.” The same dotpath gate model is referenced in cli-jaw's skill enable/disable.

  • (c) Error classifier — Required for cli-jaw's multi-backend. Hermes' FailoverReason 21 types of semantic classification + nous_rate_guard cross-session file status is a reference point for modeling "why did it fail → how to recover" through code when cli-jaw goes beyond a single Claude combination to a multi-provider. In particular, the depth of considering multimodal compatibility failures such as image_too_large·multimodal_tool_content_unsupported as a recovery category is meaningful for the design of cli-jaw's fallback logic.

  • (d) Packaging/Testing — Benchmark of operational maturity. Hermes' 3-stage sha-pin Docker + s6-overlay supervision + extras-level attack-surface reduction, and Hermetic conftest.py (credential env blocking·HERMES_HOME isolation·determinism runtime) + 17 CI workflows (including osv-scanner·supply-chain-audit) for long-running daemons It is the agent's operating baseline. When cli-jaw improves deployment/regression reliability, Hermetic test invariants and supply chain audit workflow are worth borrowing as is.

Appendix A. Environment variables / config key reference

key area Use
HERMES_LANGUAGE i18n force active language (env > config > en)
display.language i18n config-based language (alias/local tag normalization)
HERMES_GIT_SHA Packaging Baked as .hermes_build_sha when building Docker
checkpoints / --checkpoints Git/VCS enable shadow-git checkpoint
HERMES_CRON_TIMEOUT Event cron job timeout (default 600s)
fallback_providers / fallback_model model routing Fallback Chain (Merge New/Old Keys)
auxiliary.* model routing Auxiliary model pin for each task
model.auth_mode = entra_id Certification Activate Azure DefaultAzureCredential path
bitwarden.* Certification Bitwarden Secrets Manager External Source
gateway_config_gate (dotpath) feature gate expose gateway command when config truthy
PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH Packaging Browser Path for Volume Overlay Survival
